title: Staff Engineer, Chemical, Global Facilities
description: Welcome! Are you ready to be at the forefront of ground breaking Engineering within the semiconductor industry?! Micron Technology, Inc., a global leader in memory and storage solutions, is seeking a dedicated and experienced Senior Engineer to join our Chemical team within the Global Facilities Engineering department. In this crucial role, you will play a vital part in providing high-quality Engineering services and support for the planning, design, construction, operation, and maintenance of our subject area specific Facilities systems across our Semiconductor Manufacturing sites worldwide for Chemical systems and Slurry systems. Responsibilities: Technical Support, Leadership, and Expertise Act as the Technical Field Domain Expert, offering consultation and support to all Site Facilities and Manufacturing teams. Collaborate with internal partners such as Technology Development, Procurement, Finance, and Legal to champion multi-functional collaboration. Develop, review, and maintain companies global system, equipment, and component Standards, Specifications, and Best Known Methods (BKMs) to ensure excellence in engineering practices. Implement Standardization and Alignment across Micron's Global Network and Sites for Engineering and Design concepts, Construction Project execution, Reliability Programs deployment, and Operations and Maintenance practices. Find opportunities for improvement in system designs to improve performance, efficiency gains, reduce maintenance, and achieve cost savings. Benchmark both within and outside Micron to capture Engineering Best Practices and Well Known Methods for Facilities buildings and systems. Stay up-to-date with industry trends, technological advances, emerging technology roadmaps, design innovations, and sustainability practices. Design and Construction Project Support: Provide valuable support to the Design teams in the Engineering development of new large-scale Facilities Infrastructure Expansion projects. Ensure that designs align with all applicable standards, codes, and regulations. Build and refine system design concepts for deployment. Ensure effective communication with local Facilities, Design, Construction, Procurement, and Manufacturing partners to deliver project objectives seamlessly. Conduct timely and thorough review of design packages, offering detailed comments and feedback to ensure the success of the projects. Assist in providing cost and schedule estimates and justifications for project approvals. Supervise progress and provide engineering expertise during construction as required per project if vital. Participate and lead capacity databases and system operating trends throughout the network, validating utilization and diversity factor assumptions. Review and guide on scope, schedule, and cost estimates for Facilities infrastructure additions or modifications. Global Initiatives Program Support: Take charge of Global Facilities Programs & Initiatives, including establishing and defining program objectives, roadmaps, and high-level implementation plans. Lead Sustainability Programs in alignment with Micron's Corporate Sustainability goals and initiatives. Job Requirements: Bachelor’s Degree in Mechanical, Chemical, Process, or Industrial Systems Engineering required. Minimum 7 to 10years experience in Semiconductor and/or Facilities related industry. Technical expertise, knowledge, and experience on Facilities and Semiconductor Chemical Systems including Bulk & Normal Chemical Systems, Chemical Blending Systems, Slurry Systems, Valve Manifold Boxes, Hazardous and High Purity Chemicals and Piping, and Chemical Distribution Systems. Ability to prioritize and schedule workload and work on multiple tasks concurrently. Self-motivated worker, good organizational skills and service demeanor. Excellent communication skills and good team working skills, English Required. Familiarity with Semiconductor Manufacturing and Facilities systems. Ability to travel internationally 25-40% on average, but up to 50-66% of the time during Critical Project phases or Site Activities. Provides engineering for semiconductor manufacturing site facilities changes for plant, office, and production equipment layouts, including semiconductor tool installations and R&D applications, working toward economy of operation, maximum use of facilities and equipment, and compliance with laws and regulations. Prepares bid sheets and contracts for construction and facilities acquisition. Completes engineering for requested changes to develop estimates for design costs including equipment, installation, labor, materials, preparation and other related costs for semiconductor manufacturing sites, including R&D applications. Inspects construction and installation progress for conformance to established drawings, specifications, and schedules. Develops criteria and performance specifications required to meet unique operating requirements and building and safety codes.

title: Electrical Engineer
description: Job Responsibilities i. Design and Development Design and develop electrical systems and components used in wafer probers, including sensors, actuators, control systems, and power distribution. Create detailed electrical schematics, wiring diagrams, and layout drawings using CAD software. Conduct feasibility studies, simulations, and analyses to validate design concepts and performance. ii. Prototyping and Testing Build and test prototypes to evaluate the performance and reliability of electrical systems. Conduct experiments and tests to ensure that designs meet performance criteria and industry standards. Analyze test results and make necessary design adjustments to improve functionality and efficiency. iii. Collaboration and Communication Work closely with cross-functional teams, including mechanical, software, and systems engineers, to ensure seamless integration of electrical systems with material handlers and wafer probers. Communicate design concepts, progress, and issues to stakeholders and team members. Collaborate with customers to understand their requirements and provide technical support and solutions. iv. Innovation and Improvement Stay updated with the latest advancements in electrical engineering and semiconductor industry trends. Identify opportunities for innovation and continuous improvement in electrical solutions for material handlers. Implement feedback from field tests and customer use to refine and enhance designs. v. Project Management Manage electrical design projects from concept to completion, ensuring timelines and quality standards are met. Prepare and maintain project documentation, including design reports, test plans, and technical manuals. Coordinate with project managers and other engineers to align with project goals and deadlines. vi. Quality Assurance and Compliance Ensure all electrical designs comply with industry standards, safety regulations, and company policies. Conduct risk assessments and implement safety measures in design and testing processes. Document and address any potential hazards associated with electrical systems. Required Skills and Qualifications 1. Technical Skills Proficiency in CAD software (e.g., AutoCAD Electrical, EPLAN) for designing electrical systems. Strong understanding of electrical principles, control systems, and power distribution. Knowledge of sensors, actuators, and other electrical components used in material handling systems. Familiarity with PLC programming and automation technologies. 2. Analytical and Problem-Solving Skills Strong analytical skills to assess system performance and identify areas for improvement. Ability to troubleshoot and resolve complex electrical design issues. Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail. 3. Education and Experience Bachelor’s or master’s degree in electrical engineering, mechatronics, or a related field. Relevant experience in designing electrical systems for material handling or semiconductor equipment. Experience with wafer probers or semiconductor testing equipment is advantageous. 4. Soft Skills Excellent communication and teamwork skills. Ability to work independently and manage multiple projects simultaneously. Strong organizational skills and a commitment to producing high-quality work. Additional Responsibilities · Supplier and Vendor Management Work with suppliers and vendors to source high-quality electrical components for material handling systems. Evaluate and select suppliers based on quality, cost, and reliability. Manage relationships with suppliers to ensure timely delivery of components and materials. · Training and Support Provide technical support and training to manufacturing and maintenance teams on electrical systems. Develop and maintain training materials and technical documentation for end-users. Assist in the resolution of customer issues related to electrical systems. · Sustainability and Efficiency Promote sustainable practices and environmental responsibility in electrical design activities. Identify and implement measures to improve efficiency and reduce waste in electrical processes. Conduct regular reviews and audits to ensure compliance with sustainability goals. · Customer Focus Develop and maintain strong relationships with key customers. Ensure that electrical solutions meet or exceed customer expectations. Act as a primary point of contact for customer inquiries and issues related to electrical systems.

title: Research Fellow
description: About SUTD SUTD is the world’s first Design AI university. With Design AI, artificial intelligence is treated as a partner and a member of the team – not just a tool. As a result of this unique SUTD treatment, AI and humans brainstorm, spar and prototype together, resulting in solutions that are elevated several-fold. This human-AI team concept has been made possible because of SUTD’s unique cohort-based interdisciplinary pedagogy. As a trailblazer in the field of design and technology education and research, SUTD has been pioneering innovative programmes and initiatives since its formation 16 years ago. As part its new mid-term growth strategy called SUTD Leap, it will work closely with industry to co-create solutions in both education and research – for a better world. About The Job We are in search of a highly motivated and accomplished Research Fellow to join our team and significantly contribute to our ongoing research project “AI-Native Token Communication for Physical AI". The successful candidate will collaborate closely with our research team, working on the development of ground-breaking algorithms and methodologies for Physical AI. This role offers an exciting opportunity to engage in cutting-edge research and have a substantial influence on the field. What You'll Do Undertake research in the field of token communication and Physical AI over wireless networks. Develop distributed and scalable inference frameworks and execute experiments to validate the proposed approaches. Co-supervise graduate students and research assistants. Stay up-to-date with the latest advancements in token communication, Physical AI, and related fields, and integrate new findings into research projects. Prepare research papers for publication in prestigious conferences and journals. Deliver presentations on research findings at conferences and other pertinent events. Who We’re Looking For A Doctoral degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, or a related discipline, with a specialization on wireless communication and distributed machine learning A strong background in communication theory, signal processing, and optimization. Proficiency in programming languages such as Python and MATLAB. A proven track record of publishing research papers in esteemed conferences or journals in wireless communication and AI. Strong communication skills and the capability to work effectively in a collaborative research environment.

title: Lecturer (NUS College)
description: Interested applicants are invited to apply directly at the NUSCareer Portal Your application will be processed only if you applyvia NUSCareer Portal We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Job Description NUS College is the honours college of the National University of Singapore. Our focus is a rigorous and stimulating interdisciplinary education designed to help students cultivate the necessary skills to work effectively across boundaries, We plan to hire a dedicated individual  to fill a Lecturer’s position.  This is a full-time position that includes opportunities for pedagogical research funding and for promotion to an Associate Professor on the NUS Educator Track. The primary responsibility is to offer our mandatory courses, to which you will be expected to bring your own academic and intellectual expertise. Duties & Responsibilities For a start, you will be expected to offer the following, Computational Problem Solving (NPS2001) The CPS is a common curricular course designed to introduce NUS College students to the fundamental concepts, computational tools and techniques necessary to solve complex problems. The focus is on computer science and information technology, but course objectives are more general in nature, with the goal of developing essential skills for approaching and understanding new technology to critically assess potential benefits and risks. By having a comprehensive understanding of the potential of new technologies, students will be able to formulate a problem, outline potential technological solutions and set up a team to implement the solutions that can help achieve project goals. Impact Experience Project (NEP3001) The Impact Experience (IEx) is a eight-unit capstone course that all NUS College students will have to undertake for two years. Students will work in interdisciplinary teams to collaborate with non-profit partners and innovate future solutions for the benefit of communities and society in Southeast Asia, including Singapore. Each team of 6-7 students is supervised by a lecturer who specializes in the area they are working with. Students would choose to work with lecturers clustered into three broad areas: Science and Technology, Arts and Design, Civic and Community. Applicants from any field representing the Computer Sciences are welcome to apply for this position. We are interested in candidates whose work is cross-disciplinary and can be applied to better understand and tackle social, environmental and technological problems in the Singaporean context. The selected candidate's primary responsibility will be to teach the CPS together with other course instructors to ensure that students have a common curricular experience while allowing for individual instructor creativity in their seminars. Applicants should demonstrate their ability to teach such a course by providing evidence of teaching interdisciplinary computing courses in the university. The other responsibility will be to supervise IEx student teams working on projects that involve computing for social good. Successful candidates are expected to offer 2-3 sections of Computational Problem Solving in the academic year, an elective of their own design, supervise 3-6 IEx teams, and take on a small supervision load of independent study modules. Qualifications Candidates must hold a PhD. Remuneration and benefits are highly competitive on the global research university level. Expatriate benefits may include travel assistance from overseas and limited housing benefits. Appointments begin at the level of Lecturer on the Educator Track. Although the NUS Educator Track is not a tenurable line, it offers a pathway to promotion to Associate Professorship on an open contract (and beyond), as well as opportunities for pedagogical research funding, including overseas research and conference travel. The contract is for three years in the first instance and is renewable subject to good performance. Interested applicants should prepare and submit the following documents when they apply: •    A cover letter, clearly specifying which position they are applying for. •    CV: An abridged CV (5-page maximum), including details on education, employment history, past experience of teaching and supervision, research activities (including publications) and achievements, honours and awards, and service accomplishments. •    Qualifications: Include scanned copies of academic degrees. •    Teaching Portfolio: o    A Teaching Statement that commences with a preface (maximum 300 words) that makes the case for  appointment. This should be a highly distilled summary of key contributions to student learning and, if applicable, educational leadership, guided by your teaching philosophy. The rest of the Statement, of not more than 2,000 words, should outline your teaching philosophy and your educational contribution across multiple dimensions and roles. Please include evidence on how you have impact students' learning  experience and outcomes as well as how you have influenced peers and the community. o    Teaching evaluations and peer review reports, if available. Other evidence of teaching excellence and/or other contributions to education, e.g., curriculum development, pedagogical innovation etc., should also be provided. •    A proposal, of not more than 500 words, describing a prospective syllabus for the target course. •    A statement, of not more than 500 words, highlighting how you plan to contribute to pedagogical innovation and/or advance interdisciplinary honours education at NUSC. •    FOUR referees' letters (one of whom must be the applicant's PhD supervisor). Interviews will normally be conducted online. Only shortlisted applicants will be notified. More information about NUSC and our curriculum can be found on our website: https://nuscollege.nus.edu.sg/ Additional information about Academic Appointments at NUS can be found here: https://www.nus.edu.sg/careers/

title: Technician, Production
description: Step into a career with ASM, where cutting edge technology meets collaborative culture. ​ For over 55 years ASM has been ahead of what’s next, at the forefront of innovation and what’s technologically possible. With more than 4,500 ASMers representing 70 nationalities, our people and our advanced semiconductor devices are playing a crucial role in trends such as 5G, cloud computing, AI, and autonomous driving.  But we’re more than just a tech company. We value diversity, inclusion and sustainability as we strive to make a positive impact on the world.  Our development programs help support your growth, shaping your future and pushing the boundaries of innovation to unleash potential. Job's mission As a Production Technician at ASM, you’ll be at the heart of building precision mechanical assemblies for advanced semiconductor equipment. Your expertise ensures every component meets strict quality and performance standards, supporting ASM’s mission to drive innovation in technologies that shape the future. This role is ideal for problem-solvers who thrive in dynamic, high-tech environments. What you will be working on Maintain a clean, safe workspace and follow all safety protocols. Interpret engineering drawings, electrical schematics, and manufacturing manuals to assemble machines accurately. Troubleshoot electrical controls using tools such as multimeters and amp meters. Plan and execute assembly procedures to ensure efficiency and quality. Assemble complex mechanical units requiring precision alignment, calibration, and skilled workmanship. Inspect parts and sub-assemblies for dimensional accuracy and proper finish. Use hand and power tools safely and effectively. Participate in cross-training and skill development across product families. Complete required certifications within specified timelines to support operational excellence. Perform other duties as assigned by management. What we are looking for Minimum Diploma in Engineering or equivalent technical discipline. Minimum 3 years of experience in semiconductor or manufacturing industries. Ability to read and interpret technical drawings, mechanical diagrams, and electrical schematics. Proven experience in mechanical assembly, testing or troubleshooting of machines. Ability to work in a cleanroom environment Willing to work on an 8-hour fixed shift, Monday to Friday. Company transport and shift allowance provided. Normal color vision for safe handling of electrical components and safety compliance. Good English communication skills, both written and verbal. Ability to lift up to 23 kg and perform physical tasks such as climbing ladders and kneeling. Skilled in using hand and power tools and precision measurement devices safely. Ability to work in environments with strong magnets during assembly and testing. What sets you apart Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail. Good interpersonal skills and ability to work in a team-oriented environment. Flexibility to adapt to changing product volumes and tasks. Apply today to be part of what’s next. We make the tech that enables the chips in devices which improve lives around the world. We do this with an eye to the future, pushing the boundaries of what’s possible through cutting-edge innovation, and driving the next wave of technological breakthroughs that shape how we live, work, and connect. To learn more about ASM, find us at asm.com and on LinkedIn , Facebook , Instagram, X and YouTube . ASM is an equal opportunity employer and considers qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, age, nationality, social or ethnic origin, sexual orientation, gender, gender identify or expression, marital status, pregnancy, political affiliation, disability, genetic information, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Process Engineer (Semiconductor Equipment / Skills in Data Analysis & Equipment Design)
description:
Responsibilities Design and implement machine processes to improve machine efficiency, reliability, and product quality. Conduct process corner studies to optimize key parameters and ensure machines operate within defined
specifications. Analyze machine performance data to identify trends, improve accuracy, and resolve process-related issues. Develop and validate process control strategies to maintain consistent equipment performance and meet customer specifications. Lead equipment qualification activities (FAT / SAT). Define and document machine technical requirements, including detailed specifications, SOPs, and risk assessments (PFMEA). Perform root cause analysis on equipment and process issues, implementing corrective and preventive actions using methodologies such as 8D and DMAIC. Collaborate with design engineering teams to ensure manufacturability and maintainability of new equipment. Support new product development by scaling prototypes for production readiness. Train manufacturing and customer service personnel on process standards, troubleshooting, and best practices. Evaluate new customer materials and components for compatibility with existing equipment and processes. Provide technical support for customer escalations, including in-house and on-site troubleshooting. Ensure machine compliance with regulatory requirements such as JDEC/EIA standards throughout product
development and implementation. Apply statistical process control (SPC) and Six Sigma methodologies to reduce variation and improve machine process capability. Drive continuous improvement projects to enhance equipment performance and reduce operational costs. Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in engineering or related discipline. Over 3 years of experience in process or application engineering within semiconductor equipment manufacturing/OEM environment. Strong data analysis skills and using tools such as MS Excel, JMP, or Minitab and methodologies (SPC, Six Sigma, Lean) is required. Strong understanding of semiconductor equipment design, process integration, and manufacturing principles. Excellent problem-solving skills and ability to work cross-functionally with engineering, manufacturing, and
customer teams. Programming experience with MS Excel macros or Python is a plus. Six Sigma Green Belt certification or higher is highly desirable. Willingness to work onsite in customer environments approximately 20% of the time. title: Staff Engineer, Gas, Global Facilities
description: Welcome! Are you ready to be at the forefront of ground breaking Engineering within the semiconductor industry?! Micron Technology, Inc., a global leader in memory and storage solutions, is seeking a dedicated and experienced Senior Engineer to join our Gas team within the Global Facilities Engineering department. In this crucial role, you will play a vital part in providing high-quality Engineering services and support for the planning, design, construction, operation, and maintenance of our subject area specific Facilities systems across our Semiconductor Manufacturing sites worldwide for Bulk Gas and Specialty Gas Systems. As a Senior Engineer, Gas for the Global Facilities Engineering department at Micron Technology, Inc., you will be responsible to provide high quality Engineering services and support for the planning, design, construction, operation, and maintenance of your discipline specific Facilities systems in support of the Semiconductor Manufacturing sites across the Micron Global Manufacturing Network – for Bulk Gas systems and Specialty Gas systems. In this role, you will support the Facilities Teams and Manufacturing sites across the network as the Technical Subject Matter Expert through all phases of design, construction, installation, start-up, operations and maintenance with focus and emphasis on Global Standardization and Alignment, Innovation, Reliability and Performance, and Sustainability. Responsibilities: Technical Support, Leadership, and Expertise Act as the Technical Field Domain Expert, offering consultation and support to all Site Facilities and Manufacturing teams. Collaborate with internal partners such as Technology Development, Procurement, Finance, and Legal to champion multi-functional collaboration. Develop, review, and maintain companies global system, equipment, and component Standards, Specifications, and Best Known Methods (BKMs) to ensure excellence in engineering practices. Implement Standardization and Alignment across Micron's Global Network and Sites for Engineering and Design concepts, Construction Project execution, Reliability Programs deployment, and Operations and Maintenance practices. Find opportunities for improvement in system designs to improve performance, efficiency gains, reduce maintenance, and achieve cost savings. Benchmark both within and outside Micron to capture Engineering Best Practices and Well Known Methods for Facilities buildings and systems. Stay up-to-date with industry trends, technological advances, emerging technology roadmaps, design innovations, and sustainability practices. Design and Construction Project Support: Provide valuable support to the Design teams in the Engineering development of new large-scale Facilities Infrastructure Expansion projects. Ensure that designs align with all applicable standards, codes, and regulations. Build and refine system design concepts for deployment. Ensure effective communication with local Facilities, Design, Construction, Procurement, and Manufacturing partners to deliver project objectives seamlessly. Conduct timely and thorough review of design packages, offering detailed comments and feedback to ensure the success of the projects. Assist in providing cost and schedule estimates and justifications for project approvals. Supervise progress and provide engineering expertise during construction as required per project if vital. Participate and lead capacity databases and system operating trends throughout the network, validating utilization and diversity factor assumptions. Review and guide on scope, schedule, and cost estimates for Facilities infrastructure additions or modifications. Global Initiatives Program Support: Take charge of Global Facilities Programs & Initiatives, including establishing and defining program objectives, roadmaps, and high-level implementation plans. Lead Sustainability Programs in alignment with Micron's Corporate Sustainability goals and initiatives. Job Requirements: Bachelor’s Degree in Mechanical, Chemical, Process, or Industrial Systems Engineering required Minimum 7 to 10years experience in Semiconductor and/or Facilities related industry Technical expertise, knowledge, and experience on Facilities and Semiconductor Gas Systems including Bulk Gas Systems, Specialty Gas Systems, Gas Purification, Gas Cabinets and Valve Manifold Boxes, Hazardous and High Purity Gases and Piping, Compressed Air, and Gas Distribution Systems. Ability to prioritize and schedule workload and work on multiple tasks concurrently Self-motivated worker, good organizational skills and service demeanor Excellent communication skills and good team working skills, English Required Familiarity with Semiconductor Manufacturing and Facilities systems Ability to travel internationally 25-40% on average, but up to 50-66% of the time during Critical Project phases or Site Activities

title: Senior Product & Project Engineer
description: Svanehoj Singapore Pte. Ltd . is seeking a Senior Product & Project Engineer to join our growing engineering team. The successful candidate will work closely with cross-functional teams, suppliers, and customers throughout the full project lifecycle. Main Duties & Responsibilities 1.      Perform technical solutions in medium or large size engineering in pump products and fire water pump packages. 2.      Create engineering drawings and specifications for components, products and mechanical designs working with internal and external customers through the development and approval process. 3.      Maintain quality and accuracy of product master data in ERP system as per latest updates required by sales, purchasing, production and aftermarket. 4.      Provide technical support to customer solution engineering, product improvement within Engineering and to other stakeholders. 5.      Develop, maintain product data books, instruction and operation manuals. 6.      Work with Operations, Marketing, Purchasing, Production, Sales, executive management, and others to develop winning solutions. 7.      Coordinate and consult with other team members to design or detail components. 8.      Proactive technical communication, execution with suppliers, customers, and internal stakeholders during end-to-end project execution. 9.      Identify, mitigate, monitor and report risks in own responsibility area. 10.   Work proactively to promote teamwork at all levels of the company. 11.   Other tasks and special projects as assigned. Pre-requisites Education & Qualification: ·        Bachelor’s degree in mechanical engineering. ·        Experience with mechanical designs involving rotative equipment packages, steel structures, piping and couplings are required. ·        Ability to use AutoCAD, 3D CAD(NX/SolidWorks) required, good understanding of CAD & GD&T standards. ·        Good knowledge on sustaining engineering and ability to use enterprise software. ·        Basic understanding of centrifugal pumps and pumping systems ·        Efficient in using tools like Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook, PowerPoint, etc.) and the internet. Experience: ·        5+ years of experience in product development (preferably in pump products and systems in marine applications such as package engineering, hydraulic power packs and diesel engine driven systems)

title: Principal Electronics Engineer
description: Position Overview: The Principal Electronics Engineer is a senior leadership role within EOS Defence Systems (EOS DS) and is responsible for solving problems using disciplined engineering techniques (e.g., software, mechanical, electronics, system, testing, project etc) to assure and advise on the development and/or delivery of products. The Principal Electronics Engineer possess deep technical and industry knowledge with the ability to influence and guide company direction, ensuring strategic alignment. The Principal Electronics Engineer is also responsible for developing products and designs that support the product development and product goals of EOS DS. The Principal Electronics Engineer will lead and mentor engineers, up to and including Senior Engineers. They may provide technical assistance to multiple teams and provide a high level of support to those teams. In consultation with senior leadership, suggest staffing and workflow priorities and monitor and manage performance, health and wellbeing. The Principal Electronics Engineer will have extensive engineering experience across the product lifecycle in a Defence environment, allowing them to provide leadership across engineering teams and execute technical authority as allocated. Primary Duties: Specific Functions & Tasks Provide necessary guidance and direction to produce electronics designs and analysis that satisfy defined product development requirements in the specified time frames and exercise allocated technical authority. Work with systems engineers to ensure all requirements are identified, captured and analysed. Lead conceptual design and schematic capture of complex electronic circuits. Ensure test and evaluation activities on electronics and system designs are undertaken to verify and sign-off of all design requirements. Initiate and/or participate in planning on electronics designs. Develop, review and champion the electronics engineering process within EOS DS. Ensure the preparation and review of all documentation to support designs. Perform conceptual, preliminary and detailed design of complex electronics assemblies. Simulate analogue and digital circuits. Document designs including calculations, design options and decisions. Prepare for and contribute to design reviews. Perform test, evaluation, characterisation, verification & validation of electronic subsystems in collaboration with test engineers. Prototype electronic circuits, wiring harnesses and other systems. Work closely with other engineering disciplines to ensure cohesive and complete electronic designs are delivered on time and on budget and meet project requirements. Apply DFx principles (manufacturability, testability, excellence). Review work and provide guidance and mentorship to Senior Electronics Engineers. Engage key stakeholders and subject matter experts across technical engineering and specialty areas to understand the concept of operations and user needs to elicit and decompose electrical requirements. Adhere to and ensure understanding and compliance with company procedures to achieve a safe and secure work environment. Other duties as required. Strategy and Architecture Contribute to and influence strategy for emerging, new, and current technologies, products, services within Defence Systems and have authority over and accountable for the innovation and delivery practices required to assure the implementation of this strategy. Assess and ensure a high level of leadership and deep industry, defence system knowledge and systems engineering best practice to maintain competitive advantage, exploit EOS DS technologies and minimise exposure and risk. Communicate, advocate and influence persuasively through effective negotiation and communication while fostering an environment of innovation, learning and continuous improvement within and outside the organisation. Development and implementation of Engineering Self-initiated and managed under broad direction and governance to be responsible for demonstrably leading self and others to produce designs and products that satisfy defined requirements in the specified time frames and meet group objectives. Undertake cohesive best practice designs and analysis and develop efficient work practices to support product development activities and business outcomes. Lead and work collaboratively with engineers across functional teams to solve complex problems as a team and ensure all requirements are identified, captured, and analysed such that the design/product can be developed that satisfies the safety, quality, policy, legislative and design authority requirements. Undertake clear and pragmatic tests, evaluation, and verification activities on integrated system designs and prototypes to verify and provide recommendations for sign-off of all design/product requirements to the Chief Engineer and Design Authorities based on relevant legislative requirements, standards, and contracts. Prepare and review all documentation required to support designs and provide tailored advice and recommendations to teams and decision makers. Relationship and engagement Actively listen, share knowledge and experience, provide guidance and mentorship, and work collaboratively to mobilise teams across engineering and other relevant internal and external customers to deliver engineering and business outcomes. Work with Production Engineers and Technicians to ensure the best design solutions are delivered to solve production problems and obtain business outcomes under the broad direction of the Chief Engineer. Support the leadership alongside with the EOS Executive, Project Managers, and Project Engineers to ensure design solutions are delivered on time and on budget and meet other project management requirements. Communicate insights and provide thorough engineering judgement for progress and the advancement of engineering solutions. Apply interdisciplinary, collaborative approach to requirements including analysis of functional and non-functional requirements and functional performance specifications to develop effective solutions. Delivery and operation Guide the design and execution of user acceptance testing and other verification activities to provide recommendations to the Chief Engineer and other Design Authorities. Support production and review manufacturing and supplier requirements to meet specified timeframes, engineering standards, and business outcomes. People and skills Work in cross functional multidisciplinary teams to enable self and others to solve engineering problems and implement solutions. Key Attributes: Ethical Leader. Influencer. Deep Technical knowledge. Problem solver. Engineering judgement. Details oriented. Works Collaboratively. Insightful. Communicates well. Inventive. Solution orientated. Team builder Uses experience for sound judgement

title: SENIOR MECHANICAL ENGINEER (AUTOMATION)
description: Job Description & Requirements We are seeking a talented and detail-oriented Senior Mechanical Design Engineer to join our automation engineering team. In this role, you will be responsible for designing, developing, and refining mechanical systems for automated machinery and robotic solutions used in industry, Manufacturing, packaging, electronics, etc. You will work closely with electrical engineers, software developers, and project managers to deliver high-performance automation systems that meet rigorous standards for safety, efficiency, and reliability. Key Responsibilities: Design and develop mechanical components and systems for automated machinery and robotic platforms. Create detailed 3D CAD models, drawings, and specifications using software such as AutoCAD, or Inventor. Select appropriate materials, actuators, and motion components based on design requirements. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure integration of mechanical designs with electrical and control systems. Support prototype development and lead mechanical assembly and system testing. Troubleshoot mechanical issues in both the design and post-deployment phases. Document design processes, changes, and test results according to company and industry standards. Qualifications: Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering or related field. 5+ years of experience in mechanical design, preferably in automation, robotics, or machinery. Proficiency in 3D CAD software (e.g., AutoCAD, Inventor). Familiarity with pneumatic and servo systems, bearings, linear guides, and other motion components. Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail. Excellent communication and teamwork skills.

The Preferred - You Might Also Have: Analytical mindset towards programming & developing automation solutions Certifications in core subjects, industry internship, innovative projects Experimental mindset, creativity and deliver innovative solutions What We Offer: Our benefits package includes … 16 weeks classroom training in Singapore in the first year of employment Comprehensive mindfulness programs with a premium membership to Calm Volunteer Paid Time off available after 6 months of employment for eligible employees. Company volunteer and donation matching program – Your volunteer hours or personal cash donations to an eligible charity can be matched with a charitable donation. Employee Assistance Program Personalized wellbeing programs through our OnTrack program On-demand digital course library for professional development ... and other local benefits! You may be just the right person for this or other roles. #LI-Hybrid #LI-JT1

title: Manager / Senior Manager (Semiconductor Process & QC)
description: Job Summary: Leading a team in responsible for the strategic design, implementation, and continuous improvement of all quality and process control systems within Fab Operations. This role is critical for ensuring world-class product yield, reducing defectivity, and maintaining strict compliance with customer specifications, internal quality standards, and relevant industry certifications. Responsibilities: Develop and drive a strategic roadmap for yield improvement and defect reduction across critical process modules. Lead the design and maintenance of advanced, real-time Statistical Process Control (SPC) , Fault Detection and Classification (FDC) , and Advanced Process Control (APC) systems to stabilize and optimize all fabrication steps. Cross-contamination management to achieve balance between research and development flexibilities in integration runsheet flow path vs process tools health and process repeatability. Small volume prototype protocol and product and process control plan alignment and set-up with requestor, process margin runs till demonstration of product SPC repeatability and line defect and electrical yield. Serve as the technical expert and primary quality interface for key customers, leading customer audits, addressing quality-related concerns, and driving customer-specific quality improvement plans. Problem Solving: Direct and oversee high-level, cross-functional root-cause analysis (RCA) and corrective and preventive action (CAPA) teams for major process excursions, non-conformances, and field returns, utilizing methodologies like 8D or Six Sigma Metrology & Inspection: Oversee the selection, qualification, and maintenance of all in-line and off-line metrology and defect inspection tools, ensuring measurement system accuracy (Gage R&R) and high data quality. Process Window Definition: Collaborate with Process Integration and R&D teams to define, qualify, and stabilize robust manufacturing process windows for all new product introductions (NPI), ensuring Design for Manufacturability (DFM) principles are met. Data Analytics: Leverage large-scale manufacturing data (Big Data) to identify subtle process drifts, predict failures, and proactively implement controls. Lead, mentor, and manage a team of Quality Engineers, Process Control Engineers, and Technicians, setting performance goals, developing career paths, and fostering a culture of ownership and data-driven decision-making. Collaborate closely with all stakeholders to balance the trade-off between quality stability, process efficiency, and tool uptime. Requirements: Degree in Engineering, Materials Science, Chemical Engineering, or a related technical field. Preferable 10+ years of experience in the semiconductor industry, with a minimum of 5 years in a leadership/management role focusing on Process Control, Quality Assurance, or Yield Engineering within a Fab environment. Deep technical knowledge of semiconductor fabrication processes in different modules. Expert-level knowledge of Statistical Process Control (SPC) , Design of Experiments (DOE) , Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) , and Lean/Six Sigma methodologies (Black Belt certification highly preferred). Strong leadership, presentation, and interpersonal skills, with the proven ability to influence and align technical and executive stakeholders. title: MFG Technology - Mechanical Engineer
description: MFG Technology-Mechanical Engineer role requires a strong background in mechanical engineering, especially with alloys and composites used in oil and gas products, capable of withstanding high temperatures, high pressures, and corrosive environments. This role emphasizes designing manufacturing processes, testing, and improving mechanical components and assemblies to meet the highest quality and performance standards. The mechanical engineer should also have broad knowledge of sourcing, machining, 3D printing, and composite manufacturing to provide solutions for technical challenges. Role and Responsibilities: Manufacturing and Testing: Oversee the manufacturing process of mechanical components, ensuring adherence to quality standards and specifications. Develop and implement inspection and testing procedures for mechanical structures and components. Conduct rigorous testing of mechanical devices to ensure reliability and performance under operating conditions. Design and Development: Assist in designing and developing new mechanical structures and components. Use CAD software to produce detailed designs and technical drawings. Work with the design team to optimize mechanical designs for manufacturability and performance. Quality Assurance: Ensure all mechanical components meet company quality standards and industry regulations. Conduct quality audits and inspections.· Ensure all mechanical components meet company quality standards and industry regulations. Implement continuous improvement initiatives to enhance product quality and manufacturing efficiency. Documentation   and Reporting: Maintain detailed documentation of testing procedures,   troubleshooting processes, and maintenance activities. Prepare and present technical reports on the performance and reliability of mechanical components. Ensure all documentation complies with QMS requirements. Collaboration   and Communication: Work closely with cross-functional teams, including design, production, and quality, to ensure seamless integration of manufacturing processes. Provide technical support and training to production staff on the assembly, testing, and maintenance of the products. Liaise with suppliers and external partners to ensure the quality and availability of mechanical components. Skills and Experiences: Strong knowledge of mechanical engineering principles, particularly in materials. Extensive experience in the manufacturing and testing of mechanical components and systems. Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills. Strong communication and interpersonal skills. Proficiency in CAD software (SolidWorks and PDM) and other engineering tools. General: Committed to a high standard of safety and willing to comply with all safety laws and all the company’s safety policies and rules. Must be willing to report safety violations and potential safety violations to appropriate supervisory or management personnel. Perform other duties as assigned. Qualifications and Education Requirements: Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering or a related field. Minimum of 5 years of experience in mechanical engineering. Knowledge of industry standards and regulations relevant to mechanical components in the oil and gas sector. Key Competencies: Safety awareness Technical Expertise Curiosity about technology Problem Solving Quality Assurance and Control Team Collaboration Communication Attention to Detail Accountability

title: Manufacturing Engineer
description: The Manufacturing Engineer supports the Engineering Department in the planning, design, implementation, validation, and continuous improvement of manufacturing processes for medical devices. This role ensures that production systems comply with applicable regulatory and quality management system (QMS) requirements, while achieving targets for performance, reliability, safety, cost, and delivery. The position focuses on analyzing manufacturing workflows, optimizing processes, and supporting compliant production operations in accordance with ISO 13485, internal procedures, and applicable regulatory standards. Key Responsibilities Manufacturing & Process Support •	Support daily manufacturing operations to ensure compliant, efficient, and consistent production of medical devices. •	Develop, document, and maintain assembly processes, work instructions, and standard operating procedures (SOPs). •	Perform and support high-level assembly (HLA) activities by interpreting engineering drawings, specifications, and procedures. •	Train production operators on approved processes, equipment usage, and GMP requirements. Validation & Regulatory Compliance •	Support medical device process and equipment validation activities, including Installation Qualification (IQ), Operational Qualification (OQ), and Performance Qualification (PQ). •	Ensure manufacturing processes comply with ISO 13485, Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP), and internal QMS requirements. •	Participate in change control activities to ensure regulated changes are reviewed, approved, and documented prior to implementation. Quality & Continuous Improvement •	Support New Product Introduction (NPI) builds and process transfers in accordance with regulatory and quality requirements. •	Investigate product and process quality issues, perform root cause analysis, and implement Root Cause and Corrective Action (RCCA/CAPA). •	Assist in product evaluation, process characterization, and data collection for quality and regulatory purposes. •	Drive continuous improvement and cost reduction initiatives while maintaining regulatory compliance and product quality. Equipment & Production Line Support •	Support preventive maintenance (PM) activities and maintain records for engineering tools, fixtures, and production equipment. •	Assist with production line reconfiguration and machine/workstation moves, including equipment installation, setup, and qualification. •	Ensure equipment and tooling are suitable for validated and controlled manufacturing environments. Safety & Compliance •	Adhere to all safety, health, environmental, and company security policies. •	Ensure compliance with documentation, data integrity, and record retention requirements. •	Maintain effective communication with cross-functional teams including Quality, Manufacturing, and Engineering. Required Qualifications & Experience •	Diploma, Advanced/Higher/Graduate Diploma, or equivalent in Mechanical Engineering, Mechatronic Engineering, or a related discipline. •	Minimum 2 years of experience in manufacturing engineering, preferably within a medical device or regulated manufacturing environment. Preferred Knowledge & Skills •	Knowledge of medical device manufacturing regulations and standards, including ISO 13485. •	Hands-on experience supporting IQ, OQ, PQ, and validation documentation. •	Familiarity with GMP, risk management, and change control processes. •	Strong analytical, problem-solving, and documentation skills. •	Effective written and verbal communication skills.

title: Satellite Engineer
description: Job Overview We are seeking an experienced satellite Engineer with at least three years of professional hands-on experience in satellite design, development, and deployment. The successful candidate will leverage their technical expertise to conduct training programs in satellite engineering for undergraduates, young professionals and youths. This role involves both technical development and educational outreach, ensuring participants gain practical skills in satellite design, assembly, and operation. Key Responsibilities Training Program Development and Delivery •	Design and deliver comprehensive training programs on satellite building, covering topics such as: o	Systems engineering o	Subsystems (power, communication, attitude control, etc.) o	Payload integration o	Satellite testing and deployment protocols •	Develop instructional materials, including manuals, presentations, and hands-on projects, to suit various audience levels (youth, professionals, and educators). •	Conduct hands-on workshops and labs, guiding participants through real-world satellite assembly and testing activities. •	Collaborate with academic institutions, industry partners, and space agencies to expand training opportunities and resources. •	Other Topics outside of Satellite Engineering that would be good to cover: o	Space mission design and planning o	Principles of Space Engineering and Applications o	Project Management in Space Evaluation and Continuous Improvement •	Evaluate training program outcomes through feedback, assessments, and participant performance. •	Continuously update training content to reflect advances in satellite technology and industry trends. •	Provide mentorship to participants during and after training programs to support their career or project goals. Qualifications and Skills Required •	Bachelor’s degree in Aerospace Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or a related field. •	Minimum of 3 years of hands-on experience in satellite development, including design, integration, and testing. •	Strong understanding of satellite subsystems, including power systems, communications, payload integration, and attitude determination and control. •	Proficiency with satellite design tools (e.g., STK, MATLAB, SolidWorks, or similar). •	Experience with satellite testing equipment and procedures, including vibration testing, thermal testing, and deployment mechanisms. •	Excellent communication and teaching skills, with the ability to simplify complex concepts for diverse audiences. •	Experienced in conducting training programmes for youths, undergraduates and professionals •	Experiences: Satellite Engineering •	Apply expertise in designing, building, and testing satellites, including CubeSats and other small satellite platforms. •	Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure training programs align with the latest developments in satellite technology and industry standards. •	Maintain and operate testing facilities, including cleanrooms, thermal vacuum chambers, and other satellite testing equipment. Preferred •	Master’s degree or certification in satellite technology or systems engineering. •	Experience in delivering STEM or technical training programs. •	Familiarity with satellite mission operations and ground station integration. •	Proficiency in programming languages such as Python, C++, or MATLAB for satellite systems. •	Experience working with space agencies or commercial satellite companies. Key Attributes •	Passionate about inspiring and educating others in satellite technology. •	Innovative and adaptable in designing hands-on learning experiences. •	Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail in both technical and instructional tasks. •	Team-oriented, with the ability to collaborate across departments and with external stakeholders. ________________________________________ Benefits •	Opportunity to shape the next generation of satellite engineers and innovators. •	Competitive salary and benefits package. •	Professional development opportunities in both technical and educational fields.

title: Mechanical Design Engineer
description: Job Purpose We are seeking a highly skilled and self-motivated Mechanical Design Engineer capable of independently handling mechanical design tasks. The candidate will be responsible for designing, analyzing, and optimizing mechanical systems and components for various projects, ensuring compliance with industry standards, safety regulations, and client requirements. The engineer will also participate project tender and site to understand customer enquiry and expectation, develop concept design and support procurement team, communicate with customer, vendor and supplier to improve proposal process and quality. Key Responsibilities 1. HVAC System Design : Establish cleanroom design according to client desire and production need, prepare cleanroom architectural layout dwg by using AutoCAD, Revit, or other design software. Develop mechanical HVAC system design following cleanroom design, prepare HVAC P&ID. Calculate mechanical load requirements and size components such as HVAC heatload calculation, fan ESP sizing, pump head calculation, duct/pipe sizing, HVAC equipment model selection. Establish HVAC control philosophy write-up, cause and effect analysis, develop alarm list and interlock mechanism. 2. Mechanical System Design : Establish process utility design following customer’s tools matrix summary, such as CDA, Vacuum, PCW, Steam, Exhaust, Drain, Specialty Gas, RODI, WWTP and etc Develop process utility P&ID and piping/ducting layout. Develop plant room space management study and services routing from plant room to production floor. Calculate mechanical load requirements and size components such utility pump/fan sizing, pipe/duct sizing, utility flow calculation. Establish process utility system control philosophy write-up, cause and effect analysis, develop alarm list and interlock mechanism. 3. Project Tendering : Review tender requirements, participate site visit, communicate with client to ensure the proposals meet client and project specifications. Develop concept dwg and equipment schedule to enhance vendor and supplier knowledge on tender specification and specific challenges. Participate tender costing exercise, support procurement team on proposal preparation. Support procurement team to evaluate vendor/supplier proposal qualification, materials/equipment selection, ensure proposed solution sound practicable and reasonable, meeting quality and regulatory compliance, adhere tender scope of work, delivery schedule, deviations and partial compliance element. Collaborate with vendors to improve competitive pricing for project tenders. 4. Project Management : Coordinate with clients, contractors, and project stakeholders to clarify requirements and resolve design-related issues. Conduct system design presentation to explain proposed design, equipment selection, comparison of different design approach, convince client’s doubt and persuade business decision. 5. Documentation : Prepare technical documents, includes MEP dwg, equipment schedule, RFQ/RFP, sub-contract work package specifications. Maintain accurate and up-to-date project files and records. 6. Collaboration : Work closely with the electrical, control, civil, and architectural teams to ensure seamless integration of HVAC and mechanical systems into overall project designs and execution. Review and evaluate supplier/vendor proposal, assist QS and project team on equipment selection and seek supplier/vendor clarification on design specification compliance and performance assurance. Qualifications and Skills 1. Educational Background : Bachelor’s degree in mechanical, manufacturing, process engineering, or Industrial related engineering field. 2. Experience : Minimum of [4] years of experience in mechanical design engineering, with a proven track record of handling projects independently. 3. Technical Skills : Proficient in mechanical design software such as AutoCAD, Revit or similar tools and Microsoft Office. Decent knowledge of hvac and mechanical codes and standards (e.g. ASHRAE, ISO-14644, SMAGNA) Computer skills - ACAD, MS Project, BIM Experience with project bidding, develop BOQ, material sourcing, compile project quotations. 4. Soft Skills : Strong problem-solving and analytical skills. Self-motivated and capable of working independently with minimal supervision. Effective communication: capable of drafting clear transmittals, datasheets and technical queries; comfortable liaising with vendors and site teams to clarify requirements. 5. Additional Skills (Option) : Electrical LV wiring design experience, such as cable sizing and develop SLD for LCP of mechanical system. Understand general control and instrumentation logic diagram for mechanical system. Understand BIM and able to perform simple BIM modelling. Mobility as when needed.

Attend and participate in devotions, teachers’ meetings, evening programs (if involving his or her students or as requested by the administration), and other meetings as assigned. Carry out supervisory duty as assigned (before school, break, lunch, after school). Maintain a classroom in a clean and educationally stimulating environment. Maintain regular and accurate attendance and grade reports that provide comprehensive information for each student’s learning progress. Keep students, parents, and the administration informed of students’ progress or deficiencies, and provide sufficient and timely notice to parents when students fail to meet learning standards. Submit all appropriate reports to the administration, guidance office, and business office as required by school regulations or as requested. Work with the administration in implementing all policies, procedures, and directives governing the operation of the school. Complete professional development programs as required by the administration and/or ICS Board. Maintain teaching credentials as required through sponsoring agencies (state/provincial education department, ACSI, etc.). Acquire and maintain teaching credentials as required by the laws of Singapore, NICS, and/or ICS. Demonstrate the character qualities of enthusiasm, courtesy, flexibility, integrity, kindness, self-control, perseverance, and punctuality. Exemplify strong interpersonal and communication skills. Meet everyday stress with emotional stability, objectivity, and optimism. Develop and maintain rapport with students, parents, and staff by treating others with friendliness, open-mindedness, dignity, and consideration. Respectfully submit to and demonstrate loyalty to constituted authority. Maintain a personal appearance that models cleanliness, modesty, good taste, and is in agreement with school policy. With more than 4,500 ASMers representing 70 nationalities, our people and our advanced semiconductor devices are playing a crucial role in trends such as 5G, cloud computing, AI, and autonomous driving.  But we’re more than just a tech company. We value diversity, inclusion and sustainability as we strive to make a positive impact on the world.  Our development programs help support your growth, shaping your future and pushing the boundaries of innovation to unleash potential. Job's mission As a key member of our Global Supplier Development team, this role supports the organization's objective to strengthen supplier capability, elevate design and manufacturing performance, and ensure long-term supply resilience across our global network. The mission of the team is to proactively build supplier maturity, drive continuous improvement, and align supplier capability growth with mid- and long-term category strategies to support product quality, operational excellence, and New Product Introduction (NPI) readiness. What you will be working on Assess supplier quality and technology gaps, and execute plans to address them. Review and influence Design Engineering early in design phase to achieve target costs and DFx. Identify areas for cost down during design phase plus manufacturing methods and eliminate waste. Evaluate supplier processes and risks; collaborate to improve capabilities through lean supplier development methods (i.e. six sigma) and implement mitigation plans. Apply engineering and material science expertise with suppliers to boost capacity, product quality, yield and reduce costs. Partner with Category leaders to drive long-term category strategies to support aggressive production scaling. Align development efforts with long-term category strategies and develop partners within the suppliers. Conduct on-site supplier assessments (capabilities, MSA, PCS/SPCE, etc) and identify gaps during selection. Implement improvement plans with suppliers to ensure readiness and support NPI. Coordinate globally with Business/Key Product Unit Engineering teams to meet end-use specifications. Advance manufacturing capabilities, DFMEA / FMEA, and implement Process Control Systems, PFMEA, at key sites. Support statistical PCS / SPC (lean six sigma) data analysis for internal and external stakeholders. Perform root cause failure analysis and report findings to relevant teams. Lead supplier engagement, support surveillance audits, and qualification activities globally. Independently manage supplier improvement projects, including onboarding training and qualification. Oversee multiple projects to resolve issues and enhance product performance, reliability, and efficiency. Lead / mentor group of Supplier Development team on best practices and develop skillsets to support group growth and expansion What we are looking for MS, or PhD in Engineering, Science, or related technical field (Engineering, Physics, Material Sciences) relevant to semiconductor equipment, or proven expertise. At least 10 years of experience or significant expertise in engineering, designing, and manufacturing of complex mechanical systems. Experience in the semiconductor capital equipment industry with technical background in semiconductor manufacturing technologies required. Strong English communication skills for complex technical topics. Effective collaboration across regions, cultures, and disciplines. Proven Lead Engineer in matrix environments and ability to work independently while managing a team. Proficient in project and time management skills required Drive business process efficiencies and improvements with key internal stakeholders Candidate should have knowledge of the following tools, but will be trained to more advanced levels in: Applied Operational and Engineering Statistical Methods Process Control Systems (PCS) and Statistical Process Control (SPC) FMEA include Design FMEA (DFMEA) and Process FMEA (PFMEA) Structured Problem Solving (8D) and Effective Problem Solving (EPS) Operational Excellence tools including Failure Analysis, Lean Manufacturing, Value Stream Mapping Project Management, Team Management, Escalation Management and Communication Techniques across a broad range of business escalation types, (Customer, Supplier, Technical, Manufacturing, etc.) Should costing estimate modeling (aPriori) Apply today to be part of what’s next. We make the tech that enables the chips in devices which improve lives around the world. We do this with an eye to the future, pushing the boundaries of what’s possible through cutting-edge innovation, and driving the next wave of technological breakthroughs that shape how we live, work, and connect. To learn more about ASM, find us at asm.com and on LinkedIn , Facebook , Instagram, X and YouTube . ASM is an equal opportunity employer and considers qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, age, nationality, social or ethnic origin, sexual orientation, gender, gender identify or expression, marital status, pregnancy, political affiliation, disability, genetic information, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Principal/Staff Engineer, Process Integration, NTI NAND
description: As a Principal/Staff Engineer, Process Integration, NTI NAND at Micron, you will have the outstanding opportunity to work on groundbreaking 3D NAND technology. This technology will develop the future of data storage solutions. This role suits those who are ambitious, enjoy solving complex problems, and want to lead innovative projects that push the boundaries of what is possible. Responsibilities Module Ownership: Lead one or more process modules related to the development and operation of 3D NAND semiconductor coordinated circuits. Specification Definition: Define structural specifications and process requirements for assigned modules. Strategic Direction: Set a clear direction and vision for the module and establish a continual improvement plan. Proactive Issue Resolution: Identify potential structure, electrical, and reliability issues early and address them with process control. Solve problems using a model-based approach. Performance Optimization: Focus on structural development, electrical performance, parametric verification, and process control. Optimize manufacturability assessments. Material Innovation: Analyze materials physically and electrically. Collaborate with process groups to select new materials for development and transition them from research to production. Build Experiments: Build and complete device and process experiments. Extract, analyze, report, and draw conclusions on experiments and clarify the statistical significance of the data. Team Leadership: Assemble and coordinate a development team, including process engineers, to drive module development. Decision-Making & Goal Setting: Make key decisions and provide clear direction, goals, and metrics for team success. Innovation & IP Generation: Innovate to address complex problems and consider new options for improving and developing new technologies. Regularly submit patent disclosures. Cross-Functional Coordination: Coordinate activities across cross-functional teams to enable rapid and low-error project progression. Technology Transfer: Support the transfer of new technology from R&D to high-volume manufacturing. Communication & Influence: Present key information and projects, drive decisions, and foster group discussions. Continuous Development: Pursue continual growth in technical and management skills through on-job training and education. Requirements Passionate about semiconductor technology and innovation. Adept at data analysis, problem-solving, and process integration optimization. Excellent communication skills and capacity to collaborate within cross-functional teams. Experience Proficiency in scripting/programming/engineering/business intelligence/Analytics software, such as Python, SolidWorks, Tableau, Power BI, etc., is desirable. Minimum of 5 years of experience in Wafer Fabrication Process Development in Dry Etch, Photolithography, Films, Wet Process, CMP or Process Integration. Strong complete understanding of PI modules on new NAND technologies and related structural & electrical fail mechanisms. In-depth knowledge and direct experience with non-volatile memories (NAND) and understanding of the NAND process flow, interaction of process & device, and common yield & reliability issues. Experience in R&D and technology transfer to manufacturing. Committed to quality, collaboration, and continuous improvement. Self-motivated with a proven ability to work in a fast-paced & dynamic environment. Education Masters/PhD in Electrical Engineering, Microelectronics, Chemical Engineering, Material Science, or a related field, or equivalent experience.

title: Engineer CE PYE (Physical Failure Analysis/Fault Isolation)
description: We are currently looking for motivated individuals to join Micron Technology Inc. Central Engineering (CE) - Product Yield Enhancement (PYE) Engineering Team. As CE PYE Failure Analysis (FA) Engineer for Micron Technology, Inc., you will be responsible for providing full fault isolation and failure analysis support for qualifications, customer returns, new device introduction, and test failures. You will become the subject matter expert on fault isolation and physical failure analysis methods. As the CE PYE FA Engineer, you will be responsible for understanding all relevant methodologies to isolate electrical failures by working closely with Product Engineering Teams. You will also have a thorough understanding of the product architecture, process flow, and test sequence to work with Process Integration, Product, Test, and Quality Reliability Assurance Engineering groups ensuring physical defects are root-caused and classified. Job Description: Responsibilities: •             Perform sophisticated electrical device characterization to determine electrical fail mechanisms. •             Operate fault isolation equipment to accurately narrow down failure defect locations. •             Conduct physical failure analysis to identify and classify physical defects. •             Establish and hypothesize potential electrical or process fail mechanisms based on detailed electrical fail models and physical failure analysis characterization, supporting process improvement. •             Collaborate closely with a diverse range of collaborators, including teams from frontend to backend and the Global Quality (GQ) department, to address and resolve defectivity issues. •             Prepare and present comprehensive electrical and physical failure mechanisms on a weekly basis to drive continuous improvement. •             Develop and implement new fault isolation and physical failure analysis techniques to stay ahead of industry standards. •             Coordinate the maintenance and high uptime of fault isolation and physical failure analysis tools to ensure world-class operational efficiency. Requirements: •             Bachelor's or equivalent experience, Master's or equivalent experience, or PhD degree in Electrical/Electronic Engineering or Material Science. •             Proven experience as a great team player with outstanding analytical and problem-solving skills and a strong drive to excel. •             Excellent communication skills, with the ability to present complex information effectively in large meetings. •             Highly motivated individuals with a passion for innovation and excellence. •             Knowledge of fault isolation equipment, techniques, and physical failure analysis is a significant advantage. •             Strong understanding of NAND/DRAM/HBM devices and knowledge of Fab processes is a desirable asset. •             Ability to support shift rotation as required to meet operational needs. Manages production support engineering for a specific product or group of products after transfer from design to high volume production. Interfaces with design, process, test and reliability engineering to solve problems. Sustains products with cost reduction and yield improvements.

Develop and implement robust systems and platforms for monitoring project progress on a weekly basis, providing timely feedback to the project planner as assigned. Exhibit strong decision-making capabilities in fast-paced project environments. Provide support to other project teams as and when required to achieve overall project objectives. Qualifications: Education & Training: High School Diploma or equivalent. Diploma in Electrical & Electronic or Mechanical Engineering. Bachelor of Engineering (B.E.) in Electrical/Electronic Engineering is highly preferred. Experience: Minimum of 3 years of hands-on working experience in Electrification projects. Knowledge of Siemens electrification solutions is a significant advantage. Experience in reviewing and updating drawings, specifications, Factory Acceptance Test (FAT) plans, FAT reports, and Site Acceptance Test (SAT) reports. Hands-on experience with high voltage and secondary voltage test equipment in T&C works. Strong understanding of safety significance in the operation and maintenance of High, Medium, and Low Voltage systems, as well as Traction Power Systems in a railway environment, with safety as the highest priority. Proven ability to take complete responsibility for Site Acceptance Testing (SAT/ITC/TTR). Hands-on experience or knowledge of PLC systems (e.g., Siemens, Mitsubishi). Knowledge in ETTS/TSSS systems. Technical Competencies: Basic knowledge of competitor systems in the market. Basic understanding of Functional Safety (IEC 61508) fundamentals and safety functions within core components. Proficiency in Global Business English, both speaking and writing, to facilitate clear international communication. Strong communication skills, including active listening, clarifying, interpreting, and adapting to various situations and personalities.
