title: Global Facilities and Construction Staff Architect
description: Job Description As the Global Facilities & Construction (South Asia)– Senior Architect , you provide high‑quality architectural leadership and technical authority for the planning, design, construction, operation, and maintenance of facilities systems supporting Micron’s global semiconductor manufacturing network. You will act as a subject matter expert throughout all project phases – from concept design and construction to installation, on-going operations, and maintenance – ensuring environmental, safety, regulatory, and code compliance. Key Responsibilities Architectural Design & Leadership Lead and define architectural design strategies for semiconductor facilities, including FABs, CUPs, Probe, Admin Building, and Miscellaneous Buildings. Develop conceptual designs, detailed plans, and specifications in compliance with Micron’s global standards and local/international codes. Provide technical support during construction, including submittal reviews, evaluating GC-initiated changes, developing project modifications, and conducting QA/QC reviews of design documents (e.g., architectural plans, specifications, and calculations). Perform technical reviews of structural design work by A&E firms, GC design teams, and sub-consultants to ensure quality and compliance. Prepare architectural design, RFPs, design documentation reports, and develop architectural layout plans, specifications, and cost estimates for projects. Oversee layout, interior design, and space planning for FABs, CUPs, Probe, Admin, and Miscellaneous Buildings. Conduct field inspections during construction to verify compliance with scope, schedule, and budget. Apply International Building Code (IBC) for exiting, fire compartmentation, LEED, Green Mark, sustainability, occupancy, and building classifications. Design specialized cleanroom components, including raised floors, demountable walls, and ceiling grid systems. Ensure constructability in complex environments, minimizing disruptions near operating buildings. Lead alignment of facility design standards, construction project management practices, and O&M best-known methods (BKM). Perform regular field inspections and monitor projects to ensure adherence to Micron specifications, codes, regulations, and agreed scope, schedule, and budget. Validate cost estimates and construction schedules for architectural aspects of projects. Review and approve design drawings and specifications for completeness and accuracy. Evaluate architectural drawing submissions and supporting documentation to ensure design integrity. Review Autodesk and BIM 3D models for functionality, egress, maintenance accessibility, constructability, and clash analyses coordination with other engineering disciplines. Inspect construction and installation progress for conformance to established drawings, specifications, and schedules. Support all sites, including on-site presence for construction projects and resolution of critical architectural issues Collaborate with consultants, contractors, and multidisciplinary teams to ensure timely, cost-effective project execution. Site Master Planning (SMP) & Space Optimization Develop, propose, and collaborate with global and site teams to generate preliminary site master plan (SMP) concept designs for greenfield and brownfield projects aligned with company strategic goals. Collaborate with multi-functional facilities teams to identify opportunities for space planning optimization and establish standards for critical facilities, rooms and areas. Standardize and continuously improve facilities space management for critical systems, leveraging industry best practices and lessons learned. Assess and mitigate risks related to space constraints in brownfield projects by conducting clash checks during preliminary stages and providing support for project continuity. Project Leadership & Strategic Alignment Lead design and construction of regional greenfield and brownfield projects in alignment with global standards. Collaborate with central sustainability, cost, construction, and line planning teams to ensure strategic alignment. Provide technical support for site projects, Alpha to Beta technology transfer, and sustainability initiatives. Review and approve site capacity expansion and sustainability CAPEX proposals. Evaluate priorities to enable business-driven decisions on Facilities Reliability Improvement project CAPEX. Reliability & Strategic Program Support Provide architectural support for site improvement projects, including System Reliability Improvements, Quality Enhancements, and Equipment End-of-Life (EOL) replacements. Oversee regional efforts and identify opportunities to align strategic programs (e.g., BCP, Critical Specifications, Quality Defense Line). Support major Site Infrastructure Planning reviews and ensure architectural compliance. Sustainability & Certifications Champion LEED and Green Mark certification processes for new and existing projects. Integrate sustainable design principles and energy-efficient solutions into all architectural plans. Support the network effort to implement, monitor and drive continuous improvement for success of Micron’s Sustainability programs in alignment with the long-term Sustainability strategy. In depth, review of site sustainability projects and sustainability data monitoring to support sites and company’s sustainability targets. Support sustainability projects achievement data acquisition and validation in central tracking depository. Interior Design & Space Optimization Oversee interior design for cleanrooms, office spaces, and collaborative environments. Ensure ergonomic, functional, and aesthetic excellence in space planning. Project Oversight & Coordination Manage architectural deliverables throughout design and construction phases. Collaborate with consultants, contractors, and multidisciplinary teams to ensure timely, cost-effective project execution. Compliance & Standards Ensure adherence to local and international building codes, safety regulations, and Micron’s internal standards. Manage permitting and regulatory approval processes. Qualifications Education: Master’s or bachelor’s degree in architecture. Experience: Minimum 10 years of architectural experience in industrial construction projects & minimum 5 year in Semiconductor and/or facilities related industry. Technical expertise, knowledge, and experience on building design and construction of Semiconductor Wafer Fab building and CUB building. Strong background in consulting and construction for fast-track large-scale semiconductor/industrial projects. Certifications: LEED and Green Mark (or equivalent) experience required. Skills: Proficiency in CAD software and BIM tools Expertise in semiconductor cleanroom design and high-tech manufacturing environments. title: Principal Software Engineer (C++, Dart)
description: Job Description Job Description Bring more to life. Are you ready to accelerate your potential and make a real difference within life sciences, diagnostics and biotechnology? At Leica Microsystems, one of Danaher’s 15+ operating companies, our work saves lives—and we’re all united by a shared commitment to innovate for tangible impact. You’ll thrive in a culture of belonging where you and your unique viewpoint matter. And by harnessing Danaher’s system of continuous improvement, you help turn ideas into impact – innovating at the speed of life. Shape the Future with Us! At Leica Microsystems, we have been shaping the future for over 175 years with groundbreaking optical and digital solutions.  With a culture rooted in customer focus, innovation, and teamwork, we lead the market in microscopy, imaging, and analysis, unveiling the invisible and empowering our customers to build a better, healthier world. Joining Leica Microsystems means contributing to scientific discoveries and supporting surgeons in making critical decisions. Our advanced microscopes and AI-based image analysis solutions enable users to gain profound insights into development and engineering challenges. Here, you will work on meaningful projects alongside passionate colleagues, driving progress and pushing the boundaries of what’s possible. Learn about the Danaher Business System which makes everything possible. The Principal Software Engineer is responsible to design and develop full-stack solutions for real-time medical device embedded systems. Lead implementation of user interfaces and backend services, architect scalable solutions, optimize performance across the stack, and ensure code quality while maintaining regulatory compliance and embedded system constraints. This position reports to the Manager, Software and is part of the Research & Development Medical located in Singapore and will be an on-site role. In this role, you will have the opportunity to: Design and implement full-stack solutions across user interfaces and backend services, ensuring compliance with regulatory and embedded system constraints. Own the software development lifecycle from requirements refinement through design, coding, testing, documentation, and release, maintaining high standards of quality and performance. Create and review technical documentation, providing guidance to team members and ensuring clarity and accuracy in all deliverables. Provide technical support to internal teams (Customer Service, RA&QA, Production) by reproducing and resolving issues and toolchain challenges. Collaborate with global, cross-functional teams to align architecture, processes, and deliverables, while driving continuous improvement using DBS and agile methodologies. The essential requirements of the job include Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Human-Computer Interaction, or a related field, coupled with strong communication and collaboration skills for distributed team environments. 8–10 years of professional software development experience, with strong programming skills in C++, Dart, or other modern object-oriented languages (e.g., C#). Proficiency in Flutter or similar cross-platform frameworks for building responsive, high-performance user interfaces, and experience with AI coding tools. Solid understanding of software development methodologies, including Scrum, Agile, and waterfall practices, as well as software testing principles. Deep UI/UX knowledge for embedded systems and familiarity with regulatory compliance requirements in medical device development. Leica Microsystems, a Danaher operating company, offers a broad array of comprehensive, competitive benefit programs that add value to our lives. Whether it’s a health care program or paid time off, our programs contribute to life beyond the job. Check out our benefits at Danaher Benefits Info .

title: Research Fellow
description: About SUTD SUTD is the world’s first Design AI university. With Design AI, artificial intelligence is treated as a partner and a member of the team – not just a tool. As a result of this unique SUTD treatment, AI and humans brainstorm, spar and prototype together, resulting in solutions that are elevated several-fold. This human-AI team concept has been made possible because of SUTD’s unique cohort-based interdisciplinary pedagogy. As a trailblazer in the field of design and technology education and research, SUTD has been pioneering innovative programmes and initiatives since its formation 16 years ago. As part its new mid-term growth strategy called SUTD Leap, it will work closely with industry to co-create solutions in both education and research – for a better world. About the Role We are in search of a highly motivated and accomplished Research Fellow to join our team and significantly contribute to our ongoing research project "Empowering Real-World 3D Scene Understanding: Navigating Noise, Distribution Shifts, and Incremental Learning". The successful candidate will collaborate closely with our research team, working on the development of ground-breaking algorithms and methodologies for Real-world 3D scene understanding problems. This role offers an exciting opportunity to engage in cutting-edge research and have a substantial influence on the field. What You'll Do Undertake research in the field of real-world 3D scene understanding. Design and execute experiments to validate the proposed approaches. Analyze and interpret data sourced from inputs, including images or point clouds. Stay up-to-date with the latest advancements in real-world 3D scene understanding and related fields, and integrate new findings into research projects. Prepare research papers for publication in prestigious conferences and journals. Deliver presentations on research findings at conferences and other pertinent events. Who We're Looking For PhD in computer science (artificial intelligence, machine learning, computer vision, or related fields), with a solid theoretical foundation and extensive research experience. Ability to handle high-intensity research work and demonstrate independent working capabilities. Significant research achievements in computer vision and machine learning, with strong publication records in 3D computer vision and top international journals or conferences. Strong programming skills, proficient in Python, C++, and familiar with common deep learning frameworks such as TensorFlow, PyTorch. Team player with strong academic communication skills and collaborates effectively with team members from diverse backgrounds. International academic exchange and project management experience. Proficiency in both written and spoken English, with the ability to write academic papers and reports and communicate fluently with project collaborators. Strong self-motivation and problem-solving abilities, with capability to drive project progress within a team. Passionate and curiosity for research, willing to actively undertake research projects, mentor students, and participate in academic presentations and seminars.

title: Technical Solutions & Product Engineer
description: 🔐 ABOUT SECUREVISION Securevision is a Singapore-based security systems integrator specialising in intelligent,IP-based security solutions. We design, deploy, and support integrated systems including CCTV, video analytics, intercoms, access control, alarm systems, and cloud-based platforms for residential, commercial, and institutional clients. As we continue to expand our portfolio of brands and solutions, we are looking for a Technical Solutions & Product Engineer to strengthen our technical backbone and product capability. 📌 ROLE OVERVIEW The Technical Solutions & Product Engineer sits at the intersection of product engineering, technical support, and solution enablement. You will be responsible for understanding our systems at a deep level, validating new products and software, supporting internal teams, and translating technical complexity into deployable, supportable solutions. You will work closely with management, sales, installation, and service teams, and play a key role in shaping Securevision’s technical standards and knowledge base. 📝 KEY RESPONSIBILITIES 1. PRODUCT & SOLUTION ENGINEERING Test, evaluate, and validate new products, firmware, and software platforms before adoption Understand security systems at an architecture and system level , including: CCTV, NVRs, and VMS platforms Video intercom systems Access control systems Alarm systems IP networking and cloud components Assess system stability,integration feasibility, deployment constraints, and limitations Provide structured technical feedback and recommendations to management. 2. TECHNICAL SUPPORT & TROUBLESHOOTING Provide 2nd / 3rd level technical support to installation and service teams Troubleshoot system, software, and network-related issues Support customers primarily via remote troubleshooting, with on-site support for complex cases when required Assist in root cause analysis and preventive improvements. 3. KNOWLEDGE BASE & TRAINING ENABLEMENT Build and maintain Securevision’s internal technical knowledge base Summarise vendor manuals into practical operational guides and reference materials Develop internal training decks and technical learning materials for technicians Use AI tools where appropriate to accelerate documentation and knowledge creation. 4. SALES & PROJECT ENABLEMENT Prepare and support system demos Participate in early-stage project discussions and technical solutioning sessions Support technical aspects of proposals and presentations as required Attend project meetings where technical input is needed to shape viable solutions 🎓 REQUIREMENTS Diploma or Degree in Engineering (Electrical / Electronics / Mechatronics), IT, Networking, or a related discipline 2–6 years of experience in security systems, IP-based systems, or enterprise IT environments Strong understanding of IP networking concepts (IP addressing, ports, basic network troubleshooting) Comfortable with software configuration, firmware updates, and system validation Able to work independently and think at a systems level 🧠 PREFERRED EXPERIENCE 1. Hands-on experience with one or more of the following: CCTV and VMS platforms Video intercom systems (e.g. Akuvox or similar) Access control systems (e.g. ZKTeco or similar) Alarm systems (e.g. Risco or similar) 2. Experience conducting product demos or technical training 3. Familiarity with cloud-based or mobile security applications 4. Comfortable using AI tools to assist with learning, documentation, and summarisation 🤩 PREFERRED ATTRIBUTES Strong systems thinker with the ability to connect multiple technologies Clear communicator who can explain complex technical concepts simply Confident in offering alternative or contradictory technical viewpoints when appropriate Detail-oriented, structured, and methodical Curious, fast learner, and adaptable to new technologies. Able to converse professionally in English and Chinese (spoken) to our local customers & vendors Able to read and understand technical documentation in English for audit purposes. Valid Class 3/3A driver’s license for site visits and travel preferred. 💪  WHAT YOU’LL GAIN Exposure to a wide range of modern security technologies and platforms Opportunity to shape product adoption and technical standards A role with strong growth potential into senior technical or solutions leadership Direct involvement in real-world, system-level problem solving 📌 ADDITIONAL INFORMATION This is a Singapore-based role Foreign candidates are welcome, provided they meet role requirements and language expectations Occasional on-site support may be required for complex technical cases 💰 SALARY & BENEFITS Salary Range commensurate with experience Performance bonus, Annual leave & medical benefits Friendly and supportive team environment 🕒 WORK SCHEDULE Location: Singapore, Near Paya Lebar MRT Monday to Friday 8:30 AM to 6:00 PM Occasional overtime may be required.

title: R&D Hardware Design Engineer
description: Job Description Do you demand the best from your professional career? Are you inspired by excellence? At Hewlett Packard Enterprise, you will have the power to make the most out of your career. Hewlett Packard Enterprise is one of the world’s largest and most successful IT Companies. We are successful not just because of the technology solutions that we deliver, but also because of our core values and the amazing people that we have. We invest in our employees’ personal growth & development in an environment that will challenge and reward them. Hewlett Packard Enterprise is filled with energetic people, sparking technology revolutions and creating the future to help improve the lives of every customer. Networking is one of the fastest growing businesses within Hewlett Packard Enterprise and offers opportunities to gain experience with leading edge technologies. Aruba, a Hewlett Packard Enterprise company, offers a comprehensive open and standards-based portfolio designed to address a broad range of customer segments, from small & medium businesses, to the largest enterprises with advanced requirements for leading branch, campus LAN and data center solutions. Aruba’s innovative technology and open, standards-based approach to networking is resonating with customers around the world, and Aruba revenue exceeds the sum of its next five competitors. Here, employees leverage new technologies and innovate to solve real world customer challenges. We are currently looking for a dynamic, talented and innovative individual to be part of our high-speed digital circuit designer team developing state-of-the art networking products. Explore your career options with Aruba, together we will do something amazing! http://www.arubanetworks.com/company/about-us Sounds like you? Then we have the right opportunity for you! HOW YOU WILL MAKE YOUR MARK : As a Hardware Design Engineer, you will be part of a dynamic and highly skilled R&D team that create high quality products. Responsibilities include being responsible for design, component selection, schematic implementation, hardware bring-up, debug and validation. You will collaborate with a multi-sited product development team to integrate your deliverables toward release of new products into production. You will also take part in conducting feasibility, design margin and validation analysis, and empirical testing on new and modified designs. ABOUT YOU: Must have a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Electrical Engineering or related fields. Possess good knowledge and technical understanding of both digital and analog circuit design. Good analytical and problem solving skills. Familiar with Cadence CAD tools. Knowledgeable of programming languages such as Python, Perl and Matlab. ADDITIONAL PREFERRED ABILITIES Outstanding interpersonal skills, with ability to work in teams, ability to work independently and make autonomous decisions when necessary, experience as a team player, and willing to take on a variety of projects. Capability to build relationships and self-motivator. Good oral and written communication skills, plus ability to read, comprehend, and explain engineering drawings and technical documents.

title: Robotics Technician
description: We are building Asimov, an open source humanoid, with embodied intelligence. Our mission is to enable scalable general-purpose autonomy for real world problems (the 3Ds), through large-scale learning, multi-modal data, and robust control. We are seeking a highly motivated Robotics Build Technician to join our growing team and play a pivotal role in bringing Asimov to life. This role is perfect for you if you like getting your hands dirty assembling electronics, welding brackets, or debugging CAN networks and want to work directly on cutting-edge robotic hardware. Responsibilities Perform precise mechanical and electrical integration: harness routing, PCB soldering, cable crimping, and component installation Actively identify mechanical or design flaws during assembly to improve reliability and scalability Build and test custom mounts, fixtures, and test rigs under tight timelines Support the construction of the final robot platform once components arrive (next 3 months) Maintain workshop cleanliness, organize build zones, and ensure safe, compliant operations Document every step clearly, e.g. including photo evidence tied to tickets or linear workflows Troubleshoot systems using automotive-style diagnostic methods (preferred background welcomed!) Work autonomously with minimal oversight, but excellence expected Preferred Qualifications Experienced in mechanical fabrication and electronic circuitry Confident reading wiring schematics and interpreting layout drawings Proficient with hand tools, power tools, multimeters, and basic CAD views Skilled in harness prep, termination, and routing (crimping, heat-shrink, connectors) Familiarity with CAN bus communication and protocol analysis (a plus) Comfortable performing iterative rework and iteration-based problem solving Passionate about clean documentation and reproducible processes Preferably have prior experience in automotive, industrial equipment, or robotics fields Bonus Skills Experience with chassis integration or vehicle-scale systems Knowledge of torque specs, tolerance stacking, and structural integrity checks Exposure to startup paced product cycles

Here, you will work on meaningful projects alongside passionate colleagues, driving progress and pushing the boundaries of what’s possible. Process Knowledge: Demonstrated experience in software requirement engineering, architecture design, and quality management processes. Awareness of emerging technologies and ability to leverage them for improvement. Knowledge of US and EU medical device regulatory requirements is an advantage. title: Principal FPGA Verification Engineer
description: Bring more to life. Are you ready to accelerate your potential and make a real difference within life sciences, diagnostics and biotechnology? At Leica Microsystems, one of Danaher’s 15+ operating companies, our work saves lives—and we’re all united by a shared commitment to innovate for tangible impact. You’ll thrive in a culture of belonging where you and your unique viewpoint matter. And by harnessing Danaher’s system of continuous improvement, you help turn ideas into impact – innovating at the speed of life. Shape the Future with Us! At Leica Microsystems, we have been shaping the future for over 175 years with groundbreaking optical and digital solutions.  With a culture rooted in customer focus, innovation, and teamwork, we lead the market in microscopy, imaging, and analysis, unveiling the invisible and empowering our customers to build a better, healthier world. Joining Leica Microsystems means contributing to scientific discoveries and supporting surgeons in making critical decisions. Our advanced microscopes and AI-based image analysis solutions enable users to gain profound insights into development and engineering challenges. Here, you will work on meaningful projects alongside passionate colleagues, driving progress and pushing the boundaries of what’s possible. Learn about the Danaher Business System which makes everything possible. The Principal FPGA Verification Engineer is responsible for leading the development and implementation of comprehensive testing procedures and protocols for FPGA-based systems. This role focuses on verifying and validating the performance, functionality, and integrity of FPGA designs for advanced microscopy products. The successful candidate will leverage their expertise to develop effective verification and test solutions, collaborating closely with the Research and Development team to identify and address issues throughout the product development cycle. Additionally, the engineer will work with cross-functional teams—including Operations, Supply Chain, Quality, and Logistics—to ensure that product quality, reliability, and robustness meet established standards and exceed customer expectations. This position reports to the Verification Manager and is part of the Research & Development Medical located in Singapore and will be an on-site role. In this role, you will have the opportunity to: Verification Plan and Report Development: Create detailed verification plans, strategies, test cases, and reports to evaluate FPGA functionality, performance, and compliance with project requirements. Provide clear, concise reports with insights and recommendations for improvement. Verification and Validation: Perform comprehensive verification of FPGA designs using various testing tools and equipment to ensure compliance with specified criteria such as speed, reliability, and power consumption. Issue Identification and Debugging: Identify, record, and diagnose issues in FPGA designs, ensuring thorough documentation and efficient resolution in collaboration with the development team. Automated Testing: Develop and implement automated test procedures to enhance testing efficiency and accuracy. Continuous Improvement and Quality Assurance: Oversee continuous improvement in FPGA design verification and validation processes, contribute to quality assurance by evaluating designs against industry standards, and collaborate with cross-functional teams for seamless integration. The essential requirements of the job include Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, or a related field, with strong knowledge of FPGA technologies, FPGA development tools (Xilinx, Altera, etc.), and FPGA testing methodologies. Proficiency in FPGA testing equipment and software, ensuring accurate and efficient verification processes. Experience with scripting and programming languages for test automation, such as Python, VHDL, and Verilog. Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail, with the ability to diagnose and resolve complex technical issues. Preferred background in the Medical Device industry, bringing familiarity with regulatory and quality standards. Leica Microsystems, a Danaher operating company, offers a broad array of comprehensive, competitive benefit programs that add value to our lives. Whether it’s a health care program or paid time off, our programs contribute to life beyond the job. Check out our benefits at Danaher Benefits Info .

title: Product Manager – CMOS Thermal Imaging
description:
About the Role Meridian Innovation is expanding its CMOS-based thermal imaging portfolio and is seeking a Product Manager to lead the definition, execution, and lifecycle management of our thermal products as we scale into our next phase of growth. Built on Meridian’s proprietary CMOS thermal technology platform, our products are designed for manufacturability, cost efficiency, and broad market adoption. This role is responsible for translating market needs into clear product definitions and executing predictable, scalable roadmaps that meet customer, operational, and financial objectives. The Product Manager serves as the bridge between customers, engineering, and the business—ensuring that our thermal technology moves from innovation to high-volume deployment. Key Responsibilities Own product vision, strategy, and multi-generation roadmap for Meridian’s CMOS-based thermal imaging products Define comprehensive product requirements covering resolution, sensitivity, power, frame rate, form factor, and cost, while incorporating AI image processing, reliability, and manufacturability
considerations. Lead products through the full lifecycle: - concept, architecture, design, tape-out, validation, calibration, qualification, production ramp, and sustainment. Drive disciplined trade-offs between thermal performance, silicon complexity, yield, cost structure, and
time-to-market. Work closely with CMOS design, process, test, packaging, firmware, AI image processing, and operations teams to ensure execution predictability Coordinate with foundry, OSAT, optics, and module partners to achieve system-level performance and cost targets Conduct market and competitive analysis to guide product differentiation and portfolio prioritization Support go-to-market execution including positioning, pricing, design-win strategy, customer engagements, and sales enablement Track and manage product KPIs including revenue, ASPs, gross margin, yield, design wins, and customer adoption. Communicate product strategy, milestones, risks, and progress clearly to executive leadership and, as needed, investors and board members. Qualifications – Required Bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering, Physics, or related technical discipline. 5+ years of experience in product management or technical leadership within semiconductors or imaging technologies. Solid understanding of CMOS design trade-offs and high-volume manufacturing constraints. Experience working cross-functionally across design, operations, and commercial teams. Strong analytical skills with the ability to balance technical depth and business outcomes. Clear, concise communication skills suitable for executive and customer-facing discussions. Qualifications – Preferred Experience with CMOS-based thermal, IR, or imaging sensors. Familiarity with calibration, temperature compensation, and system-level thermal performance. Experience scaling products from early adoption to high-volume deployment. Experience in fast-paced growth environments or investor-facing roles. What Success Looks Like CMOS thermal products that meet cost, performance, and scalability targets. Predictable product execution from tape-out through production ramp. Strong design-win momentum across industrial, smart device, and consumer applications. Clear alignment between product roadmaps, operational readiness, and revenue growth. Why Meridian Innovation Mission-driven focus on making thermal imaging mainstream and accessible. Proprietary CMOS thermal technology with clear cost and scalability advantages. High-impact role at a pivotal stage of company growth. Competitive compensation, equity participation, and long-term upside as we scale.

title: Physical AI Engineer
description:
We are looking for Physical AI Engineers focused on developing and
integrating Machine Learning (including Generative AI) models that enable real robots to perceive, reason, and act autonomously. This role bridges Robotics and Agentic AI — working on perception, decision-making, and multi-model integration for multi-robot and embodied systems. You will work on training and adapting Generative/Foundation models
(vision, language, planning, VLA models, swarm reasoning) and deploying them onto edge and robotic hardware. Responsibilities ·       Develop and integrate AI/ML and Generative/Foundation models for perception, mapping, task planning, and embodied decision-making. ·       Fine-tune or adapt Generative/Foundation models (Small Language Model/Small-Vision-Language/ Vision-Language-Action) models for edge deployment for real-world robotic applications. ·       Implement on-device model optimization (quantization, distillation, TensorRT, etc.). ·       Build data pipelines: simulation → real-world → feedback loop for continual model improvement. ·       Conduct domain adaptation and reinforcement/interactive learning for robot skills. ·       Integrating models into ROS2 and swarm autonomy stacks. ·       Evaluate model performance in both simulation and hardware deployment. ·       Optimize inference latency and reliability on edge compute platforms. ·       Formulate the conceptual and detailed technical solution for the development of applications to meet customer requirements. ·       Provide recommendations on relevant emerging technology in Physical AI/Robotics to senior management. ·       Identifying and leading strategic technical capability development for Physical AI/Robotics. ·       Collaborate on research and development projects to explore new capabilities and applications for Physical AI/Robotics technology. Minimum Requirements ·       Bachelor’s/Master’s in Computer Science, Machine Learning, AI, Robotics, or related field. ·       3 years of experience building robotics/Physical AI systems. Candidates without work experience but with relevant skills are also welcome to apply. ·       Strong foundation in ROS2 or robotics middleware integration. ·       Extensive experience building/fine-tuning of AI/ML and Generative/Foundation models (vision, transformer-based, or RL) for robotic systems. ·       Good knowledge of model compression/acceleration for embedded devices (Jetson, Pi,etc). ·       GPU/CUDA experience or on-device inference for embedded AI. ·       Experienced with deployment on embedded/edge Linux systems. ·       Good understanding of robotics model training pipelines (perception → planning →control). Preferred Experience ·       Vision-Language-Action (VLA) or Multi-Modal model experience. ·       Reinforcement learning / imitation learning / interactive training loops. ·       Synthetic data and simulation-based training (Isaac Sim, Habitat, etc.). ·       Knowledge of distributed training pipelines or MLOps for robotics. Additional Skills ·       Strong experimentation and iterative problem-solving mindset. ·       Comfortable bridging research prototypes into production-grade systems. ·       Able to collaborate tightly with AI and systems engineers. ·       Curious and self-driven — able to explore new Physical AI approaches and rapidly test them. What We Offer ·       Opportunity to work on state-of-the-art embodied AI models powering real robots. ·       Combination of research and deployment — not just writing models, but seeing them act in the physical world. ·       High-impact work on cutting-edge robotic autonomy and swarm behaviours.

Also to manage project costs using BIM models , ensuring accurate quantity take-offs, cost planning, and cost control throughout the project lifecycle. Key Responsibilities Tender and Site Walks Participate in tender site walks to assess project requirements and constraints. Lead site surveys to gather accurate data for project planning and estimation. Prepare tender overview and checklist Prepare vendor status checklist Raise queries (RFI & RFC) for any information and clarification. Mechanical and Process Systems o   Demonstrate experience and knowledge in: Mechanical Wet Systems: Chilled Water System (CHW), Condenser Water System (CDW), Drain Water System (DR). Mechanical Dry Systems: HVAC & Mechanical Ventilation Ductwork. Process Wet Systems: Process Cooling Water (PCW), Compressed Dry Air (CDA), Nitrogen (N2), and Waste Water Drainage Systems. Process Dry Systems: Exhaust Ductwork. Technical Drawings and Layouts Knowledge to understand technical documents such as drawings and write-up to meet client requirement. Generate isometric drawings and sketch layouts for contractors to prepare quotations. Scope of Work and Bill of Quantities Develop detailed scope of work (SOW) documentations and bills of quantities (BoQs). Disseminate SOW and BoQs to contractors for quotation preparation. Quotation Management Review vendors’ quotations for accuracy and alignment with project requirements. Gather vendors’ quotations for comparison and cost analysis. Conduct review on cost analysis workout with Project Manager for vendor selections and pricing finalization. Generate project quotations upon final review for submission. Manage and update project tender listing for reference. Material Procurement and Control Identify and finalise materials requirement and to ensure in-line with project specifications and timelines. Finalise terms & conditions and prepare contract agreement and/or purchase order to vendor. Monitor and control materials usage to minimize waste and ensure project efficiency. Monitor Project for any variations or additional work scope. Monitor Contractor Progressive Claim Collaboration and Coordination Coordinate with project teams, clients/consultant, contractors, and suppliers to ensure smooth tender & project execution. Provide technical guidance to vendors during the tender to construction phases Perform quantity take-offs directly from BIM models (Revit / Navisworks / CostX) Maintain 5D BIM cost models and update costs when designs change Qualifications and Requirements Education: Diploma in quantity surveying, Mechanical Engineering, Construction Management, or a related field. Preferance with BIM certification (e.g. Autodesk / buildingSMART) Experience: Minimum of [2] years in quantity surveying or a similar role in the construction industry, with specific experience in: Mechanical Wet Systems (CHW, CDW & DR). Mechanical Dry Systems (HVAC & MV Ductwork). Process Wet Systems (PCW, CDA, N2, Drain). Process Dry Systems (Exhaust Ductwork). BIM-based cost management Technical Skills: Proficient in AutoCAD, Revit, or other drafting software. Strong knowledge of SOW and BoQ preparation. Familiarity with construction project cost estimation and material procurement. Soft Skills: Excellent analytical, organizational, and problem-solving abilities. Critical thinking to spot issues and come up with smart solutions Attentive to detail to ensure every aspect is considered for better results Strong communication and negotiation skills to manage contracts confidently Ability to work collaboratively in a fast-paced environment. Time management skills to be able to meet deadlines under pressure Teamwork capabilities with all parties to avoid costly misunderstandings Mobility: As when needed

title: Software Developer (Learning Sciences & Assessment Department) [NIE]
description: ABOUT THE NATIONAL INSTITUTE OF EDUCATION (NIE) The National Institute of Education (NIE), Singapore , is Singapore’s national teacher education institute and we are proud to be an integral part of the nation’s education service. We play a key role in the preparation of teachers and in the provision of teacher professional and school leadership development programmes. We are committed to our vision of being An Institute of Distinction: Leading the Future of Education and our mission to Inspire Learning, Transform Teaching and Advance Research . Read more about NIE here . NIE invites applications for the position of part-time software developer for the project “PhET-style simulations to promote AI literacy”. The project’s goal is to develop a curated library of interactive, high-quality online resources for teachers that puts NIE at the forefront of AI literacy. To work towards this goal, the project will start out by developing customizable web-based simulations and corresponding avatar-based narrative instructional videos catering to three fundamental topics in AI literacy - Personalization using Machine Learning and Neural Networks , Ethical Issues in AIED , Human-AI Collaboration. These simulations would allow student teachers to experiment with relevant critical task features in a hands-on manner, thus improving their understanding of abstract technical concepts in AI and the implications of their usage in AI systems for education. Project evaluation would focus on usability evaluation and learning evaluation of the programmed simulations. Facilitation resources and validated design guidelines for developing effective AI literacy simulations will be made available to educational stakeholders upon project completion. This is a contract position till 30 November 2026. Responsibilities Gathering requirements for simulation design from domain experts Agile prototyping of mock UI/UX interfaces for the simulations Programming and iteratively refining the simulations based on expert feedback and usability testing Building data logging capabilities in the designed simulations Developing facilitation guides to assist stakeholders’ work with designed simulations Working with NIE’s IT teams to host simulations securely and ensure seamless interoperability with existing NIE platforms Managing the project and ensuring timely completion of milestones Requirements Essential A Polytechnic Diploma Full stack software development and debugging experience Knowledge of computer graphics and interface design Fluent in coding with generative AI Solid understanding of fundamental machine learning and AI techniques Creativity, good communication skills and ability to work as team player Able to work independently with minimal supervision Preferred Work, teaching, or corporate training experience in AI for education Familiarity with AI literacy efforts worldwide, in APAC and Singapore Up-to-date with latest advancements in generative AI (for education) Passion for improving teaching and learning with technology Application We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Other Information NIE staff can take chartered buses at their own expense from or near their home to the NIE campus. This is subject to the availability of seats.

title: Director Automation & Systems Integration (Tuas)
description: At ResMed, we’re on a mission to change lives with every breath — and our global supply chain is rapidly transforming to enable growth, resilience, quality, and value at scale. We’re hiring a Director of Automation & Systems Integration to lead the design, standardisation, and deployment of automation and digital-integration platforms across our manufacturing and distribution network. This role builds the automation backbone of our next-generation supply chain; engineering scalable, reliable, and intelligent systems that increase productivity, improve asset performance, and enable scale. The Opportunity As Director of Automation & Systems Integration leads the global engineering organization responsible for robotics, automation, controls, and systems integration across all supply chain sites. You will define automation design principles and standard platforms, and form strategic engineering partnerships with technology providers and Automation OEMS — going well beyond a “smart customer” to co-create, refine, and scale automation solutions across the network. This role integrates automation with MES, WES/WCS, SCADA, PLC/controls, and digital data layers to enable real-time visibility, predictive insights, and improved decision making. You’ll work closely with Industrial Engineering, Manufacturing Engineering, Global Logistics & Distribution, Planning, Quality, Digital Supply Chain, and Product Development to ensure automation solutions deliver throughput, reliability, safety, and long-term capacity. This role is foundational to our Network 2030+ vision: a globally consistent, modular, automation-ready network capable of scaling with speed, resilience, and confidence. Key Responsibilities Automation Strategy & Network 2030+ Alignment Build and execute a global automation strategy that accelerates automation as the core productivity driver of our next-generation supply chain. Define standardised automation design principles, modular architectures, controls frameworks, and platform strategies deployable across regions. Develop a multi-year roadmap for robotics, AGVs/AMRs, AS/RS, automated test platforms, and material movement automation aligned to network growth and supply chain transformation. Serve as a strategic partner in evaluating automation opportunities and ensuring investments align with business value, operational needs, and supply chain transformation priorities. Systems Integration & the Digital Thread Lead systems integration of automation assets with MES, WES/WCS, SCADA, PLC/Controls IoT, and enterprise data systems Establish integration toolkits, templates, interface libraries, and testing protocols to ensure consistency and reliability. Develop and partner to create standard integration architectures, integration toolkits, and interface libraries that ensure interoperability, scalability, and reliability. Partner with Digital Supply Chain teams to connect automation performance data to real-time dashboards, predictive analytics and digital twin, enabling smarter CapEx and planning decisions . Ensure new and existing automation platforms are integrated securely and consistently, enabling faster deployment and reduced engineering rework. Robotics, Automation Platforms & Deployment Excellence Lead engineering evaluation, technology selection, and deployment of robotics, automation technologies across manufacturing and distribution environments (paperless systems, robotics, LSR automation, AGVs/AMRs, material handling systems, automated test and inspection). Govern engineering specifications, vendor selection, lifecycle cost models, standard controls architecture, FAT/SAT protocols, and commissioning excellence. Establish an Automation Center of Excellence to accelerate standardisation, replicate best practice, and scale solutions globally at pace. Champion design-for-replication to enable rapid, reliable deployment of automation platforms across sites. Asset Performance, Reliability & Automation Health Ensure all automation and integrated systems produce accurate, high resolution performance data to support OEE improvement, predictive maintenance, and capacity optimization. Partner with Industrial Engineering and Asset Performance teams to leverage downtime analytics, bottleneck analysis, TPB aligned reliability engineering practices. Embed automation health monitoring, diagnostics, and predictive analytics to improve uptime, throughput, and line stability. Enable real-time and global automation dashboards, linking physical automation performance directly into planning and continuous improvement. New Site Establishment & Automation-Ready Design Define automation and integration requirements for new supply chain sites, ensuring automation readiness from concept to commissioning. Partner with Facility and Industrial Engineering to embed automation early in layout design, utilities planning, safety architecture, and CVS (Commissioning, Validation, Scale) processes. Deploy modular automation solutions that reduce commissioning times, improve CapEx efficiency,  and enable predictable scalability across the network . Leadership, Governance & Capability Building Build a world-class global team of automation, robotics, controls, and systems integration engineers. Establish engineering governance to ensure global standardization, interoperability, and consistent decision-making across all sites. Create training pathways and capability-building programs to develop future-ready, digitally enabled automation talent. Foster a culture grounded in engineering rigor, innovation, collaboration, and continuous improvement. About You 12–15+ years in automation engineering, robotics, systems integration, or advanced manufacturing engineering, including 5+ years in senior leadership roles. Deep expertise in automation platforms including robotics, AGVs/AMRs, automated test systems, material storage and handling automation, and controls architecture and digital integration (MES, SCADA, PLC, WES/WCS, IoT). Strong manufacturing automation experience is essential; exposure to distribution automation is valuable. Experience with TPM, reliability engineering, or maintenance strategies for automated systems is highly beneficial Demonstrated success delivering global automation deployments in highly regulated, multi-site supply chain environments. Strong understanding of OEE, bottleneck analysis, predictive maintenance, flow modeling, and asset performance improvement. Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Automation, Mechatronics, Systems, Electrical, or Manufacturing Engineering; advanced digital or robotics certifications a plus. Exceptional leadership and communication skills, with the ability to influence senior stakeholders across global supply chain landscapes. Success Metrics Automation Excellence Deliver measurable productivity and flow improvements through scalable automation solutions. Platform Standardization: Drive adoption of standardized automation architectures across the majority of applicable sites within the first 18–24 months. Asset Performance & Reliability Improve uptime and automation reliability through enhanced diagnostics, predictive maintenance, and data-driven monitoring Digital Thread & Integration Velocity Reduce integration time and engineering rework by implementing standard architectures and reusable components. Speed, Scalability & Capital Efficiency Shorten automation deployment timelines at new or expanding sites through modular design and engineering governance. People & Capability Build and sustain a high-performing automation engineering team with strong engagement and development progression. Why Join ResMed This is a rare opportunity to define the automation and integration blueprint of a global, next-generation supply chain. You’ll architect intelligent, connected automation systems that power how we grow, scale, and deliver — enabling extraordinary outcomes for patients worldwide. At ResMed, you’ll join a global team committed to excellence, innovation, and purpose. Together, we’ll build the most integrated, data-driven, and scalable supply chain engineering network in the world.

title: Senior Engineer, Process Development, CMP
description: Micron’s NAND Process Development (PD) Singapore group is looking for Senior Process Development Engineer to join Advanced Node team to support TD activities associated with rapid deployment of technology nodes, the process margin improvement work necessitated thereof as well as to ensure extended engagement with manufacturing on the upcoming technology nodes. Main Responsibilities: As a CMP Process Development Engineer, you will be primarily responsible for developing and optimizing CMP processes to improve product quality and reliability for the next generation of Micron's memory parts. Focus on understanding and improving the process margins required for meeting the mature yield goal for 3D NAND parts. Advanced 3D NAND CMP process development will include performing fundamental research, consumables development, and hardware evaluation, as well as testing processes for novel applications. Initiate and manage experiments to widen process margins as well as to evaluate manufacturability of next node solutions. Extensively collaborate with vendors to develop processes that meet integration requirements for current and next-generation 3-D NAND nodes. Incorporate best known manufacturing methods into early development phase of upcoming nodes. Design and implement advanced process monitoring and control methodologies. Provide strategic roadmap for N+, N++ and future NAND Nodes. Function in a technical advisory role within Micron contributing to technical projects for improvements to processes, procedures, and equipment. Define and leads complex, multi-functional projects of critical importance to Micron, helps to define strategic direction. Provide advice and counsel to management on significant technical issues. Leads and initiate innovation projects through patents/technical papers. This position requires communication and collaboration with associates both within the region and internationally. Strict adherence to Micron Intellectual Property Protection policy is necessary. Other Responsibilities: Mentor new hires and less experienced team members on CMP fundamentals, process development, and Micron systems, Engage with Procurement & Operations Central Teams and other partners both locally and globally pushing for breakthrough solutions. As a technical expert, lead or drive team or sites on complex, multi-disciplinary projects in quality focused meetings, process and yield improvement meetings, cost, and productivity improvement meetings. Job Requirements/Qualifications PhD in Materials Science, Chemical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Chemistry or Physics with minimum 1yr of relevant experience in semiconductor industry with background in Chemical Mechanical Planarization (CMP) in 300mm wafer fabrication Must Have Technical Skills Hands-on experience CMP equipment operation such as AMAT (LKP), EBARA-300X and next generation CMP equipment, optical metrology, profilometry, polishing slurries, conditioners, and polishing pads Experience in dielectrics/metals CMP process development on 3D NAND or other memory technology Understanding of various integration and structural impacts and constraints related to film deposition and other upstream/downstream processes Must Have Soft Skills Excellent oral and written communication skills with ability to convey the message in concise and effective manner both in local as well as remote communications. Strong collaboration and communication skills (verbal and written). Self-driven, adaptable, and able to manage multiple projects. Proven ability to take calculated risks and execute novel solutions. Recognized as a mentor, team player and technical expert with the ability to solicit feedback, accept input, and analyze success/failure. Highly Desirable/Preferred skills or experience An understanding of the general process steps and process flow for memory processing planar and vertical NAND memory. Apply Lean and Industrial Engineering methodologies to improve Man, Machine, Material, and Space utilization. Drive improvements in factory yield, OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness), and cycle time. Build and design automated workflow systems that support Factory 4.0 initiatives. Lead development of capacity dashboards to enhance visibility for key stakeholders. Lead overall factory capacity planning to meet customer volume demand. Collaborate with functional departments to expand capacity through process and equipment improvement. Support the IE Manager with budget planning and long‑term factory strategies. About you: Bachelor’s degree in Industrial Engineering or relevant disciplines. Strong communication, teamwork, interpersonal, presentation, and leadership skills. Ownership, adaptability, and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast‑moving environment. Proficiency in data management, data processing, automation tools, Tableau, PowerBI, KNIME, Python, and optimization programming. A positive, self‑motivated, and flexible working approach. Willing to travel to Thailand for one (1) month for on-job-training. Your experience includes: Minimum 5 years in industrial engineering or manufacturing planning. Proficiency in data analysis and simulation using Python, FlexSim, and Arena. Strong knowledge of hard disk drive (HDD) manufacturing processes.

title: Research Engineer (in Multimodal AI ) - MRP
description: Job Purpose: The primary responsibility of this role is to deliver on an industry innovation research project where you will be part of the research team to develop a Multimodal AI for Fire Detection & Safety Systems. Key Responsibilities: Participate in and manage the research project with Principal Investigator (PI), Collaborator and the research team members to ensure all project deliverables are met. Undertake these responsibilities in the project: - Develop and deploy multimodal AI algorithms for fire, smoke, and hot-work detection by fusing optical, thermal/infrared, LiDAR, RADAR, and gas sensor data under varying environmental conditions. - Design computer vision and human-behavior analysis models for detecting personnel, posture, casualties, and hazardous situations, including operation in low-visibility scenarios. - Implement scene mapping, environment perception, and path-planning algorithms using LiDAR and RADAR data to support evacuation guidance and first-responder navigation. - Build real-time predictive models for fire intensity and spread forecasting and integrate LLM-based scene description and decision support into an end-to-end deployable system. Carry out Risk Assessment, and ensure compliance with Work, Safety and Health Regulations. Coordinate procurement and liaison with vendors/suppliers. Work independently, as well as within a team, to ensure proper operation and maintenance of equipment. Job Requirements Bachelor’s degree (minimum) in Electrical / Electronics Engineering, Computer Engineering, Computer Science, Robotics, or a closely related discipline, with foundational knowledge in signal processing and machine learning. Working knowledge of computer vision and deep learning concepts, including object detection and image-based classification, with hands-on experience using Python and at least one deep learning framework (e.g., PyTorch or TensorFlow). Basic experience handling and processing sensor data (e.g., camera, thermal, LiDAR, RADAR, or similar perception sensors) in real-world or laboratory settings. Familiarity with Linux-based development environments and standard software tools for data analysis, model training, and evaluation. Ability to independently implement, test, and document applied research solutions, with prior experience through projects, internships, or research assistantships considered sufficient. Prior experience in publishing is desirable.

title: Senior/Principal IT & AV Design Consultant
description: The Team Our dedicated ICT Consultancy (sitting within our wider Technology Group) plays a key role in digitally enabling the built environment to provide the technologically enhanced solutions commensurate with our clients’ needs. We pride ourselves on being a market leader in our field, working globally with high profile clients in data centres, fintech, HQ offices, and smart buildings. Our reputation has led to us advising on global technology standards for multi-national companies and benchmarking and technological certification of buildings across the globe. We pride ourselves on our ability to solve complex technical problems, demonstrate adaptability, work collaboratively as a team, Practice and Company, and share knowledge, problems, and solutions as a team to harness the power of all minds and perspectives. Operating globally, our Team is based in the heart of Singapore near Orchard Central, Central London right next to St Paul’s Cathedral, and in Dubai overlooking to Dubai Marina. The Role This is an excellent opportunity for an experienced and client-focused consultant to join the team at our Singapore office, to play a key role in ensuring the successful delivery of innovative ICT design solutions for some of the most exciting and high-profile projects in Singapore and internationally. Data centres are a key growth area for Cundall, and you will have the opportunity to take ownership of our new and existing projects in the APAC region. In this role, you will have the opportunity to work closely with senior and junior members of the team and wider project teams. You will lead the design and implementation of ICT and Telecoms systems for not only data centres, but across a range of different projects and sectors, with oversight of AV and Smart Building systems where required. As a Senior or Principal Consultant, you will play a key role in requirements capture, technical assessments, and developing strategies and solutions that meet project objectives, industry standards and best practices. You will have the opportunity to contribute to the development of ICT masterplans, site assessments and feasibility studies, design specifications, all whilst collaborating with internal and external multidisciplinary teams. Through your foundational knowledge of ICT systems, based on a strong academic background with plenty of work-based experience, you will: Act as technical lead for allocated projects, coordinating the production of designs with relevant team members, ensuring that designs conform to client requirements, health and safety laws, industry standards and best practice. Design ICT physical infrastructure to support passive, and at times active systems, such as structured cabling systems, outside plant, ICT rooms, Wi-Fi, containment systems and risers. Coordinate the production of project drawings (e.g. schematics, layouts, installation details, rack elevations etc.)  using Autodesk Revit and AutoCAD, in accordance with company and project standards Lead the QA and QC process as a checker and verifier. Develop complete design packages including detailed drawings, calculations, technical specifications, employer’s requirements, and tender documentation. Be involved with construction site visits to undertake compliance monitoring roles of ICT works, working alongside other disciplines. Collaborate within the wider Cundall team and external partners, working closely with colleagues of various disciplines to support the delivery of technically excellent and successful multi-disciplinary projects. Support the senior management staff within the team, embracing the collaborative and supportive behaviours Cundall exhibit. This includes line management, team mentoring and chartership mentoring. Contribute to commercial planning and management of projects. The Skills Educated to degree level in Electrical Engineering, ICT or Computer Science (MEng, BEng or BSc), and working towards, or already be, a member of a professional institute. You will be aiming for a career in ICT and telecoms systems design and construction in the built environment. We look for 5-8 years of experience working in an engineering design consultancy or for a contractor. Desired traits for the successful candidate include: Ability to take ownership for tasks and deliverables. Capacity to problem-solve and help guide the team and client towards solutions Organised and confident communicator, with the ability to gather client requirements and lead internal and external meetings Knowledge and experience in designing ICT and telecoms systems: Passive ICT systems, such as structured cabling systems and containment. Active ICT systems, such as Wi-Fi and GSM. Hyperscale and colocation data centre telecoms infrastructure, including outside plant. Satellite communications, antennas, and aerials. MEP facilities for ICT, including power and cooling. Interface between ICT and other building systems, including BMS and Security. Experienced in leading site visits for surveys, witnessing and commissioning, to support design work. Understanding of project resourcing and commercial management would be advantageous. Working knowledge of drawing and model production using Autodesk AutoCAD and Revit. Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications. Excellent communication skills in written and spoken English. WiredScore and/or SmartScore Accredited Professional would be an advantage. Interest or ability to code in languages such as Dynamo and Python would be desirable.

Track and report any changes to the scope of work and help ensure that changes are properly documented and communicated to all relevant parties. Assist in preparing and submitting reports to the Project Manager, clients, and stakeholders, highlighting progress, challenges, and upcoming milestones. title: Simulation Senior Staff Engineer
description: Job Responsibilities Develop and validate complex mathematical models and algorithms to simulate the physics of the slit coating process, including fluid flow,material properties (viscosity, surface tension, rheology), tool parameters (coating speed, coating gap, slot die head design), and thermal dynamics. Utilize CFD software to analyse and screen a broad range of tool parameters and material variables to identify optimal die geometry, ideal material properties (e.g., a target viscosity range), and a stable operating window without consuming expensive materials or time. Design and execute simulation experiments to test hypotheses, identify critical process parameters (e.g., coating speed, flow rate, die gap, substrate speed), and validate model accuracy against physical experimental data. Collaborate with cross-functional teams including design engineers, material scientists, and manufacturing teams to design highly focused DoE guided by the insights from simulation and validate the simulation models and confirm the optimized parameters in a real-world setting Analyse simulation results, interpret findings, and provide actionable insights and recommendations to improve coating quality, reduce defects, and enhance process efficiency. Document simulation methodologies, assumptions, and results comprehensively in technical reports and presentations for internal and external stakeholders. Stay current with advancements in simulation software, numerical methods, and relevant industry standards related to thin-film deposition and organic electronics. Job Requirements Required Education: Master's or Ph.D. in a relevant engineering or science field with a focus on computational methods or fluid dynamics. Experience: Proven experience using simulation tools for complex engineering problems, ideally in fluid dynamics or coating processes. Technical Skills: Proficiency in simulation software (COMSOL, ANSYS Fluent), programming (Python, MATLAB, or C++), fluid dynamics, surface tension, and material science. Knowledge of quality control and data analysis is also required. Soft Skills: Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills. Ability to work both independently and as part of a team. Preferred Experience with slot-die coating or other thin-film deposition techniques. Familiarity with CAD software. Knowledge of machine learning/AI for simulations. Experience in the slit coating of organic dielectrics.

title: System Engineer
description: We are searching for a System Engineer to be part of our diverse team here in Glamco. Our System Engineers are key component of our operations by providing technical support, testing, installation, training, support and maintenance services for our programs in Singapore. In addition, the System Engineer shall support the Program / Project Manager on the project and system maintenance life cycle from pre-sales to maintenance. Candidates must show initiative, highly independent, hands-on, aspiration for increase role and responsibilities, and a demonstrated willingness to work in a team. Job Responsibilities: Reporting to the Program / Project Manager, the System Engineer will be using their expertise and experience to support both internal Glamco and external Customer activities for our Singapore commercial program. Specific responsibilities but not limited to the following includes: · Provide system engineering and networking support to our programs in Singapore including but not limited to hardware and software configuration and set up, troubleshooting networks and system defects, and fielding technical questions about hardware and software. · Manage and provide onsite and remote customer support including system and infrastructure installation, configuration, testing, training, maintenance, site-surveys etc. · Provide system engineering and networking expertise to our programs in Singapore during pre-sales and post-sales. · Develop, prepare and conduct test cases for customer’s program. · Develop and maintain technical and configuration documentation, training materials and user manuals. · Serve as a Technical Support expert and capable of handling escalations, incidents, outages and infrastructure challenges. · Evaluate and upgrade system hardware and/or firmware with consultation with program partners. Perform system patching and deployment. · Lead and manage a team of Technical Officers to provide technical and maintenance support to clients. · Support the Program / Project Manager to manage the project and system maintenance lifecycle from Proposal development to Project Kickoff to Initial Design to Development and Testing to Customer Acceptance Testing to System Maintenance Program. · Support the Business Development Team in determining project scope of work. · Ensure project and maintenance SLA (Service Level Agreement) and customer satisfaction levels are met. · Maintain standard operating procedures (SOP), processes and guidelines. · Maintain security, backup and redundancy strategies. · Research new technologies and solution in support of the programs. · Any other technical, operations or project related duties that may be required. Requirements · Diploma or Degree in Information Technology or Computer Engineering or related field. · Minimum of 3 years working experience in IT Support, Network, Systems, Cyber Security type function. · Knowledge or experience with network and system administration including firewall and network infrastructure topology, networking protocols, routing and switching, and firewall configuration. · Knowledge or experience with server and workstation administration. · Checkpoint Firewalls, PaloAlto Firewalls, Fortinet Firewall, Cisco Switches, HP Aruba Switches, VPN Technologies, DNS, NTP, 2 Factor Authentication technologies. · Experience in running projects is a plus points. · Strong interest and experience in the field of Radar and Radio Frequency (RF) technologies. · Hardware, software and technical aptitude. · Strong analytical and critical thinking skills. · Show initiative, self-discipline, excellent judgment, career aspirations for increased role and responsibilities, a demonstrated willingness to work as a team with the ability to work independently. · Willing to perform potential shift duties, weekends and overtime. · Willing to travel overseas to support overseas requirements. · Willing to work in an SME environment. · Must possess Class 3 Manual driving license. - Ownership of a car is an advance. About Glamco: GLAMCO Aviation Pte Ltd (GLAMCO) prides itself as a leading solutions provider for the Aerospace, Commercial, Air & Maritime Traffic Management and Defence & Security industries. Established in Singapore since 1985, GLAMCO provides component repair and overhaul, spares support, specialized products and other related consultancy services to Aircraft operators, Defence and Security agencies, Civil Airport and Seaport Operators. Representing Aircraft Manufacturers, Systems OEMs, Stocking Distributors and OEMs worldwide, we are able to support Programme Management, quick response and delivery. GLAMCO has also expanded its activities to include the supply of specialized equipment for the Navy, Army, as well as Civil Airport and Seaport. Since 2020s, GLAMCO has further expanded our services to provide logistics management (warehouse), system engineering and maintenance support to our customers. To learn more about us and our products, please visit our website at www.glamco.com.sg .

title: Environmental Designer
description: Atelier Ten is an innovative multidisciplinary firm of building services engineers environmental design consultants, and lighting designers focused on delivering high-quality building services design and sustainable design consultancy within the built environment sector. We aim to deliver highly sustainable design solutions based on the touchstones of environmental integrity, economic viability, and pragmatic engineering solutions. These principles are embedded in our collaborative and creative approach to problem solving and efficient resource utilisation. We seek to reduce complexity. A core objective is to meet the needs of our clients by developing well-integrated buildings with simple systems that work with natural laws of physics to provide comfort while reducing energy consumption and contributing back to the greater environment. At Atelier Ten, we take a wider role in the design process than traditional building services engineers and consultants: in particular we work with the design team to integrate environmental moderation and control into the fabric of the building. From this improved basis we develop efficient and well-integrated building services systems to provide comfortable and efficient internal environments. Atelier Ten has a history of innovation. Founded in 1990, we are not afraid to challenge conventional thinking. Our environmental ethos is not an additive one; rather we strive to make buildings do more with less by working from basic principles and employing rigorous analysis. Our success is built on the expert delivery of innovative and sustainable engineering solutions. To support Atelier Ten’s continued success. We have established, and continue to maintain, efficient and effective business knowledge and quality management systems in order to provide our clients - in both the private and public sector - with first-class services covering the full range of design, construction, building services, engineering and maintenance disciplines, and by providing first-class project development and management services including an award-winning design solutions consultancy service. The post holder will be responsible for supporting work on a range of projects assisting the delivery of environmental and sustainability advice on projects for both internal clients and external clients. This will involve providing consultancy on environmental design, sustainability benchmarking/implementation, building energy optimisation, daylight & shading design, sustainable systems, materials and waste. This consultancy will be demonstrated through sound analytics including building physics. Projects will range from masterplanning to detailed design of specific buildings across all scales and locations of the globe. Using industry benchmark and internal standards, the post holder will ensure not only the timely delivery of accurate project information but also provide a point of contact between engineering staff and the other members of the environmental team. Key Accountabilities: Technical ability Coordinate, complete and ensure Green Rating projects in any given green rating scheme (Green Mark, LEED etc.) Complete simulations such as energy modelling, CFD, daylighting etc. Actively committed to pursuing CPD of all relevant disciplines Demonstrate reasonable knowledge of environmental design, sustainability benchmarking/implementation, building energy optimisation, daylight & shading design, sustainable systems, materials, waste and building physics Actively committed to developing specialist skills in one, or more, disciplines Demonstrate an understanding of Atelier Ten’s approach Able to provide accurate calculations and informative design options in relevant disciplines Deliver technical solutions in accordance with client brief Help facilitate the development of client brief by supporting other members of their team Willingness to develop an awareness and understanding of other disciplines Project management Liaise with client, architect and other members of the internal or external design team Can effectively co-ordinate with in-house engineers, the environmental teamand members of the graphics team Can develop appropriate design, proposals or analysis within the agreed time scale Maintain overview of project work and provide updates as and when required to line manager/project manager Interpersonal Skills Appreciates the need for effective liaison between Atelier Ten, clients and architects Report any feedback or issues concerning the project from clients Foster positive relationships within Atelier Ten Requirements: Sustainability, Engineering, Architectural or related disciplines Bachelor’s or higher degree in a field related to environmental building design, architecture, building services engineering, and/ or building science Fresh graduates are welcome to apply. title: Principal Software Test Engineer
description: Bring more to life. Are you ready to accelerate your potential and make a real difference within life sciences, diagnostics and biotechnology? At Leica Microsystems, one of Danaher’s 15+ operating companies, our work saves lives—and we’re all united by a shared commitment to innovate for tangible impact.
