title: Process Safety Engineer
description: We are looking for a Process Safety Engineer (m/f/d) to join our Process Safety department at Advario in Singapore, starting as soon as possible. Job Profile: The Process Safety Engineer leads the implementation and continuous improvement of Process Safety Management (PSM) systems to identify, assess, control and monitor major hazard risks in the terminals. The PSE leads the delivery of Process Safety initiatives by collaborating with Engineering, Operations, HSSE teams and regulatory authorities to ensure compliance with Singapore’s regulations and industry best practices. He/She tracks PSM performance data and contributes to the development and maintenance of the relevant sections of the organisation's Major Hazard Installation (MHI) Safety Case. He/She conducts process safety reviews and hazard/risk studies for existing and new facilities. The PSE also participates in process safety incident investigations and follows up on preventive and corrective actions. Your Tasks: 1) Administer Process Safety Management Systems Support Process Safety framework adoption and implementation for the local organization in accordance to relevant local regulations, internal and industry standards/best practices. Ensure governance of Process Safety Management (PSM) system is in place to achieve organization's operational effectiveness and efficiency, reliable reporting, compliance to laws, regulations and policies through: - Develop operational process safety solutions by defining, studying, estimating, and screening alternative solutions. - Improve operational process safety results by studying, evaluating, and recommending process re-design; implementing changes; contributing information and opinion to engineering design and modification teams. - Monitor and report key process safety performance indicators, track action closure, support audits and compliance reviews, promote continuous improvement in risk control. Investigate Process Safety incidents with the local team and propose suitable improvements / recommendations to avoid reoccurrence. Identifies operational process safety problems by observing and studying system and performance results. 2) Support Process Engineering Projects Review and/or provide support in e.g. Process Hazard Analyses (PHA), MOCs, risk assessments, safety studies, Safety Case and project designs, to ensure process safety related considerations are identified and addressed. Develop recommendations and addressing Process Safety deficiencies identified in these reviews, where applicable and ensure relevant documentation is complete. To systematically seek out process safety improvement opportunities and assess risk to ALARP 3) Administer Staff and Organizational Development Build Process Safety competency in the organization through training sessions on process safety topics. Your Profile: Bachelor’s degree in in Chemical Engineering, Process Engineering, Safety Engineering (or equivalent with relevant experience) Minimum 5 years working experience in oil and gas industry, specializing in process safety and hazard management Strong knowledge of process hazard identification and risk assessment methods and tools (e.g. HAZOP, LOPA, QRA, Bowtie). Knowledge of international and local safety standards and regulatory requirements (e.g., API, NFPA, SS, functional safety). Strong terminal operations knowledge, its drivers from a technical and risk point of view. Good communication, analytical, problem-solving and stakeholder-management skills; able to work across engineering, operations, maintenance and HSSE teams. Proficient in consequence modeling software (preferred). Please note: This position is based in Jurong Island Official Working hours: Mon - Fri, 0830 - 1700 hours We offer you among other things : Special benefits and company events organized for employees Employees are covered under the group insurance plan, with dependent coverage for hospitalization Lunch is provided while in the terminal and company shuttle bus transport to and from Jurong East MRT

Conduct regular maintenance checks and repairs on marine engines and related equipment to avoid breakdowns and maximize operational efficiency. Troubleshoot and identify mechanical difficulties, then apply effective remedies. Ensure that all actions are carried out in accordance with corporate policy and maritime safety standards. Maintain a clean and safe workplace at all times. Fresh graduates in related field are welcomed.

title: Stamping Process Engineer
description: Job Responsibilities •           Analyze, troubleshoot and resolve stamping related problems systematically to achieve productivity, quality and cost targets. •           Assist in stamping tool design reviews for new product development •           Work closely with the Production Team to achieve set goals and targets. •           Establish process capability, optimum parameter setting, mill cost, Bill of Material (BOM) and work instruction for dies and processes. •           Identify / propose cost reduction program / upgrading to achieve quality and competitive cost to meet present and future stamping requirements. •           Provide technical guidance / training to Toolmakers / Production Technicians to ensure effective operations of the area. •           Train and lead a team of Toolmakers and Production Technicians •           Work to comply with safety requirements and maintain safe working habits. Job Requirements •           Possess a Diploma in Mechanical / Production or NTC II in tool and die making. •           For diploma holders: Minimum of 5 years’ experience in high speed stamping environment. •           For NTC II holders: Minimum of 15 years’ experience in die maintenance and stamping. •           Taking initiative and contributing innovative ideas are advantageous to this position. •           Good analytical skills and time management capability. •           Able to read and interpret die and part drawing •           Possess good knowledge in metal forming and die making / die maintenance. •           Able to work under tight schedule. •           Able to plan work systematically •           Able to analyze problem for solution with minimum effort and time •           Experience in running mass production of precision parts is advantageous Working hours: Mon to Fri, 8:15am to 5:45pm

title: Learning Designer
description: Responsibilities: Support the design and development of e-learning courses that meet client training goals and learning outcomes. Assist in conducting learning needs analysis and gathering content from subject matter experts. Develop instructional content, storyboards, and assessments based on sound learning principles and methodologies. Create engaging and interactive digital learning content using authoring tools (e.g., Articulate Rise, Storyline). Collaborate with the team to design blended learning experiences that include videos, quizzes, and interactive media. Translate learning objectives into learner-centric experiences that are easy to understand and apply. Review and refine learning content based on feedback and testing. Work closely with instructional designers, multimedia specialists, and developers to ensure content quality and alignment with learning outcomes. Requirements: Degree in any field. ACTA / ACLP certified (Advanced Certificate in Training and Assessment) preferred. 1–2 years of experience in instructional design, curriculum development, or e-learning development. Familiarity with instructional design models (e.g., ADDIE, SAM) and adult learning principles. Proficient in using authoring tools and comfortable with technology-enhanced learning. Good communication and teamwork skills. Detail-oriented with a focus on delivering high-quality learning content.

title: Technical Service Engineer (Chemical plant)
description: Our client is a leading chemical solutions provider, specializing in innovative products and services for the oil and gas industry. Due to their current expansion plan, they are looking for Technical Service Engineer to join their team, in hopes of developing the current high performing team. They are located at West. Job Responsibilities: Technical Monitoring & Troubleshooting: Monitor and analyse chemical programs using plant data and laboratory results, conduct troubleshooting, and prepare incident and findings reports to ensure stable operations. Client Support & Engagement: Respond promptly to client queries and concerns, propose value-added initiatives and improvement plans, and maintain strong client relationships to ensure satisfaction. Operational Excellence & Safety: Drive reliability, safety, and operational excellence by coordinating with site technicians, ensuring HSSE compliance, and supporting housekeeping and on-site activities. Cross-Functional Collaboration: Share technical findings with internal teams, coordinate on new programs, and support KPI achievement through close collaboration with technical stakeholders. Business & Operational Planning: Identify new business opportunities, support report preparation, manage inventory and delivery planning, and ensure timely submission of customer monitoring reports. Requirements: Min Diploma in Chemical Engineering in Oil and Gas related field with 3 years of experience Possess knowledge of Chemical Engineering Principles and operations Experience working in Refinery, Ethylene plant will be an added advantage Interested candidates who wish to apply for the advertised position, please click APPLY NOW or email an updated copy of your resume/cv. We regret that only shortlisted candidate will be notified. Email: jobs@recruitpedia.sg Recruitpedia Pte. Ltd. EA License No. 19C9682 EA Reg. No. R23115141 (Tan Su Yee)

title: Chief Technology Officer
description:
932 Design Group is looking a hands-on Chief Technology Officer (CTO) to lead our technology vision and build the foundation for scalable growth. This role is ideal for a technology leader who thrives in a fast-paced, highly regulated fintech environment, balancing strategic leadership with deep technical execution. The CTO will lead the development of secure, high-performance, and scalable platforms, ensuring technology decisions align closely with business objectives, regulatory requirements, and customer needs. Responsibilities Technology Strategy, Leadership & Execution Define and execute the company’s long-term technology and IT strategy aligned with business goals. Advise executive leadership on technology trends, risks, and opportunities. Lead digital transformation initiatives to drive innovation and competitive advantage. Establish technology governance, standards, and best practices across the organization. Lead and assemble a diverse, high-performing team of technologists/ engineers/ innovators to develop AI-driven initiatives across the organization. Product Development, Architecture & Platforms Oversee the design and delivery of secure, scalable, and high-performance platforms. Ensure system reliability, scalability, and performance while meeting business and customer requirements. Optimize application architecture, system integrations, and development processes to support rapid growth. Security, Risk Management & Compliance Own the cybersecurity and technology risk management strategy. Safeguard digital transactions, sensitive data, and mission-critical systems. Ensure compliance with financial regulations, data protection laws, and industry standards. Lead audits, regulatory reviews, disaster recovery planning, and business continuity initiatives. IT Infrastructure, Operations & DevOps Oversee cloud infrastructure, networks, and enterprise systems to ensure high availability and resilience. Drive DevOps adoption, automation, and continuous improvement to enhance efficiency and reliability. Manage IT budgets, resources, and operational performance. Team Leadership, Collaboration & Vendor Management Build, mentor, and lead high-performing engineering and IT teams. Foster a culture of innovation, accountability, and continuous improvement. Collaborate closely with business leaders, product teams, and external stakeholders. Manage third-party vendors and partners to ensure quality, performance, and value. Innovation & Technology Enablement Evaluate and adopt emerging technologies such as cloud, AI, automation, and data platforms. Enable data-driven decision-making and support customer-facing product innovation. Ensure technology solutions remain modern, flexible, and future-ready. Champion creativity and experimentation in the design and development of AI-powered solutions,
automation, and intelligent systems that enhance operational efficiency, unlock insights, and support business growth. Translate business challenges into practical, scalable AI solutions while ensuring ethical use, security, and regulatory compliance. Qualifications Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in computer science, information technology, engineering, or a related field. Proven experience as a CTO or senior technology leadership role. Strong background in building and scaling secure, cloud-based platforms. Extensive understanding of software development, IT infrastructure and cybersecurity best practices. Strong strategic thinking, problem-solving skills, communication, and stakeholder management skills. Hands-on knowledge of AI-driven creative workflows, including generative design, BIM, advanced visualization technologies, and other AI applications, applied to interior design, architecture industry. Please apply by sending in your CV and cover letter to samantha.koh@932designgroup.com.

title: Assistant Engineer - EV Chargers
description: Company Overview Power Automation is a wholly-owned subsidiary of SP Group, providing innovative and cutting-edge technologies that help to deliver Power System Control, Substation Automation, Sustainable Energy Solutions, and Protection Systems. Leading up to 1996, there was a marked increase in competition and decrease in operating margins of electric utilities, due to the deregulation and unbundling of energy production and transport processes in Singapore. Taking on the role as a systems integrator well-versed in utility automation solutions based on open-systems technologies while adhering strictly to industry standards, we produce quality products and services to our clients and customers. Job Summary You will perform regular inspections and preventive maintenance on EV chargers at various sites in Singapore, ensuring operational reliability and safety compliance while coordinating with stakeholders and maintaining accurate service records. Responsibilities Conduct regular inspections and perform preventive maintenance on EV chargers across multiple sites to ensure optimal functionality Identify, diagnose, and report faults found during maintenance to enable timely repairs Update firmware and software on charging stations to maintain up-to-date system performance Adhere strictly to safety regulations and industry standards during all maintenance activities to ensure safe operations Coordinate effectively with site owners, facility managers, and the customer's engineer/technical officer from the Tech Ops team to facilitate smooth maintenance processes Maintain detailed service logs and prepare reports for management to track maintenance activities and equipment status Required competencies and certifications Hold a minimum of Higher Nitec in Electrical Engineering Possess a valid Class 3 / 3A driver's license Preferred competencies and qualifications Certification as an Electric Vehicle Charger Equipment Specialist is an advantage

title: Principal Foundation Engineer
description: Job Summary Responsible to the project manager and the Structural Engineering Department, in charge of the major engineering design work of Abu Dhabi Engineering Team, including but not limited the engineering plan, interface management, calculation and analysis, drawings and the reports. Principal Responsibilities The job responsibilities include but are not limited to: Work scope and technical requirements identification; Take part in the technical clarification and prepare discipline proposals; Interface management of major Engineering discipline; Making plans for Engineering works according to the project requirements; Responsible for the major Engineering works according to the project requirements; Cooperate with other disciplines to finish the engineering works; Responsible for preparation of major Engineering discipline’s weekly, monthly reports and other tables/reports for Engineering project; Make sure all the major Engineering works are complied with Company’s QHSE requirements. Special responsibility for Experienced Structural Engineer: in charge of the Jacket and Topside Structure engineering design Special responsibility for Experienced Geotechnical & Foundation Engineer: in charge of the Geotechnical & Foundation engineering design Education/Experience Requirements/ Certification Bachelor degree or above; Major in Naval Architecture and Ocean Engineering or structural engineering; Familiar with the international standards for offshore structural engineering (API RP-2A, ISO 19901 etc.) Special requirement for Structural engineer: Familiar with structural engineering works of offshore oil platform; Using SACS, AutoCAD softwares to perform structural engineering works; Special requirement for Geotechnical and Foundation engineer: Familiar with Geotechnical and Foundation engineering works of offshore oil platform; Using GRLWeap, SACS, AutoCAD softwares to perform Geotechnical and Foundation engineering works. title: Technical Manager - WCAN
description: Responsibilities: Lead and coordinate techno-commercial activities independently to ensure smooth business operations, under department supervision. Manage technical requirements, troubleshooting, inventory audits, inspections, and client communications. Oversee projects and service tasks including proposal development and on-site activities. Develop and maintain client relationships to support business growth in after-sales, projects, and supervisory services. Available for frequent travel and site work across multiple locations. Support service teams in quote preparation, technical discussions, and coordination with equipment manufacturers and suppliers. Service Support Responsibilities: Lead after-sales operations involving clients, vendors, manufacturers, and other stakeholders. Manage commercial aspects within the service group to ensure smooth transaction flow. Support day-to-day service activities such as quoting, purchase order management, and technical-commercial negotiations. Facilitate technical coordination and troubleshooting between clients and manufacturers. Maintain technical documentation, maintenance records, and spare parts inventories. Prepare technical and commercial proposals for projects. Collaborate with project teams on planning and execution. Foster communication and relationships with clients, procurement, and other key contacts. Conduct site visits for maintenance shutdowns or technical discussions. Business Development Duties: Lead business promotion through marketing initiatives and client engagement. Organize sales efforts including client meetings, service presentations, and follow-ups. Identify new business opportunities and develop service offerings, obtaining necessary approvals. Handle commercial functions such as billing, invoicing, contract negotiation, taxation, insurance, and compliance. Assist in preparing periodic reports on technical services for management review. Provide technical training and knowledge sharing within the team. Qualifications: Bachelor's degree in Mechanical Engineering or related field 12 to 17 years of comprehensive industry experience 8 to 10 years focused on maintenance within chemical processing facilities Minimum 5 years of specialized experience in the design and management of rotary equipment Extensive expertise in maintenance operations, including spare parts management, shutdown coordination, troubleshooting, equipment design, and vendor liaison related to rotary machinery Well-versed in reliability-centered maintenance principles, shutdown planning, and advanced troubleshooting techniques Fundamental knowledge of chemical plant processes and construction practices Proven track record in leading maintenance teams and managing projects effectively Experience working in diverse, multinational work environments Willingness to travel internationally and conduct regular site inspections Excellent communication skills with a professional and approachable attitude We regret only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. All applications will be handled confidentially. By submitting your application, you agree to the collection, use, retention, and disclosure of your personal information to prospective employers. title: Senior Interior Designer/Design Manager
description: Key Responsibilities: 1.      Design Direction and Presentation: a.      Provide design guidance, creative direction, and innovative proposal to meet client requirements. b.      Present and effectively communicate design intent during client pitches and project discussions. 2.      Client Management: a.      Efficiently handle multiple projects concurrently, overseeing them from start to finish, design, and executionphases. b. Ensure that projects are completed on time and meet client expectations. 3.      Design Implementation: a. Conceptual design for pitching projects, including mood board, rendering. b.      Translate design concepts into detailed plans, ensuring that aesthetic intent aligns with technical feasibility. c.      Collaborate with design principal, and clients, qualified Persons and vendors to refine and finalize design details. d.      Collaborate with MEP engineers to ensure the seamless integration of mechanical, electrical, and plumbing systems within theinterior design. e.      Produce comprehensive construction drawings, including floor plans, elevations, sections, and details, with a focus on precision and clarity. 4.      Building Management Submissions: a.      Prepare and submit drawing package required for approvals from building management. b.      Coordinate with QP for submission to relevant authorities. c.      Manage the submission process, tracking progress, and ensuring timely approvals. 5.      Project Design Coordination: a.      Coordinate with project teams to integrate architectural, structural, MEP, and interior design elements seamlessly. b.      Coordinate with procurement team to select feasible materials for design implementation. c.      Collaborate with Project Leaders from project initiation to completion, offering design expertise and insights. d.      Attend adhoc meetings related to design and project matters, including client meetings, project meetings, and site visits. e.      Monitor the implementation of design intent on-site and provide guidance or recommend design changes as necessary. 6. As-Built Submissions: a.      Ensure that all design changes and solutions are seamlessly integrated into project documentation. b.      Oversee the creation of accurate as-built drawings, reflecting the final state of the project post-construction. c.      Ensure that as-built submissions are complete and comply with relevant industry standards. Requirements: 1.      A recognized Degree/Diploma in Interior Design or an equivalent qualification. 2.      Proficiency in a range of design-related software, including Hand Sketch, Sketchup, AutoCAD, and similar tools. 3.      A minimum of 5 years of relevant experience in commercial design, showcasing strong technical knowledge in areas such as shop drawings, site surveys, and detail drawings. 4.      Proven experience in managing projects from  start to finish, encompassing sales, design, and execution. 5.     Exceptional flair for creative design and a meticulous eye for design details. 6.      Highly proactive with outstanding coordination and execution skills.

title: Network Technical Support Specialist
description: Responsibilities: Deliver technical solutions to clients and take responsibility for the standards of work, project sign off and client satisfaction. Provide client support, assisting with troubleshooting and design, configuration and escalated problem management. Act as key project team members charged with the analysis of client and technical requirements, specifications development, solution design and development, and in some environments may consult on testing and deployment processes. Expected to be technology leads for specific technologies and to use their deep knowledge of these technologies to propose enhancements. Education: Degree in Computer Engineering, Computer Science, Information Technology or related field CCIE, CCNP, CCNA F5, or other relevant Requirements: At least 5 – 10 years' professional technical experience related to software development, systems design and deployment, or networking and infrastructure within a large scale (preferably multi- national) technology services environment Experience in designing and building complex LAN and large WAN Expertise in routing/switching technology, including BGP, OSPF, etc. Expertise in designing and building complex data center network with Software Defined Networking (SDN) solutions such as Cisco Catalyst Center, Cisco ACI,   EVPN or any OpenFlow protocol. Experience in designing and building complex WAN with Software Defined WAN solutions such as Viptela, SilverPeak and Riverbed. Experience in designing and building complex campus network with Software Defined Access (SDA) solutions from Cisco or any other SDA vendors. Experience in network automation via scripting (e.g. Python, Ruby) or tools (e.g. Ansible, Algosec, Chef) Experience in designing and building network security (Checkpoint, Palo Alto, Fortigate, Cisco firewall, IDS/IPS, VPN, SSL, Cisco ISE). Experience in designing and building complex LAN and large WAN in F5 Context, LTM and GTM Experience in project management methodologies Singaporean/PR preferred

title: Assistant Manager - Engineering Services (Development & Estate Department) [NIE]
description:
ABOUT THE NATIONAL INSTITUTE OF EDUCATION (NIE) The National Institute of Education (NIE), Singapore , is Singapore’s national teacher education institute and we are proud to be an integral part of the nation’s education service. We play a key role in the preparation of teachers and in the provision of teacher professional and school leadership development programmes. We are committed to our vision of being An Institute of Distinction: Leading the Future of Education and our mission to Inspire Learning, Transform Teaching and Advance Research . Read more about NIE here . NIE invites suitable applications for the position of Assistant Manager (Engineering Services – Electrical/M&E) to assist Head of the Development & Estate Department in the facility management, building systems planning, infrastructure development and improvement and addition & alteration (A&A) works. Responsibilities: Manage the Electrical system (High Tension and Low Tension system) of NIE campus. Ensure the reliability of the electrical system in NIE. Manage the Extra Low Voltage (ELV) systems of NIE campus. Ensure the reliability of the ELV systems in NIE. Oversee electricity metering and billing and Power Monitoring System. Analyse electricity consumption of NIE and implement measures to reduce the energy consumption. Manage assigned M&E systems, ensuring the reliability of the system in NIE. Assist in environmental sustainability and energy conservation projects/initiatives. Manage the procurement and administration of contracts for maintenance and project works. Prepare specifications and tender/quotation documentations and call quotation/tender. Manage and supervise term contractors to ensure the smooth operations of NIE campus and facilities. Manage and oversee the maintenance regime of the systems under his/her care at NIE which includes routine and audit checks, monitoring and ensuring maintenance and repair works are done in a timely manner, managing the performance of the contractors and proper supervision of contractors' works. Manage and oversee the infrastructure development projects, Addition & Alteration (A&A) works, repair and redecoration (R&R) and improvement works; ensuring projects are completed on time and within budget
with acceptable quality. Develop, implement, review and maintain regime and review Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) regularly and ensure the systems and projects under his/her care are in full compliance with the regulatory requirements and safety requirements. Any other duties as assigned. Requirements: A University Degree in Electrical Engineering or Building Services or Building Service with at least 3 years of relevant working experience in operation and maintenance of Electrical system and/or in installation/project
management of Electrical system. Strong technical knowledge in Electrical systems and good knowledge in building services design, installation and maintenance. Well versed in relevant authority/regulatory codes of practices for Electrical. Candidates with experience in the facilities management and operation of Institutes of Higher Learning campus or hospital campus or large-scale developments will have an advantage. Relevant work experience in the following areas will be an advantage: - Strong knowledge and understanding of building construction, A&A works, feasibility studies, budget estimates, and project management processes and planning. - Strong knowledge and understanding of Mechanical & Electrical (M&E) systems such as ACMV, Fire Protection, etc. Strong team-player with good interpersonal, communication and leadership skills. Self-starter with strong sense of initiative, with ability to work with ambiguity and agility. Good analytical and problem-solving skills. Good project management skills. Closing Date The closing date of the advertisement is 20 February 2026. We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Other Information NIE staff can take chartered buses at their own expense from or near their homes to the NIE campus. This is subject to availability of seats.

title: Site Engineer
description: Company Overview / Employee Value Proposition A Construction Civil Engineering organization established in 2013 by four established local A1 main contractors in Singapore. The company is currently engaged in civil LTA projects. It fosters a teamwork environment with a continuous learning mindset. Job Summary Join a leading main contractor for LTA projects as a Civil Engineer, driving site execution excellence through proactive project monitoring, technical oversight, and stakeholder coordination to deliver quality infrastructure within schedule and budget. Responsibilities Assist Project Engineers on site to support project delivery and resolve technical issues promptly Monitor site progress and schedule to ensure timely completion of project milestones Manage site set out, leveling, and surveying activities to maintain construction accuracy Review and verify plans, drawings, and quantity calculations to ensure compliance with design specifications Ensure all materials and workmanship conform to project specifications and budget constraints Supervise project crews and subcontractors to maintain productivity and quality standards Coordinate effectively with authorities, clients, and Qualified Persons (QP) to facilitate approvals and compliance Enforce safety, health, and security protocols within the assigned work area to maintain a safe working environment Adapt to and perform efficiently in a fast-paced construction environment Required competencies and certifications Degree in Civil Engineering recognized by the Professional Engineers Board Minimum 2-3 years of civil engineering experience Preferred competencies and qualifications Ability to work effectively under fast-paced and dynamic site conditions

title: Internship Interior Designer
description: Launch Your Interior Design Career: A Career-Focused Internship About Us: At HG Works Pte Ltd, we are passionate about crafting inspiring and functional spaces. As a dynamic interior design firm, we believe in nurturing the next generation of talent. When you join our collaborative team, you’re not just completing tasks; you’re building the foundation for your professional future. Your Career Opportunity: This full-time internship is designed as a launchpad for your design career. Go beyond academic theory and immerse yourself in the complete lifecycle of live projects. Under the direct mentorship of our senior designers, you will develop the practical skills and professional mindset essential for success in the industry. The internship begins with a 3-month introductory period to ensure a valuable mutual fit, with the opportunity to extend for deeper, long-term career development. Build Your Professional Skills & Portfolio: In this role, you will actively contribute while building critical career assets: Develop Design Solutions: Assist in creating tangible design concepts, including space planning, material palettes, and colour schemes that solve real client needs. Master Project Coordination: Gain hands-on experience supporting projects from concept to installation, learning to liaise effectively with clients, suppliers, and contractors. Enhance Client Communication: Participate in client meetings and contribute to presentation materials, building confidence and professional communication skills. Execute Collaborative Workflow: Collaborate with our internal team to see how design intent is translated into actionable project plans. Strengthen Industry Knowledge: Conduct research on trends, materials, and innovations, keeping your design thinking current and informed. title: Design Development Manager - Singapore
description: The Role We are seeking a Design Development Manager to grow our presence amongst Specifiers, Designers & Architects in the luxury premium space in Singapore. Fisher & Paykel has been designing products since 1934 and has grown into a global company operating in 50 countries and manufacturing in Mexico, Italy, Thailand, and New Zealand. Our design heritage is founded on a pioneering spirit and a culture of curiosity that has challenged conventional appliance design. Our goal is to be the world's number one premium appliance brand. Not only does this mean we design and build the best appliances, but that we understand who our key customers are and engage with them according to their very specific needs. The Specifier, Designer & Architect space is essential to our strategy and requires a very special skill set to develop and leverage the key relationships required. From identifying key clients, through networking and engaging with them, it's vital to our success that we've got the right person on the job. As our Design Development Manager, you will be at the forefront of cultivating relationships with top-tier specifiers, designers, and architects. Your goal will be to create strategic plans and deliver initiatives that increase product market share and achieve profitable growth. At the heart of Fisher & Paykel are our people who design innovative technologies that elevate the everyday into something out of the ordinary. We have built our business by creating moments of delight for the people who use our products. The Design Development Manager here at Fisher & Paykel Singapore is focused on creating long-lasting relationships with top-tier specifiers, designers, and architects within the country and will report directly to the SG Head of Marketing. In this role, you will work closely with specifiers and influence them to incorporate our brands into their designs. You will be a brand ambassador, promoting our portfolio of products, engaging in design discussions, facilitating product trainings, and networking within the designer/architect community. This will, in turn, drive sales through both our Builder and Retail networks. This is an individual contributor role for some who enjoys owning things end-to-end, working close to the business, and operating in a fast-moving environment. In The Role, You Will Be the subject matter expert for Fisher & Paykel within the SDA community by being curious and deeply understanding key specifier communities and their environmental influences. Initiate, develop, and attend key designer networking events within our Fisher & Paykel Singapore Showroom to strengthen existing relationships and create new opportunities for Fisher & Paykel brand, products, and services. Ensure a seamless, brand-aligned representation of Fisher & Paykel’s value proposition across all touchpoints of the key specifier community. Plan and execute the local design development strategy with key specifiers, ensuring alignment with global imperatives and growth aspirations. Provide regular reporting updates on key specifiers to the local and global Market Group. Deliver product training that is sufficiently technical but more tailored to business benefit and competitive advantage. Manage third-party agency partnerships and formulate performance and metric scorecards. Ensure the accuracy and completeness of our brand and product messaging to key specifiers. Act as a liaison for design requirements across market channels You Have Minimum of 5 years’ experience in the appliance or building industry, in a design, project management, or sales/marketing role. Marketing, Business, or Interior Design preferred in conjunction with relevant design credentials (AKBD, CKD, Leed AP). Working experience with interior/kitchen designers and architects. Proven experience in successfully developing strategic partnerships both externally and internally to deliver quality results. Strong creative & technical design skills, including the ability to read floor plans and elevations, and make recommendations on appliance selection, placement, and configuration based on spatial and proportional characteristics of the kitchen, as well as design trends and principles. Strong project planning and management experience. Excellent communication skills – verbal and written communication skills are clear and concise. Strong team player Fisher & Paykel is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, religion, national or ethnic origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, disability, or other characteristics protected by law.

title: Engineering Lead, Issuing Platform
description: About the Team The issuing engineering team at Airwallex builds and operates core systems for card issuing, transaction processing, and settlement at global scale. The team owns mission-critical platforms that underpin Airwallex’s issuing products, with a strong focus on reliability, scalability, and compliance. Key areas of ownership include: Cards: Customer-facing and internal systems for card lifecycle management, controls, and configuration. Transactions: A global, high-throughput transaction processing platform designed for low latency, high availability, and resiliency. Settlement: Clearance, settlement, and dispute workflows that ensure financial accuracy and scheme compliance. What you’ll do As an Engineering Lead, Issuing Platform , you will lead one or more teams responsible for building and operating critical components of the issuing platform. This role combines people leadership, technical direction, and delivery ownership. You will have direct reports and are accountable for team health, execution, and long-term technical quality. While you remain technically credible and hands-on at an architectural level, your primary focus is enabling teams to do their best work rather than being the primary individual contributor. This role is based in Singapore. Responsibilities: People leadership and team development Manage, coach, and grow a team of engineers through regular feedback, performance management, and career development. Build a strong engineering culture focused on ownership, quality, and continuous improvement. Support hiring and onboarding to scale the team sustainably. Technical leadership and delivery Provide technical direction and architectural guidance for high-impact transaction processing systems. Partner with senior engineers and staff engineers to design scalable, resilient solutions. Ensure high standards for system design, code quality, testing, and operational excellence. Platform and infrastructure ownership Guide the evolution of application and platform infrastructure to reduce operational overhead and improve developer productivity. Drive improvements across reliability, observability, security, and cost efficiency. Strategic collaboration Work closely with product, operations, and other engineering leaders to align execution with business priorities. Evaluate new technologies and patterns, and make pragmatic decisions that balance innovation with stability. Who you are We are looking for senior engineers with deep technical expertise and a strong track record of impact as a tech lead. Minimum qualifications: 7+ years of professional software engineering experience, with at least 1+ years in a people leadership role managing engineers directly. Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field. Strong background in modern backend development using languages such as Java, Kotlin, Go, or Python. Proven experience designing and delivering large-scale, distributed, production systems. Solid understanding of computer science fundamentals and object-oriented design principles. Hands-on experience with event-driven and streaming architectures, for example Kafka. Experience designing and operating RESTful APIs at scale. Cloud experience with GCP (preferred) or AWS, including containerized workloads using Docker and Kubernetes. Familiarity with observability and operational tooling such as Splunk, Grafana, and Prometheus. Demonstrated ability to mentor engineers and lead the execution of technical and delivery strategy. Preferred qualifications: Experience in fintech, payments, or other regulated financial domains. Deep experience with JVM-based ecosystems, including Java or Kotlin with Spring or Spring Boot. Prior experience leading teams responsible for high-availability, low-latency systems.

title: R&D Senior Business Analyst
description: As part of the Thermo Fisher Scientific team, you’ll discover meaningful work that makes a positive impact on a global scale. Join our colleagues in bringing our Mission to life every single day to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. We provide our global teams with the resources needed to achieve individual career goals while helping to take science a step beyond by developing solutions for some of the world’s toughest challenges, like protecting the environment, making sure our food is safe or helping find cures for cancer. The Sr. Business Analyst role will be responsible primarily for preparing product requirements and specifications, participating software hazard analysis activities, contributing to user interface design, and improving the user experience of our software products. The Sr. Business Analyst will also serve as a customer facing resource in a technical capacity to determine workflow configurations for our instrumentation. The role will conduct requirements workshops with team members and meet with individuals of various key partners: Product Managers, SW Leads, R&D, FAS, Marketing, UX, etc.) and product development teams to capture and detail measurable, testable, and traceable requirements and specifications. Responsibilities will also include interviewing customers for feedback, conducting software usability testing, and contributing to UI/UX design activities. The candidate’s experience with QMS will provide design compliance input to the software engineering teams, including, but not limited to authoring Software Requirements and Specifications; and, reviewing, and approving software-related documents, such as Risk and Hazard Analysis, Design Verification, and Validation Plans. The candidate will provide support to other business units such as service/support team to assist in preparing training materials of the new workflow and technical writer team to review customer-facing documents (e.g. user guide, white paper, help).  The candidate will also contribute to process improvement. Attention to technical detail, proven written and verbal communication skills, and the ability to analyze and navigate technical problem domains are required. Responsibilities: Prepare well-written technical requirements, functional specifications, and diagrams (e.g. UML, data flow, etc) for software and life sciences instrument systems, including detailing workflows and error-handling scenarios. Apply standard process methodologies in developing and maintaining requirements, including user stories, use cases, functional requirements, non-functional and performance requirements, and business rules. Facilitate requirements workshops with cross-functional science, engineering, and product management teams to elicit and review technical requirements and risk controls for new and sustaining software products. Manage requirements, prepare analysis models and diagrams, and generate and generate reports using tools (e.g. JAMA, JIRA, HPALM, Visio and Miro or similar). Collaborate with the test team to ensure requirements and specifications are concise, accurate, measurable, and testable. Establish traceability between customer requirements, product requirements, and specifications. Prepare trace matrix reports. Participate in customer research and interview activities to elicit user needs for product development. Contribute to user interface design and usability testing activities to provide a seamless user experience in navigating complex scientific laboratory workflows. Collaborate with UX engineers and UI designers to prepare mockups and prototype user interfaces for complex life-science domain software that meets the needs of our customers. Prepare and contribute to design compliance documentation and best-practice disciplines across all aspects of software development within the SDLC. Requirements: Bachelor’s degree in computer science or equivalent. Minimum 5 years of experience with software development best practices, such as Agile or SCRUM. 8+ years industry experience in a systems/business analyst role. Proficient in the use of business analysis tools and techniques. (e.g., Jira, Miro Board, Visio) Understanding of computer systems, networking of Windows and Linux-based systems. Possess strong written and verbal communication, presentation, and facilitation skills. Comfortable working as part of a distributed, global, and cross-functional team. Prepared to work to aggressive timelines in an iterative software development environment. Highly Preferred: Experience in Life Sciences and genomics industries and/or DNA technology Certification in Business Analysis (e.g., Certified Product Owner, Certified Scrum Master)

title: Engineer II, Senior (Technology)
description: The AI/Robotics Engineer will lead the lifecycle management, from development to production deployment of advanced AI and autonomous applications to address key challenges in the maritime and offshore industries. This role seamlessly integrates the development of sophisticated robotics perception models with theoperationalization of these solutions within an Azure Cloud environment using Agile methodologies. You will be part of the ABS Singapore Innovation and Research Center, working within cross-functional squads to deliver iterative value. You are expected to possess a blend of software engineering rigor, cloud architecture knowledge, and practical AI expertise to ensure our solutions are scalable, secure, and production ready. What You Will Do: Robotics Perception & Computer Vision Sensor Fusion & SLAM: Develop and implement robust sensor fusion algorithms, specifically combining Camera and LiDAR data to create accurate 3D representations and enable Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM) in complex environments. Advanced Detection Capabilities : Lead the development of computer vision models for automated inspection, focusing on crack detection, pattern recognition, and anomaly detection on structural surfaces. Image Recognition : Engineer high-precision image recognition pipelines to identify key structural features and potential defects. Automation: Automate the interpretation of sensor data to reduce human intervention in inspection workflows. AI Model Development & Cloud Deployment Model Lifecycle Management : Develop, fine-tune, and integrate AI models for operational and safety-critical use cases, specifically targeting computer vision applications. Cloud Architecture : Architect, deploy, and manage scalable AI solutions on Microsoft Azure, utilizing services such as Azure Machine Learning, Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS), and Azure Functions. Production Deployment : Transition prototype models into production-grade applications, optimizing code for latency, throughput, and cost-efficiency. MLOps Implementation : Design and maintain robust MLOps pipelines for model training, versioning,evaluation, and monitoring in production. Agile Development & Collaboration Agile Framework: Work within an Agile/Scrum framework, participating in sprint planning, daily     stand-ups, and retrospectives to ensure timely delivery of AI features. CI/CD Implementation : Adopt a DevOps mindset, ensuring continuous integration and continuous deployment (CI/CD) practices are applied to machine learning and robotics workflows. Stakeholder Collaboration : Collaborate closely with Project Managers to translate business requirements into technical specifications and provide support to ensure smooth adoption of tools What You Will Need: Education and Experience Minimum Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Robotics, Engineering, or a related field. A Master’s or PhD is preferred. Minimum 2 years of experience in AI/ML development and deployment, preferably in a relevant field. Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities Robotics & Vision: Strong grasp of sensor fusion (Camera + LiDAR), SLAM, and experience in crack or pattern detection algorithms. Cloud Ecosystem: Hands-on experience with the Azure ecosystem (e.g., Azure MachineLearning, Blob Storage, Azure DevOps, Azure OpenAI Service). Framework Proficiency: Proficiency in machine learning frameworks such as TensorFlow or PyTorch. Scalable Deployment: Experience with distributed systems, cloud computing, and containerization tools (e.g., Kubernetes, Docker) for scalable AI deployment. API Integration: Proven experience in developing and fine-tuning AI models and integrating them via APIs. Soft Skills: Exceptional problem-solving skills, self-motivation, and strong communication skills to work effectively with non-technical stakeholders. title: Low‑Code Developer (Mendix)
description: Mendix Low‑Code Engineer — Build Enterprise‑Scale Impact at Kyndryl If you're looking for a role where your low‑code expertise genuinely shapes the way large organizations operate, this is the place for you. At Kyndryl, you won’t just build small apps—you’ll design enterprise‑grade Mendix solutions that thousands of users rely on daily. You’ll join a global engineering culture where your ideas matter, your skills are trusted, and your work directly improves how businesses run. Most companies use low‑code for basic prototypes or internal utilities. At Kyndryl, you’ll use Mendix to modernize mission‑critical systems and transform complex processes at scale. You’ll own the full solution lifecycle, work with enterprise architects, and influence how entire organizations adopt digital transformation. What You’ll Do You’ll play a key role in designing intelligent, scalable Mendix applications that streamline operations and deliver real business outcomes. Your work will be hands‑on, strategic, and highly visible—giving you the autonomy to build solutions that truly make an impact. 1) Design, Build & Deploy Enterprise Mendix Applications You’ll create end‑to‑end applications using Mendix Studio Pro—including domain models, microflows, UI pages, reusable components, and secure user roles. You’ll build applications that reduce manual work, improve decision‑making, and solve real operational challenges. 2) Streamline & Automate Business Processes You’ll use Mendix workflows, logic flows, rules, and automation capabilities to eliminate inefficiencies. You’ll turn manual steps into intelligent workflows, integrate data across systems, and ensure everything runs smoothly with proper validation and governance. 3) Integrate Systems & Build Scalable Data Models You’ll design clean, scalable data models and connect Mendix apps with enterprise systems—via REST/SOAP APIs, Databases, Microsoft services, or ERP/CRM platforms. Your integrations will form the backbone of seamless cross‑system operations. Why You’ll Love Working at Kyndryl You’ll build for the enterprise, not just for a department. Your applications will support real business operations—governments, banks, global supply chains, healthcare institutions, and more. You get ownership. Real ownership. You’ll influence solution design, architecture decisions, and integration patterns—not just “configure forms” or “drag and drop screens.” You grow into the next version of yourself. Kyndryl invests heavily in talent development. As you expand your Mendix capability, you’ll have clear paths toward roles such as: Low‑Code Solution Architect Technical Lead (Mendix) Enterprise Integration Engineer Platform Owner / Low‑Code Governance Lead Full Stack or Hybrid Developer (if that’s a path you want) Here, you’re not limited to low‑code—you can grow your engineering, cloud, architecture, and leadership career in whichever direction you choose. Required Skills & Experience At least 4 years of hands‑on Mendix or low‑code development experience (Mendix Studio Pro strongly preferred). Experience working with Git and Agile teams (Scrum, CI, pair programming). Knowledge of HTML, CSS, JavaScript, SQL/NoSQL databases. Experience integrating apps with REST/SOAP APIs and enterprise systems. Strong analytical and problem‑solving skills, plus the ability to collaborate with both technical and non‑technical stakeholders. High attention to detail and commitment to delivering quality applications. Preferred Skills & Experience Mendix certifications (Rapid, Intermediate, Advanced, Expert). Experience with CI/CD pipelines or DevOps practices. Exposure to mobile development in Mendix. Familiarity with other low‑code platforms (OutSystems, Power Apps, Appian, ServiceNow, Dynamics 365, SharePoint Online). Experience managing platform‑level features such as rules engines, workflows, access roles, validations, UI flows, and reusable modules. At Kyndryl, you’ll be part of a global engineering powerhouse where low‑code isn’t an afterthought—it’s a strategic capability. You’ll design impactful applications, solve meaningful problems, and grow into a technical leader who shapes how enterprises operate. If you're ready to build solutions that matter, this is where your next chapter begins.

Working knowledge of construction methods, materials, and regulations relevant to the industry. description: Job Responsibilities Responsible for the management of project Quality Assurance/Quality Control (QA/QC) documents, including formulating and maintaining the Project Quality Plan (PQP) to ensure it is in line with client standards and international standards; developing Inspection and Test Plans (ITPs) covering fields such as Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning (HVAC), piping (utility and process), and equipment installation; managing documents like work method statements (involving the fit-up of various pipe joints, such as in scenarios of underground pipes, welding, flushing, equipment installation, etc.), Welding Procedure Specifications (WPS)/Procedure Qualification Records (PQR)/welder qualifications (in line with the requirements for piping and structural works in ASME Section IX), as well as calibration certificates for torque wrenches, gauges, particle counters, airflow meters, etc. Implement Non-Conformance Report (NCR)/Corrective Action Report (CAR) procedures, which should include root cause analysis and corrective actions when handling non-conformities. Conduct material control work, carry out receiving inspections of materials, verify whether materials conform to the Approved Vendor List (AVL), and perform receiving inspections for each batch of incoming materials. Responsible for QA/QC work in the fabrication & assembly phase, including welding inspection of carbon steel pipes/stainless steel pipes: Pre-welding inspection: Review Welding Procedure Specifications (WPS) and Procedure Qualification Records (PQR) to ensure they are approved for carbon steel pipes and comply with ASME Section IX and project specifications; verify welders' qualifications in terms of welding processes (SMAW, GTAW, GMAW, FCAW) and positions; confirm that the material grade, diameter, thickness, and heat number of pipes, fittings, and flanges are consistent with drawings and Material Test Certificates (MTC); check whether the type of electrodes/filler materials is approved, storage is appropriate (baking, holding ovens), and batch/lot traceability is maintained; inspect whether the fit-up and joint preparation meet the bevel angle, root face, and root gap specified in the WPS, whether the alignment is within tolerance, and whether tack welds are qualified (no cracks, correct size). Welding inspection: Pay attention to orbital welding (parameter documents, welding videos/data logs) and welding coupon testing for critical pipes; conduct Non-Destructive Testing (RT/UT/PT) and visual inspection according to the importance of the system; for stainless steel pipes, verify surface quality and chemical cleaning records (passivation and pickling). Responsible for QA/QC work in the installation phase: Equipment installation: Check the precision alignment, leveling, and vibration isolation of pumps, chillers, compressors, etc. HVAC and cleanroom systems: Conduct duct leak testing in accordance with SMACNA/ASHRAE standards. Piping installation: Inspect slopes, supports, anchors, and spring hangers; check gaskets, flange torque, and tightness; supervise the implementation of clean construction protocols such as nitrogen purging during welding. Responsible for testing and pre-commissioning work, create test boundaries (test packs) for each construction system; conduct hydrostatic tests/pneumatic tests in accordance with ASME B31.3 (process utility piping); supervise third parties in carrying out airflow balance testing (TAB - Testing, Adjusting & Balancing) in HVAC testing; perform system flushing/cleaning until turbidity and particle counts meet the acceptance criteria. Responsible for handover and document management work, organize as-built documents (red-line drawings and updated P&IDs), weld logs and weld maps (for piping), material traceability records (if required), final test reports (hydrostatic test, leak test, airflow), mechanical completion packages (punch list cleared, signed off by the client), and system turnover dossiers (organized by subsystem for the commissioning team). Job Requirements Familiar with relevant international standards such as ASME and client standards, and understand the processes and methods of engineering quality control. Have more than 3 years of relevant engineering QA/QC work experience, with experience in chemical, construction, and other projects being preferred. Hold relevant professional qualification certificates, such as welding inspector certificates.CSWIP 3.1, CSWIP 3.2 , AWS OR CWI, Possess good communication skills, strong sense of responsibility, be rigorous and meticulous, and able to work under certain pressure.

title: Control Room Operator | FPSO | 2 Years
description: Job Description: Responsible for the efficient and effective control of the process plant operation and production-related equipment for the assigned unit, in compliance with MODEC procedures, national and international regulations and standards, thus ensuring the unit's availability, safe operation and the production and revenue target achievement, contributing to the business continuity and its growth. Overall Job Purpose Responsible for the efficient and effective control of the process plant operation and production-related equipment for the assigned unit, in compliance with MODEC procedures, national and international regulations and standards, thus ensuring the unit's availability, safe operation and the production and revenue target achievement, contributing to the business continuity and its growth. Core Responsibilities Control and carry out process plant operation and production-related equipment via FCS and other control panels, under the direction of Production Supervisor and with the support from Operators' team, including operational routines, monitoring, controlling and operational optimization of the process plant, sampling and chemical analyses, start/stop and isolations of equipment, commissioning/decommissioning and operational alignments/maneuvers, in order to ensure that the production systems of the platform are operated according to the project and its operating philosophy, thus optimizing production performance and chemical consumption. Contribute to the unit's annual budget planning (expenses and investments), by participating of the corporate planning, providing information about resources, replacements, services and materials needs and other expenses used in its activities, liaising with Supervisors and Technicians to ensure accurate information. Contribute to an effective response to emergencies on board by being part of the Emergency Response Team, participating in all trainings, simulations and acting properly in a real situation, in accordance with MODEC's procedures, guidelines, as well as national and international regulations and best practices. Support the offshore and onshore leaderships in the interface and contact with the client, authorities and internal stakeholders., by providing faithful information regarding operation's and control room routine and indicators, when requested by Production Supervisor. Contribute to the MODEC’s Permit to Work management systems' implementation, by acting as Permit user of PTW for the task performed, in accordance with MODEC's procedures. Contribute to the continuous improvement of fleet performance by participating on, when requested, RCAs, investigations of NOIs/NODs, HAZID/HAZOPs, following up or acting upon CAPAs, sharing lessons learned and promoting a culture of continuous seek for performance and integrity enhancement opportunities. Act proactively to safeguarding MODEC from noncompliance to legal/regulatory requirements by following MODEC's procedures, management systems, client guidelines, internal and external regulations, national and international standards, participating in audits and inspections in the unit, developing and ensuring the implementation of action plans when required. Guarantee the proper use of Technical Support departments, by ensuring that technical queries are properly identified, recorded in the system, and always directed to Onshore departments for review/study, promoting a culture adherent to the company's systems. Contribute to enhance MODEC's procedures, by constantly evaluating the department activities and seeking improvement opportunities, sharing lessons learned with Technical Support departments and Operations leadership, participating in procedures' review, following MODEC's Philosophy, manufacturer recommendation, and personnel experience. Ensure the proper communication regarding the department activities developed onboard, by assisting in the preparation of the OIM Daily Report, as well as periodic and on demand reports for the client, authorities, or leadership. Communicate to the Production Supt all production or safety systems' emergency, by providing accurate and relevant information immediately, request technical advice as necessary and prepare, follow up and close out any necessary vendor assistance on board for the Production department, in liaison with relevant leaderships. Contribute to the operational performance by inhibiting and overriding controller, when required, in accordance with MODEC's procedures, and properly registering all occurrences in the production and safety / emergency systems. Assume the responsibilities of the Production Supervisor whenever designated by the OIM or Production Superintendent, complying with all training, procedures and responsibilities relevant to the function. Contribute with the quality of Production works performed by core crew or vendors, by providing them with adequate and suitable information, as directed by the Production Supervisor. Contribute to the proper response to emergencies and shutdowns, understanding and applying PSD, ESD and C&E (cause & effects diagram) diagrams. HSSE Requirements and Code of Conduct HSSE: Act in compliance with the corporate HSSE Management System Policy, the MODEC Life Saving Rules, and the policies, guides, process standards and work instructions available in the Integrated Management System (IMS). Act to promote the preservation of the environment, the health and occupational safety (personnel and their team), the safety of processes, and the preservation of assets in their scale of responsibility. Ethics and Compliance: In compliance with the Code of Business Conduct and Ethics, as well as with MODEC Group Ethics and Compliance Policies and Local Procedures, their respective forms, online and live mandatory trainings is committed to comply with all applicable laws, regulations and obligations and act in accordance with the highest ethical standards and with MODEC values. Emergency Plan: As per indication on the Training Matrix, Emergency Management Guide and Offshore Emergency Management, available in the Integrated Management System (IMS). Financial and Operational Dimensions / Impact and Decision-making Authority Complies with Authorization Matrix Procedure, available in the Integrated Management System (IMS). Work Relationships As per indication on the Organizational Chart, provided by the company. Job Challenges and Problem Solving Monitor all production indicators while guiding the Operators' team in the field maneuvers, due to high level of simultaneous attention that both activities require. Work in shifts due to continuous operation and constant personnel presence required, being necessary to invert the shifts of work during the offshore period. Qualifications & Experience Minimum 5 years' experience is expected; Previous experience in Offshore/Onshore Oil & Gas area is expected. Previous experience as Production Operator in F(P)SO units is required. Previous experience in Production Control Rooms is required. Previous experience and knowledge of hydrocarbon processing and utility systems are expected. Key Behaviors: Complies with the Job Competence Model, provided by the T&D&P team. Languages: Fluent English is mandatory. Software: Microsoft Office suite (PowerPoint, Excel, Word) Working Conditions Complies as a highest importance and priority with safety, security, protection of environment and occupational health conditions for the job position.

system, preferably till commissioning phase •	Familiarity with various system protocols, including but not limited to, TCP/IP, HTTPS, PLC integration and Modbus •	Passion for self-learning and description:

Job Description We are seeking a highly motivated and skilled Research Fellow to join our team on a project to demonstrate high-performance AI computing systems. title: Civil, Structural and Geotechnical Design Engineer
description: The Role The role reports directly to the team lead. He or She is responsible for supporting the Professional Engineers on the design and supervision of new erections, addition and alteration (A&A) work to existing buildings, earth retaining and stabilising systems, specialist designs for the facade, glass, demolition works etc, as well as temporary works. The role may also be required to conduct site inspections. Responsibilities: Carry out detailed analysis and design of civil, structural, and geotechnical works; Prepare design calculation reports and structural drawings for authorities' submissions; Coordinate with various stakeholders such as clients, consultants, and contractors for projects; and Supervise and inspect construction works; Requirements: Degree in Civil/Structural Engineering from a recognized Institution; Strong engineering fundamentals and analytical skills in geotechnical and structural analysis and design work would be an advantage; A good knowledge of the application of design and drafting software such as Autocad, Staad Pro, Plaxis, Prota, and Tedds would be an advantage; Fresh graduates and experience candidates are welcome to apply. Experienced candidates may be considered for a more senior position. Passing FEE 1 and FEE 2 are an advantage for senior hires. About VEngineers VEngineers Consultancy Pte Ltd is a leading engineering consultancy, founded in Singapore, with design offices in both Singapore and Malaysia. The company was originally founded in 2018 as a sole proprietorship under the name VEngineers. To support its growth and broaden its geographical footprint, the company transitioned toVEngineers Consultancy Pte Ltd in 2020. Our projects now span from Singapore to China, Bhutan, and Cambodia, showcasing our ability to adapt to various design codes and seismic conditions. In 2023, we expanded further by establishing VNM Engineering Sdn Bhd in Malaysia, aiming to collaborate with local consultants on landmark projects across the region. Over the years, we have successfullydelivered a diverse range of civil, structural, and geotechnical engineering projects both locally and abroad. At VEngineers, innovation is at the core of everything we do. We constantly seek out innovative solutions that create value throughout the building life cycle for all stakeholders in the construction industry. Backed by a team of experienced and dedicated engineers, we are trusted by clients for delivering quality services and value-added solutions over the years. Our commitment to innovation has led to industry collaborations, including the commercialization of the Ultra-High Performance Concrete (UHPC) Prefabricated Bathroom Unit System across four residential projects. We also hold two patents in UHPC Prefabricated Prefinished Volumetric Construction System in Singapore. As we stand at the forefront of the industry, VEngineers continues to explore new ways to enhance productivity and sustainability within the built environment. Vision: To be a Civil & Structural (C&S) Consultancy firm recognised for innovative engineering solutions that are created in the course of work. Mission: To engineer construction solutions that create new values for all building stakeholders. Why Join Us? We offer attractive remuneration packages that commensurate with performance and work experience; Flexibenefits, Medical, rand Dental 13th AWS and Performance Bonus Group Insurance Training Grant and Education support, Miscellaneous allowance, Blue Sky Friday Bonding Events

Experience in new chilled water system A&A and new proposed development projects would be added advantage. Site Location: Loyang Way Expected Commencement Date: March 2026. Duties and Responsibilities: The primary objective of the M&E RE/RTO shall be to assist the M&E Engineer/Client in ensuring that the M&E Installation works are in accordance with the specified requirements with respect to design, quality and performance. Duties and responsibilities shall among others, encompass the following: - General Assist the client/consultant to oversee all project activities; to prepare weekly progress report update to Clients/ Consultants. Prepare weekly progress report update to Clients/Consultants. To ensure that all installation methods and material used are in accordance with the contract drawings and specifications as well as client’s house rule and the Greenmark and Environmental Aspects and Impacts requirements. To liaise with the Contractors and the M&E Engineer in the checking and approval of shop drawings, catalogues and samples. To liaise with the Contractors in the co-ordination of site works. To liaise with the Contractors in resolving site problems. All problems on site shall be referred immediately to the M&E Engineer. The M&E RTO shall issue sketches or site memos as and when necessary. All site memos shall be copied to the M&E Engineer. The M&E RE/RTO shall not issue Instructions/Directions. To monitor and ensure that the site progress is in accordance with the approved construction programme. Any deviation from the construction schedule shall be reported immediately to the M&E Engineer. Technical To check on site that all materials used are of the approved type and of the best quality. To check on site that all installation works are of the approved methods as specified in the approved drawings. To check on site that all material and equipment used have been approved by the M&E Engineer. To ensure that the contractors record accurately on drawings, the installation works on site. This is to ensure that the ‘As-Built’ drawings reflect accurately the work that has been done on site. To check on site that all material and equipment installed on site are according to the code and client requirements approved by the M&E Engineer. Cost and Records To record, co-relate and file all incoming and outgoing design, shop, construction and co-ordination drawings. To record, co-relate and file all incoming and outgoing mail pertaining to the M&E works. To check and record the contractor's site progress. The M&E RE/RTO shall be required to submit the daily progress report to the M&E Engineer on a weekly basis. A monthly progress assessment/summary with comments on contractor's performance and forecast of manpower requirements and problem areas. To advise the M&E Engineer on the percentage of M&E works completed, equipment/material delivered to site for the purpose of progress payment evaluation. To check Contractors' claims and variation orders. Actual site check and measurement shall be carried out.

title: Geotechnical Design Engineer
description: We are looking for a Geotechnical Design Engineer with experience in geotechnical design, analysis and writing technical proposals. The Geotechnical Design Engineer should be knowledgeable in basic soil mechanics, as well as experienced in geotechnical analysis and design software. Reporting to the Design Manager, you will have the opportunity to collaborate with the Business Development Team to provide design support for tenders. You will be expected to review tender specifications, requirements and draft the technical proposals. Likewise, you will be working closely with the Operations team to perform design-related coordination work, analysing geotechnical issues at project sites, and preparing detailed drawings for the site team. To be successful as a Geotechnical Design Engineer, you should be independent, demonstrate strong analytical thinking and be able to have prompt, open and direct communications with internal and external stakeholders to achieve project expectations. Outstanding candidates should be meticulous, detail oriented, demonstrate willingness to learn and display a positive attitude with a drive for results. Think you’ve got what it takes? A day in the life of Geotechnical Design Engineer Design support for tenders : Review and summarize soil condition reports, tender specifications and requirements. Identify and summarise geotechnical problems and technical risk. Analyse geotechnical problems and draft technical proposal. Design-related coordination work for ongoing projects : Perform analysis and design in accordance with current legislation, standards and codes of practice Prepare detailed construction drawings with the assistance of draughtsperson. Conduct regular site visits to integrate design concept and actual ground conditions. Quality Control (QC) review and verification between design assumptions and actual site conditions. Carry out geotechnical analysis and design using tools such as Slope/W and Plaxis as well as Keller’s in-house software. description: Construction Electrician Duties and Responsibilities Construction Electricians play a vital role in building projects, as they install and maintain the electrical systems that power the structures. A construction electrician refers to one whose profession is to install, maintain, and repair electrical power, communications, lighting, and control systems on industrial, residential, and/or commercial buildings. In essence, a construction electrician is responsible for installing and repairing complex electrical control, wiring, and lighting systems working from a technical blueprint.Their daily duties and responsibilities include: Reading and interpreting electrical and construction blueprints, diagrams and specifications Planning layout and installation of electrical wiring, equipment, and fixtures, based on job specifications and local codes Connecting wires to circuit breakers, transformers, or other components Testing electrical systems and continuity of circuits in electrical wiring, equipment, and fixtures to ensure compatibility and safety of system Inspecting electrical systems, equipment, and components to identify hazards, defects, and the need for adjustment or repair, and to ensure compliance with codes Installing, maintaining, modifying and repairing all electrical distribution systems and associated electrical equipment Utilizing a range of tools and equipment, such as power construction equipment, measuring devices, power tools, and testing equipment Training and mentoring apprentice electricians, providing them with the skills and knowledge needed in the field Ordering electrical supplies and equipment. Reviewing blueprints or technical diagrams Overseeing the installation and maintenance of wiring, control, and lighting systems Carrying out inspections on electrical components, such as transformers and circuit breakers Training and directing workers to install, maintain, and/or repair electrical wiring equipment Installing electrical apparatus, equipment, and fixtures for systems such as alarm Troubleshooting electrical issues with the appropriate utilization of relevant testing devices Complying strictly with National Electrical Code state and local building regulations Carrying out plans for electrical wiring for well-functioning lighting, intercom, and other electrical systems.
