title: Research Fellow (Operations Research & Analytics)
description:
Interested applicants are invited to apply directly at the NUS
Career Portal Your application will be processed only if you apply
via NUS
Career Portal We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Job Description Role Summary: Lead and manage multi-stakeholder research projects in operations research, analytics, and optimization, including collaborations with NTUC FairPrice (Plug-in Flow Routing Optimization Engine; Store Friendly replenishment packing) and PSA (data analysis to improve efficiency in the container logistics ecosystem), as well as other industry and academic projects. Drive end-to-end project delivery—from problem framing and model formulation to implementation, evaluation, and dissemination—while coordinating milestones and deliverables with collaborators. Key Responsibilities: Act as project manager for assigned projects, developing work plans, timelines, risk registers, and progress reports; ensure on-time delivery of milestones and industry-facing deliverables. Work closely with external collaborators (e.g., NTUC FairPrice, Store Friendly, PSA) and internal stakeholders to define objectives, align expectations, and translate operational needs into rigorous research questions. Design and formulate models for routing, packing, replenishment, scheduling, and network optimization; develop algorithms and decision-support tools using appropriate techniques (e.g., mathematical programming, stochastic optimization, simulation, machine learning). Lead data acquisition, cleaning, integration, and analysis; establish data pipelines and quality assurance processes; conduct experiments, benchmarking, and sensitivity analyses to validate models. Prototype and, where applicable, support pilot deployment of solutions; document methodologies, assumptions, reproducible workflows, and user guides. Prepare high-quality publications, technical reports, and presentations; contribute to dissemination through conferences, seminars, and industry briefings. Guide and mentor PhD students, research assistants, and interns; provide technical supervision, review work outputs, and foster a collaborative research culture. Coordinate compliance with NUS and partner data governance, ethics, and security requirements; manage project documentation and IP considerations in line with agreements. Contribute to grant proposals, project scoping, and resource planning; assist with budgeting and procurement related to project needs. Support broader IORA initiatives, including knowledge sharing, seminar organization, and collaborations across the University and with external partners Requirements PhD in Industrial Engineering/Operations Research/Management Science (or closely related), with a strong research record in stochastic modeling and optimization. Hands-on experience solving real-world routing, packing, replenishment, scheduling, and network optimization problems. Proficiency with large-scale decision methods (e.g., approximate dynamic programming/rollout, primal–dual or penalty controls, Lagrangian decomposition/index policies, queueing/fluid models). Strong programming skills in Python and/or Java (MATLAB a plus), with experience in data pipelines, simulation/digital twins, and reproducible experimentation. Proven project management and industry collaboration experience, delivering milestones and translating operational needs into deployable solutions. Strong communication and mentorship: publications, technical reporting, presentations, and guidance of PhD students/RAs.

Excellent communication and presentation skills with the ability to explain complex technical concepts to both technical and non-technical audiences. Experience working cross-functionally with Sales, Product, Engineering, and Customer Success teams. Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical field, or equivalent practical experience. An Entrepreneurial Builder: You are proactive and resourceful, comfortable identifying unmet needs and developing new solutions. You're driven to continuously improve our processes and help build a stronger, more efficient business. Skills Exceptional technical depth with the ability to design and articulate complex data and system architectures to both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Excellent written and verbal communicator with strong influencing skills and a proactive approach to follow-up and engagement. You are an excellent communicator (written and verbal skills), with demonstrable customer communication and coordination efforts, and the ability to take complex technical ideas and translate them to non-technical stakeholders A natural problem solver, demonstrating a high level of intellectual curiosity and a collaborative approach to solutioning. Ability to manage multiple competing, complex technical priorities effectively under time constraints in a fast-paced environment. You are a natural problem solver with a high level of intellectual curiosity and love working amongst a team to solve those problems. Adobe, In Design, Figma, JIRA, Excel and Power Point. title: PMO Analyst
description:
Zenith Infotech (S) Pte Ltd. was started in 1997, primarily with the vision of offering state-of-the-art IT Professionals and solutions to various organizations and thereby helping them increase their productivity and competitiveness. From deployment of one person to formation of whole IT teams, Zenith Infotech has helped clients with their staff augmentation needs. Zenith offers opportunity to be engaged in long term projects with large IT savvy companies, Consulting organizations, System Integrators, Government, and MNCs. EA Licence No: 20S0237 Role Overview The PMO Analyst will provide end-to-end PMO and
operational support to the Project Manager and Delivery Lead, ensuring effective governance, coordination, reporting, and day-to-day project operations across the IT project lifecycle. Key Responsibilities Project Governance & Controls Accurately capture meeting
     notes, minutes, key decisions, and action items; summarize, review, and circulate outputs in a timely manner. Maintain and regularly update
     Risk, Issue, Action, and Decision (RAID) logs in consultation with the project team. Support the upkeep and periodic
     updates of the Project Management Plan (PMP) to reflect agreed processes, governance, and delivery approach. Manage structured storage,

Reporting & Communications Coordinate, consolidate, and manage the development of project presentations and reports, including Weekly Status Reports, SteerCo / PSC decks, and management updates. Work closely with functional, technical, and delivery teams to gather inputs, ensure consistency of messaging, and maintain presentation quality. Support effective stakeholder communications across the program. Resource & Onboarding Management Manage onboarding and offboarding activities for project resources, coordinating with HR, IT and security teams as required. Maintain a comprehensive
     resource inventory, including roll-on and roll-off dates, roles, skills, and team allocations. Provide visibility and tracking support for resource utilization and upcoming resourcing needs. Operational & Administrative Support Set up project meetings,
     schedule sessions, and arrange or book meeting rooms and facilities as required. Support office and project room logistics, including coordination of supplies and other operational needs. Assist with workshop planning, key project meetings, and program events. Project Support Provide day-to-day support to the Project Manager and Delivery Lead on open tasks, follow-ups, and coordination activities. Support adherence to agreed project governance, standards, and delivery timelines. Skills & Experience Understanding of IT project lifecycles and common project management methodologies (e.g. Waterfall) Strong documentation, note-taking, and summarization skills. Proficient in Microsoft tools, including Excel, Word, PowerPoint, and MS Teams. Strong organizational and coordination skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities. Effective communication and stakeholder management skills. Experience as Executive Assistant with above experience will be useful. Preferred Experience Prior experience in a PMO or project support role within IT or digital transformation program. Experience supporting senior governance forums such as SteerCo / PSC meetings. Detail-oriented, proactive, and comfortable working in a fast-paced delivery environment.

title: Facility Engineer (Sustainability)
description:
About the Role We are seeking a Facility Engineer with sustainability experience to support the operation, maintenance, and continuous improvement of building
facilities, with a strong focus on energy efficiency, resource optimisation,
and sustainable facility management practices . The successful candidate will work closely with operations, vendors, and
stakeholders to ensure facilities are maintained to high standards while
supporting the organisation’s sustainability and compliance objectives. Key Responsibilities Facility Operations & Maintenance Oversee day-to-day facility operations including M&E systems (e.g. HVAC, electrical, plumbing, fire protection). Coordinate and supervise term contractors and service providers to ensure compliance with contractual requirements. Conduct routine inspections, preventive maintenance, and corrective works to ensure operational reliability. Assist in troubleshooting facility issues and implementing effective solutions. Sustainability & Resource Management Support sustainability initiatives such as energy efficiency, water conservation, and waste reduction programmes. Monitor and analyse utility consumption data (electricity, water, gas) and identify opportunities for optimisation. Assist in the implementation and tracking of sustainability KPIs and performance metrics . Support audits, certifications, and reporting related to sustainability standards (e.g. Green Mark, ISO 14001 / ISO 50001 / ISO 46001 where applicable). Compliance & Documentation Ensure compliance with statutory regulations, safety requirements, and environmental standards . Maintain proper documentation, reports, and records for facility operations and sustainability initiatives. Support internal and external audits, inspections, and assessments. Stakeholder & Project Support Liaise with internal stakeholders to understand operational needs and improvement opportunities. Support minor upgrading works, sustainability improvement projects, and pilot initiatives. Prepare technical reports proposals, and improvement recommendations when required. Requirements Diploma or Degree in Facilities Management, Building Service, Mechanical/Electrical Engineering, Environmental Engineering , or related discipline. Minimum 2 years of relevant experience in facility management, building operations, or sustainability-related roles. Basic working knowledge of M&E systems and building operations. Exposure to sustainability practices , energy management, water efficiency, or environmental compliance. Familiarity with local regulations and standards (e.g. BCA, NEA, WSH) is an advantage. Good analytical, documentation, and coordination skills. Able to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment. Preferred / Advantageous Skills Experience supporting Green Mark, ISO 14001, ISO 50001, ISO 46001 , or similar frameworks. Experience with utility monitoring systems , sustainability reporting, or audits. Basic data analysis skills (Excel or equivalent). Strong communication and stakeholder management skills. What We Offer Opportunities to work on sustainability-driven facility initiatives . Exposure to operational, engineering, and sustainability projects. Career development within the facilities and sustainability domain . Competitive remuneration and benefits.

title: Technician (Welder)
description: Pratt & Whitney, an RTX business, is a world leader in the design, manufacture and service of aircraft engines and auxiliary power units for commercial, military and business aircraft. Through industry-leading businesses – Collins Aerospace, Pratt & Whitney, and Raytheon – RTX pushes the limits of technology and science to redefine how we connect and protect our world. We are advancing aviation, engineering integrated defense systems, and developing next-generation technology solutions and manufacturing to help global customers address their most critical challenges. Make a difference with a career at Pratt & Whitney. What You Will Do Perform TIG welding to refurbish aircraft engine components to meet quality standards. Support the cell operations in benching, blasting, brazing, blending, polishing and cold working processes. Potential to be trained on operating new Automation Machines. Attend EH&S training programs and takes personal responsibility to ensure safety adherence. Support the continuous improvement programs, CORE (Customer Oriented Results & Excellence) activities. Qualifications You Must Have GCE ’O’ Level or NITEC in Precision, Aerospace, Mechanical or relevant disciplines. 1 year relevant experience in TIG welding and Plasma Welding. Comfortable to learn new welding methodology. Ability to understand and follow technical drawing and standard operation procedures (SOP). Meticulous with an eye for details and adopts a hands-on approach. Willing to work on rotating/Permanent shifts (Day & Night) and overtime on need basis. Qualifications We Prefer Familiar with the relevant air worthiness and regulatory agencies requirements. Relevant aerospace, manufacturing or precision engineering experience. Attended external trainings with welding certification. What We Offer Career and skills development opportunities Employee scholarship program for further education Comprehensive health, medical and dental benefits On-the-job training and in-house/external certifications Competitive renumeration package Allowance and overtime benefits where appliable. Company transport provided.

Collaborate with team members to integrate cloud technologies and adhere to IT security policies, enhancing infrastructure resilience. Work independently with minimal supervision, demonstrating resourcefulness and passion to meet operational goals Required competencies and certifications Diploma or Degree in Information Technology,  Telecommunication, Computer Science, or equivalent Preferred competencies and qualifications Familiarity with cloud technologies and IT security policies to enhance infrastructure security and scalability. Basic knowledge of IP Telephony and VoIP systems (TCP, UDP, RTP) to support communication technologies

title: Industrial Hygienist
description: Are you our next creator of change? Do you want to be part of creating solutions for mitigating climate change? At Neste, you will be working for the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel and renewable diesel. Together we will accelerate transformation for a more sustainable future. We are looking for you, Industrial Hygienist to join our Safety Team in the Singapore Refinery You will focus on preventing occupational illness and disease by anticipating, recognizing, evaluating, and controlling environmental factors arising in the workplace which may cause sickness, impaired health and well-being, or significant discomfort to the workforce. In this job you will collaborate and work closely with the safety team, stakeholders from other functions and departments including the Corporate Safety Team in Finland. You will provide your expertise to our Refinery on matters of industrial hygiene (IH) and occupational health & safety (OH&S). In our Safety team, you will report to the Safety Manager. As the technical authority and expert in IH and OH&S, you manage and lead the refinery in IH and OH&S function to reduce operational risk exposure to the workforce through governance of OEMS principles, regulatory compliance and requirements. Together with you and your expertise, we can make an impact today and keep our promises for tomorrow. Reasons to join Real impact right now. We create solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. We live by our values every day; we care, we have courage, we cooperate. Safety and caring. We value safety always and everywhere. We want our work to feel good and do good. At Neste, we nurture a culture where everyone is welcome and feels safe, respected and encouraged to be their unique true selves. Exciting growth. Transformation brings new possibilities, fostering both courage and curiosity. As a global company, collaboration is the key, and we believe in flexible, smart ways of working. We support personal development with versatile learning and career opportunities. More about the job Your role in our team / in creating a sustainable future Risk Identification, Evaluation and Control Proactively anticipate and recognize chemical hazards, physical hazards, biological hazards, and ergonomic hazards across refining areas Design, execute, and manage comprehensive Industrial Hygiene (IH) monitoring programs . Conduct qualitative and quantitative exposure assessments for airborne contaminants, chemical exposure, noise & physical agents and ergonomic risk Interpret monitoring results against local (MOM/WSH) and internationally recognized (ACGIH/OSHA) standards Conduct routine Occupational Hygiene site inspections and audits, focusing on exposure risks, ventilation systems (LEVs), and chemical storage/handling procedures Control Measures and Management Systems Recommend, implement effective industrial hygiene engineering as key control strategies and evaluate its effectiveness Oversight chemical inventory, Safety Data Sheet (SDS) management, and risk assessment process for hazardous substances Develop and enforce stringent chemical handling procedures Administer and manage IH Management system and programs such as Ventilation Systems, Hazard Communications, PPE standards, Ergonomic standards, heat stress program, medical examination & surveillance program, Hearing Conservation Program (HCP), including noise mapping, audiometric testing, noise control implementation, process and office ergonomic program Conduct industrial hygiene risk assessments for process work area, offices, laboratory, recommending necessary adjustments and controls measures Strategizing Regulatory Compliance (PEL, STEL, TWA, C) and Translating to Operable Compliance Translate PEL, STEL, OEL requirement into practical, executable operational compliance Deep working knowledge of Singapore and international acts, regulations, requirements related to industrial hygiene topics Ensure strict adherence to permissible/occupational exposure limit and relevant regulations and Codes of Practice relating to occupational health and safety Prepare detailed industrial hygiene reports, regulatory submissions and internal performance metrics. Maintain accurate records of all monitoring data, calibration records, and exposure assessments Provide technical support and expertise during health-related emergency response situations (e.g chemical spills) Training and Collaboration Develop and deliver targeted training programs on industrial hygiene topics, including chemical management and hazard communication, PPE use, respiratory protection, noise conservations, heat stress, ergonomics Collaborate closely with cross functional teams to integrate industrial hygiene into daily operations, projects and Process Safety Management (PSM). Participate as safety subject matter expert in incident investigation team, facilitate Safety department owned investigations or as directed Keys to success in this role / The best combination to succeed in this role Technical expert in industrial hygiene, occupational health role and operationalized industrial hygiene into day to day operations Minimum 10 years experience as a practicing industrial hygienist in a refinery, petrochemical or chemical manufacturing industry; track record in industrial hygiene monitoring (chemical, physical and biological to personal and area exposure)  IH engineering control, IH Management System Recognized Bachelor Degree in Safety or similar, master degree in industrial hygiene highly desired Professionally certified industrial hygienist (CIH) or registered industrial hygienist (RIH) or equivalent. Candidates without IH certification but demonstrate mastery and proven track record in IH will be considered Other strengths we value We value candidates with transdisciplinary thinking, lifelong learning and digital literacy. Let’s get you started We look forward to receiving your CV and application (with a salary request) We will do our best to contact you after the application time has ended. The recruitment process will include interviews and a possible aptitude assessment. The person selected for the position must pass a health examination including a drug test. A security clearance is obtained with your consent. Neste in brief Neste (NESTE, Nasdaq Helsinki) creates solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. The company is the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) and renewable diesel, enabling its customers to reduce their greenhouse gas emissions. Neste refines waste, residues and other renewable raw materials to high-quality renewable fuels at its refineries located on three continents. The company’s annual renewable fuels production capacity will be increased to 6.8 million tons in 2027. Neste has high standards for sustainability and the company has consistently been recognized by several leading sustainability indices. In 2024, Neste's revenue stood at EUR 20.6 billion. Read more: neste.com

title: Memory Lead Engineer
description:
Our client is a US-based pioneer and leading provider of ultra-low-power semiconductor solutions. We are seeking qualified candidate to join their Singapore team to work on breakthrough technologies powering the next wave of intelligent devices. Memory Lead Engineer Scope We are seeking an experienced Memory Lead Engineer with expertise in memory technologies including SRAM, DRAM, HBM, RRAM, and MRAM. Energy Efficient memory has become vital for our
company products, particularly as edge AI workloads grow in complexity.  As a Memory Lead Engineer, you will be responsible for end-to-end memory deployments into our products, such as defining the memory roadmap, memory evaluation, recommending memory architectures and circuit design strategies, leading memory characterization, and more.  Your role will involve working closely with cross-functional teams, including system architects, circuit designers, and process engineers within the company, as well as third-party memory vendors and foundries. Responsibilities: Research and analyze the latest memory technologies to understand their characteristics, advantages, and its limitations to provide energy efficient memory solution Collaborate with system architects and product teams to define memory requirements and      specifications for next-generation products. Evaluate and simulate memory circuits and develop memory architecture and customization proposals. Serve as lead using both internal and third-party resources to implement new memory architectures and customizations. Lead memory characterization and compiler efforts. Conduct detailed circuit and system-level simulations to evaluate memory performance, stability, and reliability. Work closely with process engineers and third-party foundry engineers to ensure high yield and reliability for new memories. Conduct thorough analysis of memory test data, identify issues, and propose design enhancements or process modifications. Stay up to date with the latest advancements and industry trends in emerging memory technologies and contribute to technical discussions and brainstorming sessions. Requirements: Bachelor’s degree in electronics engineering or a related field. A master's or Ph.D. degree is preferred. Experience in memory architecture design and memory compilers, particularly in HBM, SRAM, DRAM, MRAM, and RRAM. Proficiency in low power and high-performance memory technologies. Strong understanding of memory architectures, including memory cells, sense amplifiers, drivers, and peripheral circuits. Proficiency in industry-standard EDA tools for circuit simulation and design. Experience running memory characterization using standard EDA tools. Knowledge of layout design principles and experience working closely with layout designers. Familiarity with semiconductor manufacturing processes and integration challenges. Ability to conduct detailed analysis and troubleshooting of memory test data. Excellent problem-solving skills and the ability to work in a fast-paced, collaborative environment. Strong communication and teamwork skills to collaborate with cross-functional teams. Demonstrated ability to adapt to changing project priorities and meet tight deadlines. EA Personnel Registration No: R1106329 EA License No: 12C6254

description: About us At Dyson, we believe in the relentless pursuit of improvement, raising the bar and achieving new heights.  We’ve spent decades pioneering inventions and continually improving. It’s about inventing the future. Our philosophy remains the same as it was when James Dyson invented the first cyclonic vacuum cleaner. We remain family owned. We plot our path, unshackled from conventional thinking. At Dyson, we constantly strive to find pioneeringways to solve everyday problems. You will be joining us at a pivotal moment, as we elevate our internal communications strategy and deepen integration acrossthe organisation. You will help to implement Dyson’s internal communications and engagement agenda, inspiring employees through events and engagement opportunities, key product moments, experiences and corporate milestones and inspiring content. You’ll play an active part in bringing our strategy and employee experience to life. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with a talent for creative storytelling and engaging diverse audiences. Demonstrated ability to work effectively under tight deadlines and in fast-paced, dynamic settings, consistently delivering outstanding results for the organisation. Creative and innovative mindset, tech savvy, always seeking opportunities for continuous improvement and commitment to delivering high-quality work. title: AI Product Manager (Management Trainee)
description: Exciting news! We are on a growing journey and building a stellar team! At Sunnystep, we are not just building leaders – we are cultivating a dynamic team that thrives on innovation, collaboration, and collective success. Why should you be part of Sunnystep's success? We are an innovative force in consumer retail with a clear brand purpose — we believe wellness starts with our feet. Good shoes support our feet and our body, and help us move and live better. We are redefining comfort in every step to an unparalleled experience without compromising on style. Our mission is to revolutionize the footwear industry by crafting the most comfortable shoes with unwavering commitment to environmental responsibility ensuring that every step our customers take not only embodies unrivalled comfort but also leaves a positive impact on the planet. You'll operate as an AI Product Manager in training, working directly with the CEO / product lead to build, ship, and iterate AI-powered features that improve business metrics (conversion, productivity, customer satisfaction, cost reduction). This is not a "research role". You are expected to ship weekly, learn fast, and turn ambiguous problems into working product flows. What You'll Own (Core Responsibilities) AI Feature Delivery (End-to-End) Convert business problems into clear product requirements (PRDs, user stories, workflows).Build MVPs fast using LLMs + simple workflows (prompting, RAG, automation, human-in-the-loop).Partner with engineers/design to ship, measure, iterate.Own rollout, adoption, training, and feedback loops. User & Workflow Design (Practical, Not Theoretical) Conduct lightweight discovery: shadow users, interview store teams/internal stakeholders.Map workflows (before/after): time saved, error reduction, throughput increase.Define UX for AI features (approval flows, confidence display, fallback behavior). Data + Product Analytics Track and improve key metrics: adoption, usage frequency, activation rate, task success rate.Set up dashboards or lightweight measurement systems (Sheets/Looker/Lark/SQL).Use evidence to decide what to build next—no guesswork. Prompting + Evaluation Discipline Design prompts and test systematically (A/B variants, edge cases).Build evaluation sets and define “good output” standards.Reduce hallucinations and failure modes using guardrails + structured outputs. Operator Mindset (Execution + Systems) Turn messy workflows into repeatable SOPs.Create playbooks so results scale beyond you.Handle stakeholder alignment and updates with clarity and speed. Minimum Requirements (Must Have) Strong structure and writing: can produce clear PRDs and specs. High agency: takes initiative without being asked repeatedly. Comfort with ambiguity + speed: you don’t freeze when it’s unclear. Basic AI literacy:understands what LLMs can/can’t docan write prompts and test systematically knows simple concepts like RAG, embeddings, evaluation Analytical ability: can interpret data and form a crisp recommendation. Preferred Requirements (Big Advantage) Built something tangible before (app, automation, dashboard, prototype). Experience with tools like:Notion/Lark, Sheets, SQL basicsZapier/Make, Airtable, RetoolPython, API basics Prior exposure to retail, e-commerce, customer support, operations. Culture Fit (Non-Negotiables) You must naturally operate with: Extreme Ownership: results > excuses Speed: ship > talk Obsess over quality & detail: no sloppy edge cases Radical honesty: say what’s broken fast Ruthless prioritization: focus on impact, ignore noise Headquartered in Singapore, Sunnystep is committed to fostering community, inclusivity, integrity, and technology to support everybody’s journey of possibilities. We review each application thoroughly and will arrange an initial discussion with the shortlisted candidates who best meet our requirements. If you find that the requirements align with your experience and expertise, you may express your interest through this job advertisement or you may reach out to our Hiring Team – hiring@sunnystep.com .

title: Agile Business Analyst [Government Project]
description: 2-year contract, renewable Government project Hybrid work arrangement Roles and Responsibilities Strong business analyst experience and able to create detailed business analysis, outlining problems, opportunities and solutions for a business. Analyse the business needs, scope the problem and develop business case to address the problem. Understand technical limitations, propose or develop and implement solutions to address the technical limitations. Oversee, manage multiple vendors and external providers of ICT services, work with team members from application and infra teams on the solutions and design to seek clarification and acceptance to deliver project on time and within budget. Identify stakeholders, techniques and processes to manage, track and trace requirements. Refine and clarify business need, define solution scope and develop business case. Conduct requirements workshop to gather user requirements and Analyses business processes to provide clear analysis and documentation of the problems and proposed solution. Assess current state, analyse stated requirements of stakeholders and elaborate to sufficient detail that accurately defines business need within specified scope. Source and evaluate alternatives to identify solution that best fits business need, identify gaps, and determine workarounds/changes to the solution. Assess deployed solutions to see how well they met the original need. Participate and contribute in project activities such as Requirement Gathering, User Acceptance Testing, Training & Implementation; Review project deliverables. e.g. functional specifications, system test plan & results, user guide, operation manual. Plan and perform user acceptance testing. Assess business needs for service requests and the impact of enhancements to the System. Assess problem resolution approach and implement effective service recovery of ICT incidents and establish systems and processes to prevent recurrence of the same incident. Project Management (Scope, Budget and Schedule) including regular management reporting for maintenance and/or development projects. Ensure that projects are executed based on the project plan to meet identified business needs, within agreed parameters of cost, duration and quality. Identify potential project risks and its implication to the organisation and to recommend mitigating measures so as to manage the risks. Understand the IT management policy, quality management policy and security guidelines to ensure the development processes, procedures and system are designed to comply with these policies and guidelines. Manage and resolve day-to-day IT related issues pertaining to application, infrastructure or security, to facilitate smooth running of daily operations. Knowledge and Skills Tertiary education in Computer/Computer Science or Electronics Engineering or Information Technology or equivalent. Project Management Certification or equivalent. Hands-on experience in at least 3 full project development life cycles or project maintenance is preferred for the experienced ones. Ability to work both independently and as part of a team with professionals at all levels. Strong analytical, conceptualisation and problem-solving skills and able to prioritise and manage multiple tasks. Good design, coding, testing and debugging skills. Relevant experience in requirement elicitation techniques such as design thinking, persona analysis and customer journey mapping etc. Relevant experience in facilitating user requirement workshops. Relevant experience in web-based development and mobile IT projects. Results and customer oriented. Experience in Commercial Cloud AWS with focus on the following key area ECS Fargate AWS Lambda AWS RDS AWS QuickSight AWS EC2 AWS Cloudwatch AWS S3 Experience in DevOps/AGILE environment Agile Certification like Certified Scrum Master is preferred. Working experience in government environment will be advantageous. Familiarity with governance, adoption of digital services in enterprise IT environments. Work experience and strong troubleshooting skills in resolving application, infrastructure (including desktop, server, network) and security related issues in a (Singapore) government environment is preferred. Ability to take a broad, strategic perspective as well as drill deep to understand business needs and challenges. Team player with strong organization and people handling skills. Excellent communication skills, both oral and written, with the ability to pitch ideas and influence stakeholders.

title: Senior AI UX / UI Designer
description: Senior AI UX / UI Designer As a Senior AI UX / UI Designer, you will work closely with Product Owners, engineers, and stakeholders to design, test, and refine AI-enabled user experiences. The role requires deep involvement across research, interaction design, usability testing, and delivery, with a strong emphasis on applying UX best practices to AI use cases. Key Responsibilities: AI-Centric UX & UI Design Design end-to-end user experiences for AI-enabled products, including conversational interfaces, agentic workflows, predictive and data-driven interactions. Translate AI capabilities, decision logic, and system outputs into clear, intuitive, and user-friendly interfaces. Apply strong information architecture to support AI decision trees, confidence levels, and content hierarchies. User Research & Validation Conduct user research, interviews, and usability testing to understand user needs, behaviours, and trust considerations in AI interactions. Create personas, journey maps, task flows, wireframes, prototypes, and high-fidelity UI designs. Validate designs through usability testing, A/B testing, and feedback loops, iterating based on insights. Hands-On Delivery & Collaboration Work closely with Product Owners, developers, and data/AI teams to co-create practical, build-ready designs. Participate actively in agile ceremonies and incorporate sprint feedback into design improvements. Ensure accessibility, consistency, and adherence to UX/UI best practices across AI solutions. Design Systems & Documentation Create and maintain design systems, UI guidelines, and reusable components tailored for AI interfaces. Produce design documentation, annotated prototypes, and specifications to support development and adoption. Continuous Improvement Monitor post-launch user experience and identify opportunities to improve AI usability, explainability, and adoption. Stay current with emerging AI UX patterns, tools, and industry best practices. Requirements Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, AI/ML, or related field. 5–8 years of hands-on experience in UX/UI design and usability testing Demonstrated experience designing for AI-driven products (e.g. conversational AI, analytics-driven interfaces, automation, or decision-support systems) Strong experience in user research, journey mapping, and interaction design Experience designing conversational flows, multi-turn interactions, and AI error handling Proficiency with Figma , prototyping, wireframing, and usability testing tools Experience working in agile, cross-functional product teams 2 Positions Available Location : North East, Singapore Kindly indicate your current/last salary details and your notice period Interested candidates kindly forward your CV to michelleyy.dai@peoplesearch.jobs (Michelle Dai Yiying, Reg No: R1982577). Feel free to forward this great opportunity to someone who would be a great fit for this role. All information will be kept strictly confidential. We regret to inform that only successful applicants will be contacted. PeopleSearch Pte Ltd EA License No: 16S8057

Strong experience in security tools (Qualys, Sophos), monitoring tools (Dynatrace), and access management solutions (SailPoint). Strong experience of SDLC, SOP development, and audit readiness. Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities. Excellent communication and collaboration skills. Ability to manage multiple projects and work under regulatory compliance pressure. title: Visiting Professor in Animation and/or Game Design
description: School of Art, Design and Media College of Computing and Data Science Nanyang Technological University, Singapore Visiting Professor in Animation and/or Game Design The School of Art, Design and Media (ADM) at Nanyang Technological University Singapore (NTU) invites applications for a one-year Visiting Professor position in Games and/or Animation. ADM is part of the College of Humanities, Arts and Social Sciences (COHASS). This position is intended for eminent overseas academics such as international prize recipients or distinguished scholars whose presence will enhance the academic standing of the faculty. The primary responsibility of this role is teaching. Therefore, candidates should be able to demonstrate a strong teaching track record in a current or previous Associate Professor academic level position. We seek candidates with expertise in one or more areas of Games and/or Animation, including fields such as game design, interactive media, concept development, animation, and creative technologies. The appointed faculty will play a key role in supporting ADM’s position as a leader in contemporary digital media and design education in the region. Located in multi-cultural Singapore, the School of Art Design Media (ADM) in Nanyang Technological University (NTU) offers a studio-based education in a comprehensive university setting, providing students with the knowledge and skills needed to develop and produce groundbreaking creative works and scholarly activity. ADM also promotes Singapore’s creative culture and identity and is positioned to play a central role in the transformation of Singapore into a regional center of media and creativity. Designed and taught by international faculty drawn from nearly 20 countries, the school offer 4-year BFA programs in Design Art and Media Art, with an adaptive and evolving curriculum building upon excellence in our core disciplines of Animation, Film, Game, Photography, Interaction, Product Design, and Visual Communication, The programs are designed to meet the highest professional and academic standards worldwide and are responsive to student interests and evolving global needs. Post-graduate programs combine theory and practice, offering MA and PhD by research and a MA in Museum and Curatorial Practice. In 2026 ADM will start an innovative MSc Game Design Art & Technology, taught jointly with the College of Computer and Data Sciences (CCDS). ADM ranks amongst the top 5 art and design schools in Asia and top 20 worldwide (QS World University Rankings by subject 2025). NTU is a research-intensive university, home to 33,000 undergraduate and postgraduate students in Engineering, Science, Humanities, Social Science, Business, Education and Medicine. Ranked amongst the world’s top 30 universities by QS, NTU has also been named amongst the world’s top young universities for the past 7 years. Further information about the University and the School can be viewed at the following websites: NTU : www.ntu.edu.sg ADM : www.ntu.edu.sg/adm Applicants must be confident navigating the rapidly evolving landscape of digital media and technology and demonstrate a strong commitment to teaching innovation and student mentorship. The role is teaching-focused; applicants should bring an established record of tertiary-level teaching experience and curricular engagement. As a creative practitioner and educator with expertise in animation, games, or interactive media, candidate will demonstrate: A strong record in tertiary education (A terminal MFA (or its equivalent) or PhD degree in a relevant field is required), with the ability to guide student-led projects from concept to completion across games, animation, visual development, or interactive media. · A recognised practice in animation, games, or related fields, with an impactful portfolio showcasing innovation, storytelling, and design across emerging technologies such as AR/VR, motion graphics, generative AI, or installations. · Evidence of scholarly output in contemporary forums related to games, animation, or creative computing, and a commitment to advancing the discipline through research or creative practice. · A willingness to engage with cultural, ethical, and philosophical dimensions of media practice, and the ability to connect with fields like digital humanities, creative coding, or social platforms. · A passion for education and the ability to foster a supportive, inclusive, and diverse learning environment, with strong interpersonal and organizational skills. · National or international recognition by academic or industry, with a track record of leadership and contribution to the evolution of games, animation, or interactive media. Applicants are invited to submit their applications through the NTU career portal by clicking on the “Apply” button above. Please provide the following in your application: 1. Cover letter; 2. Curriculum vitae (including records of competitive grants and graduate supervision, significant creative works and scholarly publications; if applicable); 3. Teaching statement; 4. Research statement; The closing date for applications is 26 September 2025. Only shortlisted candidates will be notified via email.

description: Job Summary The Document Controller is responsible for maintaining and managing the engineering document control system, both electronic and hard copy. This role requires excellent proficiency in Microsoft Excel to create and maintain various document registers, logs, and trackers. The Document Controller will ensure that all engineering documents, drawings, RFIs (Requests for Information), specifications, and other related documents are accurately tracked, filed, and readily accessible to project teams. Key Responsibilities: 1. Document Management: - Maintain and manage the engineering document control system for both electronic records and hard copies. - Receive, log, distribute, and track engineering documents, including drawings, RFIs, specifications, and other relevant materials. - Ensure all documents are filed and categorized according to the company’s document control procedures. 2. Excel Tracking and Reporting - Create and maintain document registers, logs, and trackers utilizing advanced Excel functions to track document versions, statuses, and deadlines. - Prepare and distribute weekly and monthly reports on document status, outstanding items, and critical deadlines to relevant stakeholders. 3. Coordination - Coordinate with engineers, designers, and external contractors to ensure that all document versions are accurate, up-to-date, and reflective of current project needs. - Monitor deadlines and submission timelines for documents, ensuring timely reviews and approvals. 4. Version Control and Access Management - Control access permissions for all documents and ensure proper version control is maintained on all files to prevent unauthorized access or use of outdated documents. 5. Audit Support - Support internal and external audits by ensuring proper documentation filing and retrieval and maintaining an organized document management system. 6. Continuous Improvement - Identify opportunities for process improvements related to document management and suggest enhancements to the document control system based on feedback from the engineering team. Qualifications: - Previous experience in document control or a similar role, ideally within an engineering or construction environment. - Proficient in Microsoft Excel, with advanced knowledge of functions such as VLOOKUP, pivot tables, and macros. - Strong organizational skills and attention to detail. - Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work collaboratively in a team environment. - Knowledge of document control software and systems is a plus.

title: Full Stack Software Developer - A26016
description: Activate Interactive Pte Ltd (“Activate”) is a leading technology consultancy headquartered in Singapore with a presence in Malaysia and Indonesia. Our clients are empowered with quality, cost-effective, and impactful end-to-end application development, like mobile and web applications, and cloud technology that remove technology roadblocks and increase their business efficiency. We believe in positively impacting the lives of people around us and the environment we live in through the use of technology. Hence, we are committed to providing a conducive environment for all employees to realise their full potential, who in turn have the opportunity to continuously drive innovation. Co-Development Business Unit is looking for Full Stack Software Developer This is a fixed term contract role. The engagement is 1 year. Internal Code: A26016 You will be part of the team responsible for the full stack design, development, and maintenance of software testing toolkits that support the Air Traffic Management Support (ATMS) systems. These toolkits are essential for validating the functionality and performance of the ATM support systems, which support essential Air Traffic Control (ATC) operations such as flight plan processing and NOTAM dissemination. You will be involved in the end-to-end software development lifecycle, including frontend and backend development, system integration, testing, deployment of the software testing toolkits. You will work closely with system engineers, testers, and operational users to ensure the toolkits meet operational, technical, and regulatory requirements. What will you do? Design and develop full stack testing toolkits and simulators for ATM support systems, covering both frontend interfaces and backend logic. Build intuitive and responsive user interfaces for test configuration, execution, and reporting using modern web technologies. Develop backend services and APIs to support automated testing and data simulation. Integrate with third-party services, APIs, and gateways as required Implement test automation frameworks and CI/CD pipelines to streamline testing and deployment processes. Monitor application performance, troubleshoot issues, and implement optimisation strategies Collaborate with system engineers and testers to define test requirements and ensure comprehensive coverage of operational scenarios. Ensure the toolkits are scalable, maintainable, and aligned with software engineering best practices. Maintain technical documentation including system design and review, user guides, test plans, and system architecture. Ensure compliance with aviation standards (e.g., ICAO, Eurocae) and cybersecurity requirements in the development of testing tools. Provide technical support during system integration, testing, and commissioning phases. Stay current with emerging technologies and recommend improvements to development processes. What are we looking for? Minimum 2 years of experiences in full stack software development, preferably in mission-critical or safety-critical systems. Proficient in frontend technologies such as HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript/TypeScript, and frameworks like React, Angular, or Vue.js. Strong backend development skills using Python, Java, Node.js, or similar languages. Experienced with RESTful APIs, microservices architecture, and database systems (SQL/NoSQL). Experienced with system simulation, data modelling, and test data generation. Familiar with modern software practices including version control, automated testing, CI/CD pipelines, code reviews, and agile methodologies Experience with Cloud platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure) and containerization (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes) is a plus. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with attention to detail. Good communication and documentation skills. Organised and self-motivated, able to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines. title: Research Fellow (Orbitronics)

We are seeking a research fellow to conduct high-impact research on orbital current driven domain wall motion. The role will focus on thin-film deposition and onchip device fabrication, combined with electrical transport measurements to investigate how orbital currents transfer angular momentum to local magnetization and drive magnetic textures. Key Responsibilities: Depositing high quality magnetic thin films Performing structural and magnetic characterization Fabricating devices using lithography techniques Conducting high-field low-temperature electrical transport measurements Publish research results in high-impact peer-reviewed journals Mentoring PhD, Masters, and undergraduate students Job Requirements: PhD in Physics or a closely related field Strong background in experimental condensed matter or material physics, with experience in thin-film deposition, device fabrication, and electrical transport measurements Familiarity with the theory of magnetism Proficiency in MATLAB/Python and LabVIEW for data acquisition and analysis Good written and oral communication skills Ability to work collaboratively in a fast-paced research environment Experience working with and mentoring PhD, Master, and undergraduate students Ability to provide regular progress reports to the PI Ability to contribute to the development of research proposals for external funding based on project outcomes We regret to inform that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

title: Senior Engineer for Ion Implantation Process (APM), IME
description:
A*STAR Institute of Microelectronics (A*STAR IME) is leading an R&D initiative focused on advancing critical process modules for next-generation semiconductor device fabrication. One key area of innovation is ion implantation, an essential step in the fabrication of high-performance devices. This position offers an opportunity to develop strong expertise in ion implantation process technology, including lattice damage control, channelling effects, and post-implant recovery. The process engineer will work closely with a Senior Scientist and cross-functional technology teams. The role integrates process simulation, experimental execution, and advanced materials characterization to enable robust and scalable process solutions. Key Responsibilities: Process Support: Support the development and validation of ion implantation recipes to achieve targeted dopant profiles while minimizing lattice damage. Perform quantitative analysis of SIMS depth profiles, sheet-resistance (Rs) measurements, and RBS/channeling data to correlate process conditions. Ion activation: Optimize post-implant annealing conditions to maximize 
electrical dopant activation while minimizing residual lattice damage, supported by Rs, Hall, SIMS, and structural characterization. Defect Analysis: Analyze implant-induced defects using experimental data and simulations and contribute to strategies for defect suppression and recovery through process optimization. Ion-implant process development: Design and execute ion-implantation experiments with systematic variation of dose, energy, tilt, and rotation, and analyze results to improve device performance and process robustness. Operational Coordination: Review work orders, support equipment operators in resolving process-related issues, and work with equipment vendors to improve tool performance, stability, and uptime. Job Requirements: Bachelor's or Master's degree in Electrical Engineering, Materials Science, Physics, or a related field. Basic understanding of solid-state physics, including ion implantation processes and dopant activation, familiarity with defect formation and recovery in semiconductors. Experience or academic exposure to process characterization and data analysis (SIMS, Rs, RBS/channeling). Familiarity with process simulation tools is preferred (e.g., SRIM/TRIM, Silvaco, Sentaurus Process, etc.). Candidates with no experience may apply.

title: Cybersecurity engineer
description: The Mission As a Cybersecurity Engineer, you will act as a strategic partner to our clients. Your goal is to move beyond basic defense and toward a state of total resilience, ensuring that even under attack, critical business operations remain uninterrupted. Key Responsibilities Resiliency & Strategy: Design and implement architectures that prioritize "recovery by design." You’ll assist clients in developing robust incident response and disaster recovery frameworks. Compliance & Certifications: Lead the charge in preparing clients for industrial certifications (e.g., ISO 27001, NIST, SOC2, or IEC 62443 ). You will conduct gap analyses and guide the remediation process. Hardware Hardening: Take a hands-on approach to securing the physical layer. This includes configuring BIOS/UEFI security, disabling unnecessary ports, and ensuring firmware integrity across diverse hardware environments. Audit & Governance: Perform regular compliance audits to ensure internal and external standards are met, translating complex technical requirements into actionable business insights. Typical Day-to-Day Activities Conducting firewall deployment vulnerability assessments and penetration testing to identify systemic weaknesses. Drafting and updating security policies and technical documentation. Collaborating with IT and product teams to integrate security into the SDLC and hardware procurement cycles. Monitoring threat intelligence feeds to pivot defense strategies against emerging "zero-day" threats. Providing technical guidance during client workshops to bridge the gap between "technical risk" and "business impact." The Mission As a Cybersecurity Engineer, you will act as a strategic partner to our clients. Your goal is to move beyond basic defense and toward a state of total resilience, ensuring that even under attack, critical business operations remain uninterrupted. Key Responsibilities Resiliency & Strategy: Design and implement architectures that prioritize "recovery by design." You’ll assist clients in developing robust incident response and disaster recovery frameworks. Compliance & Certifications: Lead the charge in preparing clients for industrial certifications (e.g., ISO 27001, NIST, SOC2, or IEC 62443 ). You will conduct gap analyses and guide the remediation process. Hardware Hardening: Take a hands-on approach to securing the physical layer. This includes configuring BIOS/UEFI security, disabling unnecessary ports, and ensuring firmware integrity across diverse hardware environments. Audit & Governance: Perform regular compliance audits to ensure internal and external standards are met, translating complex technical requirements into actionable business insights. Conducting firewall setup and configurations to protect and tighten defenses for the perimeter. Drafting and updating security policies and technical documentation. Collaborating with IT and product teams to integrate security into the SDLC and hardware procurement cycles. Monitoring threat intelligence feeds to pivot defense strategies against emerging "zero-day" threats. Providing technical guidance during client workshops to bridge the gap between "technical risk" and "business impact." Expanded Day-to-Day Field Activities While strategy is key, your daily routine will involve high-impact, hands-on field tasks: On-Site Hardware Hardening: Physically visiting client locations to perform "Gold Standard" builds. This includes locking down I/O ports, installing physical port blockers, and verifying that server racks are tamper-evident. Infrastructure Audits & "Walkthroughs": Conducting physical security sweeps. You’ll check for rogue devices (like unauthorized "rubber ducky" USBs or Wi-Fi pineapples) and ensure that critical hardware isn't vulnerable to "evil maid" attacks. Edge Device Deployment: Configuring and installing secure gateways, firewalls, and VPN concentrators directly at branch offices or industrial plants to ensure secure connectivity from the edge to the core. Legacy System Integration: Working with "Air-Gapped" or legacy industrial systems that can't be patched remotely. You will manually apply security updates and hardening scripts via secure, vetted media. Site-Specific Compliance Verification: Validating that the physical environment meets ISO or NIST standards—checking everything from biometric access logs to the redundancy of local power supplies (UPS). Network Mapping & Discovery: Using field tools to sniff out "shadow IT" devices on the local network that might not show up on remote scans, ensuring 100% asset visibility for the client.

title: Research Fellow (Conductors and materials interfaces)
description: The School of Electrical and Electronic Engineering is one of the founding Schools of the Nanyang Technological University. Built on a culture of excellence, the School is renowned for its high academic standards and research.  With over 3,000 undergraduates students and 1,000 graduate students it is one of the largest EEE schools in the world and ranks 10th in the field of Electrical & Electronic Engineering in the 2024 QS World University Rankings by Subjects. Today, the School has become one of the world’s largest engineering schools that nurtures competent engineers and researchers. Each year, the School graduates over a thousand students who are ready to take on great ambitions and challenges. For more details, please view: https://www.ntu.edu.sg/eee At E2ESR Lab www.wrzhanglab.com , we focus on advancing electrochemistry, materials, and engineering as an integrated platform to build technologies for a sustainable and resilient future. We focus on the ion conductor and its interface with other materials. We aim to understand the ion/electron transportation in the conductor, and the chemo-electro-mechanics behavior at these materials interface. By understanding and ultimately controlling these interfaces, we will enable the development of advanced energy systems and electronic devices, including high energy Li/Na batteries, all-solid-state batteries, sensors, switch, electrochemical transistors, with scalable architectures for real-world applications. We are looking for a highly motivated Research Fellow to drive independent research in next-generation electrochemical energy systems; support interdisciplinary collaborations across materials science, electrochemistry, and mechanics; mentor graduate and undergraduate students in the lab. The role will contribute to cutting-edge research in electrochemical energy storage and conversion, with a focus on solid-state batteries, interfacial chemistry, electro-chemo-mechanics, and novel energy materials. Key Responsibilities: Conduct advanced research on solid-state batteries; high energy batteries; materials synthesis; Develop materials for electronic devices like sensors, semiconductors, switch, transistors. Design and perform electrochemical, mechanical, and advanced materials characterizations. Collaborate with internal and external academic/industrial partners on joint projects. Prepare manuscripts for high-impact journal publications and present at international conferences. Contribute to grant proposal writing and new project development. Mentor and supervise PhD, Master’s, and undergraduate students in the laboratory. Job Requirements: PhD degree in Materials Science, Chemistry, Chemical Engineering, Electrical and Electronic Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or related field At least 3 years of relevant experience in a similar role / technical hands-on experience in materials synthesis, electrochemical testing, and advanced characterization techniques. Proven track record of high-quality research publications in high-impact journals. Good written and oral communication skills Ability to work independently and collaboratively in an interdisciplinary team. We regret to inform that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

title: Global Head of Project Procurement
description: Bring more to life. Are you ready to accelerate your potential and make a real difference within life sciences, diagnostics and biotechnology? At Leica Microsystems, one of Danaher’s 15+ operating companies, our work saves lives—and we’re all united by a shared commitment to innovate for tangible impact. You’ll thrive in a culture of belonging where you and your unique viewpoint matter. And by harnessing Danaher’s system of continuous improvement, you help turn ideas into impact – innovating at the speed of life. Shape the Future with Us! At Leica Microsystems, we have been shaping the future for over 175 years with groundbreaking optical and digital solutions.  With a culture rooted in customer focus, innovation, and teamwork, we lead the market in microscopy, imaging, and analysis, unveiling the invisible and empowering our customers to build a better, healthier world. Joining Leica Microsystems means contributing to scientific discoveries and supporting surgeons in making critical decisions. Our advanced microscopes and AI-based image analysis solutions enable users to gain profound insights into development and engineering challenges. Here, you will work on meaningful projects alongside passionate colleagues, driving progress and pushing the boundaries of what’s possible. Learn about the Danaher Business System which makes everything possible. The Global Head of Project Procurement plays a pivotal role in ensuring that procurement activities within projects are executed efficiently, timely, and in alignment with strategic goals. Acting as a strategic interface between R&D, Operations, Product Management, Quality, and Commodity Management, this role ensures that key sourcing deliverables in projects — such as supplier readiness, cost optimization, and timely material availability — are met. This role leads continuous improvement initiatives across global procurement processes to enhance operational excellence and ensure regulatory compliance throughout all procurement activities. This role requires close collaboration and alignment with Danaher Business System (DBS) representatives to ensure that all process enhancements and system developments are fully consistent with DBS principles and strategic direction. This position reports to the Director Global Direct Sourcing and will be an on-site role in Singapore. In this role, you will have the opportunity to: Lead and develop the Project Procurement team, fostering a culture aligned with Danaher core values Ensure all projects are aligned with organizational goals, strategic priorities as well as commodity and supplier strategies, translating high-level business objectives into actionable plans. Enforce project management standards, tools, and methodologies while ensuring compliance with internal processes and external regulations. Oversee the entire project portfolio by monitoring progress, prioritizing based on impact and urgency, and managing resources effectively. Drive cost optimization, maximize value delivery, and provide clear, proactive communication to stakeholders and peers on status, risks, and financial performance. Drive the design, standardization, and continuous improvement of end-to-end procurement processes to ensure operational excellence, regulatory compliance, and efficiency through automation, governance, and data integrity. The essential requirements of the job include Bachelor’s or master’s degree in engineering, Industrial Engineering, Supply Chain Management or related field Over 10 years of expertise in procurement, sourcing, and supply chain within manufacturing environments, including 5+ years in leadership roles Demonstrated success in advanced project management and driving strategic initiatives Proven track record of implementing continuous process improvements to enhance efficiency and reduce costs Fluent in English (negotiation level) Travel requirements: Willingness to travel internationally (up to 20%). It would be a plus if you also possess previous experience in: Industry experience in medical devices or high-precision instruments in a high-mix low-volume (HMLV) manufacturing environment SAP, e-procurement tools, and Microsoft Office Suite and Power BI. Stakeholder management in a matrixed, multinational organization. Whether it’s a health care program or paid time off, our programs contribute to life beyond the job. Check out our benefits at Danaher Benefits Info .

title: Senior Technical Manager
description:
Job Overview: The Senior Technical Manager is responsible for providing technical leadership and operational oversight for the Dry & Wet Process Division , which covers Toxic Industrial Waste (TIW) treatment, metal recovery, recycling (RCY),
and all related operation processing activities. This role requires deep expertise in hazardous waste management, recycling technologies, process optimization, regulatory compliance, and strategic leadership . The incumbent will lead the operations team to drive productivity, efficiency, and sustainability in our operations while fostering continuous improvement and innovation. Additionally, Japanese language proficiency is required to facilitate effective communication with Headquarters in Japan and to ensure smooth reporting and collaboration. Key Responsibilities, 1. Technical & Operational Leadership Oversee the entire TIW-RCY process and ensuring compliance with industry standards and regulatory requirements. Lead the development, implementation, and optimization of waste treatment and recycling technologies. Drive process improvements to enhance operational efficiency, reduce environmental impact, and optimize resource recovery. Evaluate and transfer Japan technologies to local to improve waste treatment methods and
recycling outcomes. 2. Operational Financial Management & Budgeting Develop and manage the operations annual budget, ensuring alignment with the company’s financial objectives. Monitor operating costs and expenses, leading operations team to implementing cost-saving initiatives without compromising operational efficiency. Set and achieve budgeted EBIT targets, ensuring financial sustainability and profitability. Identifying key areas for revenue growth and cost management. Collaborate with the Finance & Administration department to track and report financial performance metrics . 3. Team Management & Leadership Manage and mentor a team of Factory Managers, R&D Engineers and Technical Specialists, fostering a culture of continuous learning and professional development. Participate in key operations meetings, including Daily Toolbox Meetings, Supervisors’ Meetings, Weekly Management & Sales Meetings, Project Meetings, and Safety Meetings and etc. Provide technical guidance and problem-solving support to the team. Promote teamwork and collaboration to achieve operational excellence. 4. Strategic Planning & Business Development Collaborate with top management to develop and implement long-term strategies for waste recycling and sustainability initiatives. Identify and develop new business opportunities, partnerships, and innovative recycling solutions. Support the company’s sustainability goals by implementing circular economy principles in waste management. Optimize costs and operational efficiency while maintaining environmental responsibility and compliance. 5. Safety & Risk Management Collaborate with the EHS department to implement and oversee strict safety protocols, ensuring a safe working environment and compliance with regulatory standards. Support risk assessments and hazard identification to ensure all equipment, processes, and personnel comply with safety, health, and environmental regulations. Work closely with EHS to analyze incidents, lead root cause investigations, and implement corrective and preventive action plans to mitigate future risks. Promote a safety is our first priority culture by reinforcing best practices and continuous improvement in hazardous waste handling and recycling operations 6. Japanese Reporting to Headquarters: Ensure accurate and timely reporting to headquarters in Japan, including financial reports, operational updates, and strategic initiatives. Coordinate with relevant departments to gather data and information for reporting
purposes. Facilitate communication and collaboration between the headquarters and local teams to support business objectives and alignment with global strategies. Qualifications: Bachelor's degree in Engineering (Mechanical, Chemical, or related field). Master degree is a plus. Previous experience in operation and project coordination, construction management, or related roles in the industrial sector. Strong knowledge of Rotary Kiln and Vertical Stocker incineration technologies, WWTP, HF & CaF₂, and engineering principle Strong organizational, communication, and problem-solving skills. Ability to work collaboratively in a multidisciplinary team and adapt to changing project requirements. Benefits: Competitive salary and benefits package. Basic  : S$ 6,300 per month Up to 4 Months Bonus. Opportunities for professional development and career growth. A dynamic and collaborative work environment. Contributing to the success, growth, and technological advancement of a leading company in the industry.

title: Research Fellow (Asphalt Pavement Materials and Modelling)
description:
Interested applicants are invited to apply directly at the NUS
Career Portal Your application will be processed only if you apply
via NUS
Career Portal We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Job Description The National University of Singapore (NUS) Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering invites applications for one (1) Research Fellow position in the area of resilient asphalt pavement materials and modelling with focus on new materials, modelling and design. The candidate is expected to work in research projects related to the topic funded by the National Research Foundation of Singapore and Land Transport Authority of Singapore, and the Defence Science and Technology Agency of Singapore. The candidate will be working with Associate Professor Ong Ghim Ping Raymond from the Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering, College of Design and Engineering (CDE)( https://cde.nus.edu.sg/cee/staff/ong-ghim-ping-raymond/) in fundamental and translational research in the development of new asphalt pavement design guidelines. The selected individual is required to: •    Perform laboratory and field experiments on asphalt pavements functional and environmental performance as core focus. •    Work on mechanistic empirical pavement design and balanced mix design standard development. •    Model pavement behaviours using analytical and numerical methods with the end goal in modification of mechanistic empirical pavement design and balanced mix design to suit local context. •    Develop state of the art solutions for high performance pavement system and work closely with contractors and agencies on field construction implementation to ensure solution is commercially viable. •    Manage research project progress, grant administration, and finance. •    Work on other relevant tasks set out by the project PI. The candidate should also possess good communication and team working skills and has the ability to present research findings in publications and professional meetings (including with agencies and the industry). To apply, please furnish the following documents: •    Cover letter •    Curriculum vitae and transcripts, including a list of publications (provide softcopies of relevant papers) •    Four referee letters (of which one must be from the PhD supervisor) Only shortlisted candidates will be notified Job Requirements •    Possess a PhD in civil engineering or a related area. •    Applicants with relevant experience in both experimental and field asphalt pavement design, standard development and construction would be favourably considered. •    Candidates without experience are welcome to apply. •    Strong written and spoken communications. •    Open to fixed-term contract. •    Only shortlisted candidates will be notified

title: Engineer (Marine and Reclamation Engineering）
description:
Job Summary: We are seeking a skilled and motivated Engineer
with specialized experience in Marine & Reclamation project management and technology. This role is critical in providing technical expertise to support our Market Development and Project Management initiatives. The successful candidate will contribute to winning new business and ensuring the successful technical delivery of complex coastal and reclamation projects. Key Responsibilities (Job Description): 1. Technical Support for Market Development: Ø Assist the business development teams by providing technical input for proposals, tenders, and client presentations related to marine and reclamation projects. Ø Participate in client meetings and site visits to understand project needs and articulate technical solutions. Ø Contribute to feasibility studies, preliminary designs, and cost estimations for new project opportunities. 2. Project Engineering & Delivery Support: Ø Provide engineering design, analysis, and technical oversight for marine structures (e.g., seawalls, breakwaters,
jetties) and land reclamation projects. Ø Prepare and review technical drawings, specifications, calculations, and reports. Ø Support project managers in planning, coordination, and monitoring of project phases (design, procurement, construction, supervision). Ø Conduct site inspections and liaise with contractors to ensure works adhere to design specifications, quality standards, and safety regulations. 3. Design & Analysis: Ø Perform engineering analyses related to coastal processes, hydrodynamics, sediment transport, geotechnical stability, and foundation design for reclamation areas. Ø Utilize engineering software for modeling and design tasks. 4. Documentation & Compliance: Ø Ensure all engineering deliverables comply with relevant national and international codes, standards, and environmental guidelines. Ø Maintain accurate project documentation and assist in the preparation of progress and final reports. 5. Collaboration: Ø Work closely with senior engineers, project managers, and multidisciplinary teams (geotechnical, environmental, planning). Requirements: 1.  Education & Certification: Ø Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering, Coastal Engineering, Ocean Engineering, Geotechnical Engineering, or a directly related field. A Master’s degree is advantageous. Ø Progress towards or holding a relevant professional engineering license/certification (e.g., EIT, PE, or international equivalent) is preferred. 2.  Experience: Ø Minimum of 5 years of direct hands-on experience in the engineering design and/or project delivery of marine and reclamation
projects. Ø Demonstrated involvement in at least one major land reclamation project is highly desirable. Ø Experience in a consulting or contractor environment is preferred. 3.  Technical Skills: Ø Solid understanding of marine and reclamation engineering principles, including dredging, ground improvement, coastal protection, and marine structures. Ø Proficiency with relevant engineering software (e.g., AutoCAD, Civil 3D, GIS tools, hydrodynamic or geotechnical modeling software). Ø Familiarity with project management tools and methodologies. 4.  Soft Skills & Competencies: Ø Strong technical writing and verbal communication skills in English. Ø Excellent analytical and problem-solving abilities. Ø Proactive team player with the ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines. Ø Client-focused attitude and commercial awareness. 5.  Other: Ø Willingness to travel to project sites and client offices as required. Ø Must be eligible to work in the specified location.

Compliance     & Assurance: Implement     secure coding practices, encryption (TLS/mTLS), and auditing mechanisms     aligned with cybersecurity standards.
